title: Field Properties
text:
Learn how to set Field properties to control the look and behavior of the fields you add to documents.

When adding fields to your documents, you can set various properties to:

- Make a field required or optional
- Change the recipient for a field
- Enter data in the field and make it read-only for recipients
- Apply data validation to enforce data entry rules for the recipient
- Set the font, size, or color for a text field
- Hide the recipient's entry in a text field with asterisks for added data security
- Set up conditional field relationships

The properties are similar for all field types, though not all properties apply to all fields. You can adjust the properties simultaneously on a single-field tag basis or multiple fields.

For more information about Field properties, explore these resources:

- [Edit Field Properties](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=kww1578456275056.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Basic Field Properties](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=ugr1578456275295.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Advanced Properties for Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=nas1578456281970.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Add Documents to an Envelope
text:
Read about how to upload documents to review and sign, and how to add documents from your local machine or a cloud storage provider.

Once you start an envelope, you add the documents you want your recipients to review and sign electronically. Docusign supports a wide variety of [file formats](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=xln1578456261162.html&_LANG=enus), covering the popular document, presentation, image, drawing, and spreadsheet applications. When you add your files, Docusign converts them to PDF format for sending and storage.

You can add documents that are stored on your local machine. Depending on your account settings, you may be able to add documents from an authorized cloud storage provider, such as Google Drive or Dropbox. Your Docusign administrator controls which providers you can use. For more information, see "Document Sources" in [Sending Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bjh1583277352580.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Align Fields
text:
Learn how to align two or more fields.

To align two or more fields:

1. Select two or more fields **(Shift+select)** or **(Cmd + select)**
2. Use the alignment tools in the fields properties panel to align the fields as
   desired.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/alignmenttoolbar1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxOTksInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.8AgRNlvxuL6Fd4slVzyzLeZDdhMZUocXANn4xu8Kzyw&_LANG=enus)

title: Sign a Document
text:
Read about how to sign a document from your Docusign account and send
it to anyone who needs a copy.

Sometimes, you just want to sign a document yourself–just you, no other signers. And maybe
you want to send a copy of the signed document to others. This may be a document someone sends
to you outside of Docusign or a form you must complete and send to someone.

With the Sign a Document feature, you can quickly upload a document to your Docusign account, sign it, and send the signed document to anyone who needs a
copy.

You can start a new document and sign it from the Home or
Agreements page.

![Home page with Sign a Document option selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/signadocument.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNDgsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7EpaCtoJiovMhiLXMDBeejN74Ul2isSXZ1yYzpcRxX0&_LANG=enus "Home page with Sign a Document option selected.")

title: Welcome to Docusign
text:
Discover how Docusign eSignature simplifies business processes for over 1.7 million customers worldwide.

Welcome to Docusign eSignature, the leading e-signature solution. Docusign brings agreements to life. Over 1.7 million customers and more than a billion people in over 180 countries use Docusign solutions to accelerate the process of doing business and simplify people's lives.

title: Welcome to Docusign
text:
Want to learn how to use Docusign? Explore these resources and more on the [eSignature landing page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ljy1706645160059&topicId=goo1706645596641.html&_LANG=enus):

- [The Signing Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=pah1578456451991.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Send Document for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=lak1578456412477.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Sign a Document Yourself](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Prepare-and-Sign-a-Document)
- [Create Templates](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/video-playlist-docusign-esignature-templates)

title: Welcome to Docusign
text:
Docusign is committed to your success. Share your thoughts on how to improve our services:

- **Product features feedback:** Send your ideas to [feedback@docusign.com](mailto:feedback@docusign.com?subject=Product%20Feedback%20-%20DocuSign%20eSignature).

Here are some other resources to help you:

- **Contact Support:** Need help with an account issue? Visit our [Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/contactSupport?language=en_US) page for options to reach Customer Support.
- **Docusign Community:** Visit the [Docusign Community](https://support.docusign.com/s/community?language=en_US), connect with other users, and share leading practices.
- **Download a PDF of this guide:** Prefer PDF? Use the PDF download option at the top of this page to download the guide.

title: Optional Fields
text:
Read about optional fields, such as signature or initial fields, and how you can place them for your recipients to consider.

You may want to include optional fields for recipients, such as an optional signature or initial. For example, a recipient signs a permission form only if granting permission or initials items they want in a service contract.

For more information about optional fields, explore these resources:

- [Field Types With a Required Option](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=skq1578456501994.html&_LANG=enus)
- [How To Tell the Difference Between Required and Optional Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=lyi1578456502456.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Make a Field Optional](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=rav1578456503065.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Set Signing Order and View the Order Diagram
heading_path: Set Signing Order and View the Order Diagram
text:
Learn how to set a signing order for your document and view a visual diagram of the
routing.

To set a signing order and view the order diagram:

1. From the Prepare view, add your recipients.
2. Select the Set signing order check box.

   Signing order values display at the left end of the recipient rows. By default, the
   signing order is the order in which you added the recipients.
3. To change the order, enter new values in the routing order boxes.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/changesigningorder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNzYsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.unsyOiln-0-tckN3JZpiLKqreE925ciTCzTSdwWLl5w&_LANG=enus)
4. To send the document to multiple recipients at the same time, set the same signing
   order value for each.
5. Select View to open the signing order diagram and see a visual
   representation of the recipient routing for your document.

   In the signing order diagram, each recipient is represented by their initials,
   following their position in the signing order. Select each recipient circle to see more
   details.
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/signingorderdiagram1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNzYsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.unsyOiln-0-tckN3JZpiLKqreE925ciTCzTSdwWLl5w&_LANG=enus)

title: Add Recipients
text:
Read about how to add recipients, and how to set a signing order to control the order where they receive your document.

Once you select the documents to send, you can add up to 99 recipients who will receive and sign your documents or receive a copy. You provide an email address and name for each recipient. Recipients don't need a Docusign account to open your document and complete their signing action.

Note: When adding recipients, special characters (like `< >`) in a name can prevent successful signature selection. To control how a recipient's name displays and to prevent changes during signing, use the Lock Recipient Name feature. This ensures that the recipient signs with the exact name that you provide. For more information, see [How to Lock Recipient Name option prevents recipients from changing their name when adopting a signature?](https://community.docusign.com/esignature-111/how-to-lock-recipient-name-option-prevents-recipients-from-changing-their-name-when-adopting-a-signature-2061)

title: Add Recipients
heading_path: What actions can you assign to recipients?
text:
You can assign different actions to the recipients of your document, such as signing or receiving a copy.

![The Add recipients card showing a list of recipient actions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/recipienttypes.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNjQsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.PWvZF-FY4JI__2ZZFDqRAjF7TsHMu6wiFOvi2QjDpko&_LANG=enus "The Add recipients card showing a list of recipient actions.")

Note: Some recipient actions are available for select plans only and may not be included in your account.

title: Add Recipients
heading_path: Receive a copy of the completed document
text:
Your account and personal preferences may be set such that you automatically receive a copy of the completed documents by email. If your account administrator supports sending the PDF with the completed email notification, and you [opt to receive the completed email notification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=dhc1578456326388.html&_LANG=enus), you'll receive a copy of your document once all recipients have signed.

If signing the document isn't necessary, you can skip adding yourself as a recipient. You can always view or download the completed documents from your Docusign account or use the copy sent to you with the completed email notification.

title: Related Topics
text:
Read related topics about adding fields to documents.

For more information about topics related to adding fields to documents, explore these resources:

- [Shortcut Keys for Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=lwy1578456273400.html&_LANG=enus): Keyboard commands for common actions on recipient fields.
- [Add Fields Automatically with AutoPlace](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=ytr1578456405118.html&_LANG=enus): Add fields automatically by matching to characters in your document.
- [Adjust Field Size](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=xfc1578456406846.html&_LANG=enus): Adjust the size of a field by selecting and dragging on any field corner.
- [Field Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=vfk1578456408630.html&_LANG=enus): Use the right field type for the information that you want from the recipient.
- [Field Properties](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=alk1578456282186.html&_LANG=enus): Control the appearance and behavior of fields through the available field properties.
- [Limit Changes to Templates and Template Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=gsz1578456308195.html&_LANG=enus): Protect the fields that you add to your template from being changed or deleted.

title: Visibility of Read-Only Fields
text:
Read about the visibility of read-only fields and the factors that affect what recipients can see.

Several factors affect which recipients can see read-only field contents. However, recipients cannot change these values.

- **Account settings to show values to all recipients**. Account administrators can enable a setting that makes the values you enter visible to all recipients before sending an envelope. For more information, see the [Sending Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bjh1583277352580.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
- **Show read-only field values to all recipients**. If the setting to show values isn't enabled, set a signing order and assign read-only fields to the first recipient in the order.
- **Show read-only field values for recipients who have finished signing.** If you don't use a signing order and don't enable the setting to hide values, recipients can see their own field values and those of previous signers.

title: Set a Signing Order
text:
Learn how to set a signing order to control the order where recipients receive and sign
your documents.

If your envelope has more than one recipient, you can choose to set a signing order. The signing
order helps you control the order where your recipients receive and sign your documents.

A convenient signing order diagram helps you visualize how your envelope will be distributed to
the recipients.

![The Add recipients section with the Set signing order checkbox selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/add-recipients-set-a-signing-order.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNzMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.XOAMqWCp6bhBUYUt9a76xZOQWFEe71PQnotTCdv-VoM&_LANG=enus "The Add recipients section with the Set signing order checkbox selected.")

With **Set signing order** enabled, you can specify a recipient routing order. You can set up a simple sequential routing order, where each recipient receives the email notification once the previous recipient has completed their action. You can also have a mix of sequential and parallel routing.

When you use a signing order, you can route an envelope to the same person multiple times. For
example, you want to send a purchase order to your manager to approve, then send it on to
purchasing to sign, and finally send a copy to your manager again.

With **Set signing order** turned off, all recipients receive the document in parallel.

title: Set a Signing Order
text:
If your business sends SOW contracts for client approval, and your client has two approvers
(the project manager and the finance officer), you need the project manager to approve it
first.

After both approvals, you send a copy to your firm's accounting manager. With
Set signing order enabled, you can arrange this sequence so that
each recipient receives and signs the document in order.

title: Log In to Your Docusign Account
heading_path: Log In to Your Docusign Account
text:
Log in to your Docusign account using your email and password, and access troubleshooting resources if you encounter issues.

You can log in to your Docusign account with your email address and
password. If you have issues logging in, see the [login troubleshooting help](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/I-am-having-trouble-logging-in) article.

1. Go to <https://apps.docusign.com/send>.

   Note: To access a demo account, go to <https://apps-d.docusign.com/send>.
2. Enter the email address for your account and select
   NEXT.
3. Enter your account password and select Log in.

   If you don't know your password, you can use the Reset
   password link to reset your password.

title: Edit Field Properties
text:
Learn how to edit field properties and adjust settings.

To edit field properties, access the settings by either of the following methods:

- For a single field, select the field to choose it.

  The field properties panel
  slides in over the page guide on the left, showing adjustable settings.

  ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/signfieldproperties2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMTMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.xUEpLkTNmZ0KoWm9l_QpeR1ny83SnUIFPB4irZzvyh8&_LANG=enus)
- To select multiple fields, drag to encompass them or use **Shift+select** or **Cmd+select** to
  choose each one.

  The field properties panel slides in, applying changes to all selected
  fields.

title: Sending Documents for Signature
text:
Read about how to send documents for signature.

With Docusign, getting signatures is quick and easy. Upload the document, add the signers' contact information, and specify what they must add, like a signature or initials.

Note: If you must sign a document yourself (with no other signers), follow the steps in the [Sign a Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=dyh1578456260672.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Sending Documents for Signature
text: Here's a video on sending documents to get you started:

title: Sending Documents for Signature
text:
The steps for creating and sending a document for signature are as follows:

1. **[Initiate Agreements and Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=gso1578456465211.html&_LANG=enus):** You can start with a new envelope, use a template stored in Docusign, or finish a previously saved draft.
2. **[Add Your Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=crk1578456412036.html&_LANG=enus):** Docusign supports a broad variety of [file formats](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=xln1578456261162.html&_LANG=enus). Select files from your local machine or from cloud storage providers like Google Drive or Dropbox.
3. **[Add Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=jar1578456351432.html&_LANG=enus):** Specify who receives your envelope and what action you want them to take.
4. **[Add Messages](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=mpf1578456350875.html&_LANG=enus):** Enter the subject and message details for your recipients.
5. **[Advanced Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=tfm1578456367923.html&_LANG=enus):** Set special recipient privileges, reminder settings, and expiration options.
6. **[Add Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=odn1578456409392.html&_LANG=enus):** Add fields for each recipient to collect signatures and other information like title and date signed.
7. **[Preview and Send](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=oeg1578456408976.html&_LANG=enus):** Review your field setup and send the envelope to your recipients.

If you include yourself as a signer, after you send the envelope, you can start the signing process from within your account. For more information, see the [Sign a Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=dyh1578456260672.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Sending Documents for Signature
text: For more information about using templates, see the [Working with Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=dqj1578456412286.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Read-Only Fields
text:
Read about how to complete fields, mark them as read-only, and ensure that recipients can view but not change the values.

For some field types, as the sender, you can complete the fields and mark them as read-only for the recipients. The Read-only property locks a field from being changed by any recipient on the envelope.

title: Required and Optional Fields
text:
Read about how to tell the difference between required and optional fields.

Required fields are filled with the recipient's color, as indicated by the color next to
their name in the recipient list.

Here's an example showing a required field on the left and an optional one on the right:

![Required and optional signature fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/reqopt.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMzksInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.hTVX-wAqVmv6IqQ4KzaBJ-3H-Dzl7DkWoZWHEtmndcc&_LANG=enus "Required and optional signature fields.")

title: Add Messages
text:
Read about how to add messages to your document recipients, either as a group message for all or a private message for individual recipients.

Docusign sends signature requests to recipients through email. After adding recipients, you enter the subject and message. The subject becomes the envelope title, helping you identify it on the Agreements page and serving as the notification email subject for recipients.

For more information about adding group or private messages, explore these resources:

- [Add a Message to All Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=ubz1578456491180.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Add Private Messages to Individual Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=voe1578456350694.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Remove a Private Message](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=wab1578456491723.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Add Fields to Documents
text:
Read about how to add fields to documents that instruct signers where to provide their signature, initials, or other information.

Once your envelope includes files, recipients, and messages, you add fields instructing your signers to provide their signature, initials, or other information. You add a unique set of signing fields for each recipient.

You can now perform a single search that scans across all available field libraries simultaneously.

Note: If you cannot add fields to the documents and templates you create, this is most likely because of your user permission profile. Contact your Docusign administrator and ask to adjust your permission settings. These settings are documented in the [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Add Fields to Documents
text:
The Add Fields view has the following basic areas:

1) Recipients
:   Lists all recipients who will receive your documents to sign. The fields you add are color-coded and apply only to the selected recipient. You can add fields for signers. Use the Edit Recipients option to edit details or add/remove recipients. You can also add sender fields or "pre-fill fields" to input data before sending the agreement.

2) Undo/Redo and Copy/Paste
:   Access common field actions with one-click icons. Use standard keyboard controls or right-click for easy field management.

3) Fields
:   **Standard Fields:** Displays the set of primary, out-of-the-box fields that you can add to your document.
:   **Salesforce Fields:** Specialized fields to merge and map data between Docusign and Salesforce records.
:   **Custom Fields:** User-defined fields created to capture specific data not covered by standard options.
:   **Data Verification:** Fields that function as input fields to ensure recipient data matches a specific required format or pattern before submission.

4) Page Guide
:   Displays a scrolling list of all pages in your document, including all added files. Select any page to make it active. You can delete, rotate, add, remove, or reorder pages from the Page Guide. Use Actions > Edit Documents for more options.

5) Field Properties
:   (Not shown) View and edit properties for selected fields, such as formatting, recipient assignment, and tooltips. This panel slides over the Page Guide when fields are selected.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/addfieldsstructure.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxOTAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.FCFLqfu-Sp9aT-Q0T007LorYhL52fbHbtHkk3-wq6Pk&_LANG=enus)

When recipients review your document, they navigate through the fields based on the Auto-Navigation setting for your account. Your Docusign Administrator sets this option using the Signing Settings in Docusign Settings. For more information, see the [Signing Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=lue1583277359885.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

The default setting navigates through all fields on the document. Alternate options include Page Only where recipients scroll manually, and Navigate required fields, which only guides recipients through the required fields.

title: Preview and Send
text:
Read about previewing a document before sending it for signature.

Once you finish adding documents, recipients, messages, and signing fields, you can preview your work and send your envelope.

The top navigation bar presents options to preview and edit the message, recipients, and documents:

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/cancelsavepreview.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNDYsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.2DcZKs2qj1BW2sx27zZVLqDaOIeQsWzqGSuVJixWQbI&_LANG=enus)

The top navigation bar presents the option to send the envelope:

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/backsendnav.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNDYsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.2DcZKs2qj1BW2sx27zZVLqDaOIeQsWzqGSuVJixWQbI&_LANG=enus)

- To save a draft without sending it, select the "x" and Save and Close.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/saveandclose.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNDYsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.2DcZKs2qj1BW2sx27zZVLqDaOIeQsWzqGSuVJixWQbI&_LANG=enus)

- To cancel the envelope preparation and return to your Agreements page, select the "x" and Discard.
- To preview your recipients' experience, select Preview.
- To send your envelope, select Send.

After sending your envelope, recipients receive an email to review and sign. You can start the process directly from your account if you're a signer. For more information, see the [Sign Documents In Your Account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=hbw1578456422642.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Preview and Send
text:
Preview mode offers a simplified view of each recipient's signing experience. You can use this mode to step through each recipient's fields.

- To preview your recipients' experience, select Preview. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/preview1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNDYsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.2DcZKs2qj1BW2sx27zZVLqDaOIeQsWzqGSuVJixWQbI&_LANG=enus)

  1
  :   Select a recipient to preview.

  2
  :   Select the display format.

  3
  :   Close the preview mode.

  4
  :   Required fields have a dark purple border.

  5
  :   Optional fields have a light purple border.
- To check field entries, select the radio buttons and dropdown values or enter text.

  ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/previewradio.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNDYsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.2DcZKs2qj1BW2sx27zZVLqDaOIeQsWzqGSuVJixWQbI&_LANG=enus) ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/previewtext.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNDYsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.2DcZKs2qj1BW2sx27zZVLqDaOIeQsWzqGSuVJixWQbI&_LANG=enus)
- To preview signature and initials placement, select the fields to see an example.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/previewsignature.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNDYsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.2DcZKs2qj1BW2sx27zZVLqDaOIeQsWzqGSuVJixWQbI&_LANG=enus)

title: System Requirements
text:
Read about the minimum system requirements required to use Docusign.

Most computers will work just fine with Docusign. But just to be thorough,
this guide provides the minimum system requirements must enjoy the Docusign
experience.

title: System Requirements
text:
Docusign supports the latest stable release
(except where noted) of the following browsers:

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |
| **Chrome** | **Firefox** | **Safari** | **Microsoft Edge** |

title: System Requirements
text: - 1024 x 768 minimum (for desktops and laptops)

title: System Requirements
text:
- Permit per-session cookies.
- Users accessing the internet behind a Proxy Server must enable HTTP 1.1 settings by
  proxy connection.
- Firewall settings must permit access to the following server:
  https://docucdn-a.akamaihd.net. Docusign uses Akamai as a content
  delivery service to enhance our application's performance.

title: The Signing Experience
text:
Read about the signing experience and how the signer signs and completes your
electronic signature transaction.

You've prepared your envelope and sent it for signature. What is the signing experience
like? How do your documents display to recipients? Where do the messages and fields that
you added show up, and how does the signer complete the electronic signature
process?

title: The Signing Experience
text:
Here's a short video demonstrating the signing experience to get you started:

You can use the [article "How do I sign a Docusign
document"](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-sign-a-DocuSign-document-Basic-Signing?language=en_US) to guide recipients through the signing process.

title: The Signing Experience
text: Let's walk through a typical signing scenario:

title: The Signing Experience
text:
You manage a small apartment building and have a new prospective tenant, Grant
England. You send Grant a rental agreement for signature and a parking memo to
acknowledge. With a waiting list for the unit, you need the agreement completed
within five days. To ensure this, you set a reminder for Grant every day and an
expiration date for the document in five days. You also explain the deadline to
Grant in the document message.

The building's owner, Lissa Burnky, wants copies of all completed rental agreements.
You add Lissa as a copy-only recipient to your document. You use the Sign in Order
feature so that Lissa receives the document only after Grant signs it. You add a
private note to Lissa explaining that Grant passed the credit and reference
checks.

Here are the steps in the signing experience. The screenshots show you in detail what your
recipients see.

1. Your prospective tenant, Grant, receives an email notification from you through Docusign.

   Grant opens the email.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/review-document.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNTAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.Y3F1HumSmoa-lomD7b9obXGNuDiPGjaxhbU4eOeQICo&_LANG=enus)

title: The Signing Experience
text:
en=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNTAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.Y3F1HumSmoa-lomD7b9obXGNuDiPGjaxhbU4eOeQICo&_LANG=enus)

   The notification includes your photo, name, email, and organization
   name. The subject is the subject that you entered in the message.
   Grant decides he's not ready to sign the lease yet.
2. A day later, Grant receives a reminder to sign your document, and now he's ready to
   sign. He selects the Review Document link. Even though
   Grant does not have a Docusign account, he can view and sign
   your document.
3. A window displays, requesting Grant to review and act on the documents sent to him.
   He selects Continue to proceed.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/review-document-modal.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNTAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.Y3F1HumSmoa-lomD7b9obXGNuDiPGjaxhbU4eOeQICo&_LANG=enus)
4. Your document displays with the signing fields.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/lease-signing.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNTAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.Y3F1HumSmoa-lomD7b9obXGNuDiPGjaxhbU4eOeQICo&_LANG=enus)
5. Grant selects the Sign field and confirms a signature name and style in the Adopt
   Your Signature window.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/adopt-signature.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNTAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.Y3F1HumSmoa-lomD7b9obXGNuDiPGjaxhbU4eOeQICo&_LANG=enus)
6. Grant notices that you have already signed the document. He hovers the mouse over
   your signature and reviews your signature ID card.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/sender-info.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNTAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.Y3F1HumSmoa-lomD7b9obXGNuDiPGjaxhbU4eOeQICo&_LANG=enus)
7. Grant finishes all the signing actions and confirms the signing.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/signing-complete.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNTAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.Y3F1HumSmoa-lomD7b9obXGNuDiPGjaxhbU4eOeQICo&_LANG=enus)
8. Grant receives an email notification that the document is complete. The notification
   includes completed, tamper-sealed PDF documents as attachments.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/complete-document.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNTAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.Y3F1HumSmoa-lomD7b9obXGNuDiPGjaxhbU4eOeQICo&_LANG=enus)
9. Lissa Burnky receives an email with the completed document, signed forms attached,
   and your private message displayed.

title: Add Read-Only Fields to a Document
heading_path: Add Read-Only Fields to a Document
text:
Learn how to add Read-only fields to a document.

To add Read-only fields:

1. Prepare your envelope as usual, adding documents, recipients, and messages.
2. In the Add Fields view, select the Text field or other field type, and place it on the active document page.
3. In the properties panel, set the desired value for the new field.

   To determine who can see the value entered, see the [Visibility of Read-Only Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=jwm1578456503575.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
4. In the properties panel, toggle the Read Only property.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/readonly.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMjMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.3dqn8cC4ztWczAWObpMLa0BS7DJ98hDBfAL-mOCk_W4&_LANG=enus)

For recipients, the field is now read-only and cannot be changed. The field and the contents you specified will be visible to all recipients.

title: Make a Field Optional
text:
Learn how to make a field optional.

To make a field optional:

1. Start an envelope as usual, adding documents, recipients, and messages.
2. In the Add Fields view, select one of the field types listed above and place it onto
   the document.
3. In the properties panel, toggle the Required
   property.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/requiredfieldproperty2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyNDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.o7LmpIc1nzWPHdQAfA_-N6j8UjhPmq2Bz1X_6gbDdO0&_LANG=enus)

The field is now optional and signers can choose whether or not
to complete it.

title: Field Types With a Read-Only Option
heading_path: Field Types With a Read-Only Option
text:
Read about the various field types that you can mark as read-only.

The following field types can be set to read only:

- Text
- Company
- Title
- Drop Down
- Checkbox
- Radio Button

Note: Even though you assign Read-only fields to a single recipient, the fields and their contents may be seen by all recipients of the document. Read-only fields cannot be edited by recipients. For more information, see the [Visibility of Read-Only Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=jwm1578456503575.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Field Types With a Required Option
heading_path: Field Types With a Required Option
text:
Read about the various field types that you can mark as required.

The following field types can be set to required:

- Signature
- Initial
- Text
- Company
- Title
- Radio Button
- Drop Down
- Attachment

title: Supported Languages
text:
Docusign supports multiple languages, allowing users to set their preferred display language through account settings or the application menu.

Docusign is available in the following languages:

- English (U.S.)
- Chinese Simplified
- Chinese Traditional
- Dutch
- French
- German
- Italian
- Japanese
- Korean
- Polish
- Portuguese (Brazil)
- Portuguese (Portugal)
- Russian
- Spanish

You can set the display language for your Docusign account through the [My Preferences](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=kde1578456454912.html&_LANG=enus) page or from the selection menu at the bottom of every page in the application.

Administrators can also set the display language for all users through the administration view. For more information, see the [User management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ixb1583277365937.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

For signers, Docusign supports 44 localized languages, as listed on our [Global Business Standard](https://www.docusign.com/products/electronic-signature/learn/global) page. Languages are detected through the signer's browser settings.

title: How to Add Recipients
text:
Learn how to add recipients to act on or receive documents and specify the signing order and other options.

Add the people who will act on or receive a copy of your documents. You can specify a signing order, require additional authentication, and set other options.

Note: When adding recipients, special characters (like `< >`) in a name can prevent successful signature selection. To control how a recipient's name displays and to prevent changes during signing, use the Lock Recipient Name feature. This ensures that the recipient signs with the exact name that you provide. For more information, see [How to Lock Recipient Name option prevents recipients from changing their name when adopting a signature?](https://community.docusign.com/esignature-111/how-to-lock-recipient-name-option-prevents-recipients-from-changing-their-name-when-adopting-a-signature-2061)

1. At the Add recipients section, enter the email address and name of your first recipient.

   ![The Add recipients section where you add recipients.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/add-recipients-how-to-add-recipients.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.A6X8CKyqKLY1pGLrA77Gi60XBg0j87sc-kAWZjk9kgA&_LANG=enus "The Add recipients section where you add recipients.")

   Note: To add a fax recipient, see the [Fax Documents for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=nry1578456314231.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
2. To set the action for the recipient, select one of the following from the dropdown list:

   Document recipients:

   Needs to Sign
   :   Recipients must complete the required fields assigned to them, such as signature, initial, and date.

   In-Person Signer
   :   Recipients join a Docusign account holder who facilitates a signing session. For more information, see the [In-Person Signing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=hak1578456316043.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

   Receives a Copy
   :   Recipients receive a copy with no further action required. For more information, see the guide to [CC a Recipient on an Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=sbq1631221886588.html&_LANG=enus).

   Needs to View
   :   Recipients must open and view the document.

   Specify Recipients
   :   Recipients can complete the name and email address of the role recipients in the same position or later in the signing order.

   Update Recipients
   :   Recipients can edit recipient details for any remaining recipients in the signing order.

   Allow to Edit
   :   Recipients can make recipient and document changes (correct) to an in-process envelope. This recipient must have a valid Docusign account.

   For more information about Manager recipients, see the [Manager Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=jbn1578456307301.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
3. To add additional recipient rows, select Add Recipient.

   Note: The maximum number of recipients for a single envelope is 99.

   Tip: To add yourself as a recipient on a document, select the Name field and select your name and email:

title: How to Add Recipients
text:
lect Add Recipient.

   Note: The maximum number of recipients for a single envelope is 99.

   Tip: To add yourself as a recipient on a document, select the Name field and select your name and email:

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/addme.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.A6X8CKyqKLY1pGLrA77Gi60XBg0j87sc-kAWZjk9kgA&_LANG=enus)
4. (Optional) Specify a signing order to control the order in which your recipients receive and sign your documents. For more information, see the [Set a Signing Order](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=kcb1578456479440.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
5. (Optional) To require a recipient to give additional information to identify themselves during the signing process, add a verification method:
   1. In the recipient's row, select Customize and Add identity verification.

      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/recipientmoremenu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.A6X8CKyqKLY1pGLrA77Gi60XBg0j87sc-kAWZjk9kgA&_LANG=enus)
   2. Select a verification method for the recipient and complete the required fields. For more information, see the [Recipient Identity Verification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=kzp1578456318101.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

      The summary verification details are shown below the recipient information. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/authsummary.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.A6X8CKyqKLY1pGLrA77Gi60XBg0j87sc-kAWZjk9kgA&_LANG=enus)

      Note: For enhanced security, you can add an access code and an identity verification method (phone call, SMS, or Knowledge-based).
6. For Standards-Based Signatures accounts (SBS) only, select the signature type. For more information, see the SBS [Sending Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xcm1643837555908&topicId=ehf1578456571322.html&_LANG=enus) guide and the list of supported [trust service providers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xww1594245808169&topicId=hxl1594246065723.html&_LANG=enus).

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/selectsignaturetype.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNjcsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.A6X8CKyqKLY1pGLrA77Gi60XBg0j87sc-kAWZjk9kgA&_LANG=enus)

title: Add a Message to All Recipients
heading_path: Add a Message to All Recipients
text:
Learn how to add a group message to all recipients.

The message subject line for all recipients is automatically generated from the names of the files you upload, prefixed with "Complete with Docusign:". For example, uploading a file named "NDA Agreement" creates the subject "Complete with Docusign: NDA Agreement".

If you upload multiple files, recipient names are added to the subject line, limited to 100 characters. You can change the subject line to anything you like.

![The Add message section of the envelope Prepare view.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/message.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxODAsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.EjJ912vJGS2_4UO3PqNAMRJUiZZqvv4LJ7cqdn1O26E&_LANG=enus "The Add message section of the envelope Prepare view.")

Note: The option to add a message to all recipients is only available when sending the envelope by email. It is not available when using the SMS (Text) option.

All recipients see the message information. As the sender, you can see the message that you added to a document on the [Details](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=ztv1578456348404.html&_LANG=enus) view.

The message fields have the following character limits:

- **Subject:** 100 characters
- **Message:** 10,000 characters

1. From the Prepare view in the Add message section, edit the default subject line as necessary.

   Important: This entry serves as both your subject and envelope title. A clear subject line indicates the nature of your documents. For repeated documents, use a custom subject line. If you keep the default subject, all envelopes will have the same title, making it hard to identify individual ones.
2. Enter your message in the message field.

Your message is added and sent to all recipients.

title: Basic Field Properties
text:
Read about the basic field properties.

- **Recipient:** Controls which recipient the field applies to. Use this property to change the recipient for a field tag.
- **Required Field:** Specifies if the field is required or optional. Required fields must be completed to finish signing. Only the following fields have this property: signature, initial, company, title, text, radio button, dropdown, and attachment. For more information, see the [Optional Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=fna1578456503209.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
- **Add Text:** For text fields, you can enter the field contents to present to recipients. Apply the Read-Only property if you want to protect your entry. Set a character limit to control how many characters recipients can enter into the field.
- **Read Only:** Controls whether the recipient can edit the field. This applies to text, company, title, dropdown, checkbox, and radio button fields. As the sender, you can complete these fields and mark them as Read Only so recipients can view but not change the values. For more information, see the [Read-Only Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=lek1578456504446.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
- **Formatting:** Defines the font family, size, color, and character styling. Your account administrator defines defaults for these settings, which you can change as needed for your document fields. For more information, see the [Sending Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bjh1583277352580.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
  - Text fields have a formatting option to hide entered text with asterisks, concealing the data. For more information, see the [Hidden Text Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=nph1579822942291.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
  - For signature and initial fields, you can adjust the field size using the scale % property (50–100%).
- **Tooltip:** Shows tips that display when the recipient navigates over the field. This property does not have a character limit. Use [Preview mode](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=oeg1578456408976.html&_LANG=enus) to ensure that your tooltip will display properly for signers.

title: Get to Know Docusign
text:
Explore a personalized dashboard with role-based widgets, a task list, and an overview of agreement activities.

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/basics-newdsoverview.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNDgsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.Fuu_tjwSfFWse90Bwd9PPu66NJUzJ_C3Yoa8SebyhOc&_LANG=enus)

- **Home:** Access a personalized dashboard featuring role-based widgets, a Tasks list, and an updated Overview of agreement activity
- **Tasks:** List up to 5 incomplete tasks in order of the most imminent due date.
- **Overview:** Track Open requests, Waiting for others, Expiring soon, Completed, and Upcoming renewals from the last 30 days.

Note: For users with access to Agreement Desk and Agreement Manager, the overview includes Open Requests and Agreement Manager Upcoming Renewals.

- **Smart Action Bar:** A button bar in the header area displays your three most recently used actions from the Start menu for quicker access.
- **Profile Management:** You can access and update your profile photo and signature by going to Manage Profile in the Profile menu.

title: Use the Contacts List to Add Recipients
heading_path: Use the Contacts List to Add Recipients
text:
Learn how to use the contacts list to add recipients to an envelope.

Your Docusign eSignature account includes a [contacts list](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=twh1578456324503.html&_LANG=enus). You can select recipients from your contacts list to add to an envelope. Your [permission settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus) control the types of contacts you have access to.

Note: If you can create personal contacts, recipients are automatically added to your contacts list when you send an envelope, so you only need to enter their information once.

1. From the Prepare view, select the Contacts icon to select a saved contact to add.

   ![The Add recipients section with the Name field circled.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/add-recipients-contact-list.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNjksInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.JYzdHfLwhcMvEETxPaj81k9N-bK03vzrPq2h8WkkGjo&_LANG=enus "The Add recipients section with the Name field circled.")

   Note: When adding recipients, special characters (like `< >`) in a name can prevent successful signature selection. To control how a recipient's name displays and to prevent changes during signing, use the Lock Recipient Name feature. This ensures that the recipient signs with the exact name that you provide. For more information, see [How to Lock Recipient Name option prevents recipients from changing their name when adopting a signature?](https://community.docusign.com/esignature-111/how-to-lock-recipient-name-option-prevents-recipients-from-changing-their-name-when-adopting-a-signature-2061)
2. Depending on your user permissions and account settings, you can search and select from the following categories of contacts:

   - **Address Book:** Your address book can contain personal contacts, contacts shared with you, and account signing groups.
   - **Directory:** Your directory contains all active users on your account. Docusign Administrators always have directory access. For other users, the feature must be enabled for your account by your Docusign administrator.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/addfromcontacts.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNjksInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.JYzdHfLwhcMvEETxPaj81k9N-bK03vzrPq2h8WkkGjo&_LANG=enus)
3. Select Apply Selected.

   The contact's information is added with the default action Needs to Sign. You can adjust the action for the recipient as needed.
4. To add multiple contacts to your list of recipients at once:

   1. Select Add Recipient and Add From Contacts.![The Add Recipient menu with Add From Contacts selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/addfromcontactsmenuselection.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxNjksInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.JYzdHfLwhcMvEETxPaj81k9N-bK03vzrPq2h8WkkGjo&_LANG=enus "The Add Recipient menu with Add From Contacts selected.")
   2. Select one or more contacts and select Apply Selected to add them as Needs to Sign recipients.
   3. Adjust each added recipient's action and details as needed.

title: Add Fields to a Document
text:
Learn how to add fields to a document for selected recipients to complete.

To add fields to a document:

1. Start an envelope and add documents and recipients.
2. Select **Next: Add Fields**.
3. In the recipient list at the top, select a person to add fields for.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/addfieldsfor.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxOTQsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.CQ4lZNAk-axrNsbPSgPDvra1yQJhZgglAkyKGfOmiSQ&_LANG=enus)

   Note: The fields you add are color-coded and apply only to the selected recipient. You can add fields for signers, but not for those who just need to view or receive a copy.
4. In the page guide on the right, select a page you want to tag with fields. The selected page becomes the active page and displays in the center panel.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/newpageguide.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxOTQsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.CQ4lZNAk-axrNsbPSgPDvra1yQJhZgglAkyKGfOmiSQ&_LANG=enus)

   Tip: To adjust a field's look, assigned recipient, or other characteristics, select the field. The field properties panel will slide in over the page guide. Set the properties as desired. For more information, see the [Field Properties](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=alk1578456282186.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Field Types
text:
Read about the different field types that you can add to a document.

You can add the following standard fields to your documents:

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/fieldtypes2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)

Note: Some advanced features are available only in certain Docusign plans. Your plan might not support all the options discussed here. For details on what's available for your account, contact your account administrator or Customer Support through the [Contact Support page](https://support.docusign.com/s/contactSupport).

title: Field Types
text: If you're sending a purchase agreement for a custom-built car, the document will include the vehicle specs and the customer's contact details. You'll need the customer’s initials on the specs, their signature, and the delivery location. Use the Initial and Signature fields for their initials and signature, and the Text field for the delivery location.

title: Field Types
text: Some fields automatically populate with the signer's data, while others require the signer to enter some information or make a choice. When you add fields to a document, you can [define field properties](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=alk1578456282186.html&_LANG=enus), such as font styling and character limits.

title: Field Types
text:
You can easily [adjust the size of your field](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=xfc1578456406846.html&_LANG=enus). Select and drag on any of the sizing handles at the corners of the field:

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/fieldsizing.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)

title: Field Types
text:
- **Signature:** New users confirm their name and initials, choose a signature style, and apply it to the field. After setup, the signature is applied with a single click. The signing name matches the recipient name that you entered. Default: Required.
- **Initial:** Just like signing, new users first adopt a signature, and their initials are applied to the document. **Default: Required**.
- **Stamp:** The stamp field helps customers add a stamped image for their signature, approval, or organization acknowledgment. Recipients upload, size, and position the stamp image or select a saved stamp. They can resize or replace it before completing the document. **Default: Required**.

  The stamp field must be enabled for your account by your account administrator in the [Sending Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=bjh1583277352580.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
- **Date Signed:** This field is automatically completed with the current date. Your account administrator can [set the date format](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=lue1583277359885.html&_LANG=enus). The format can include a time stamp.
- **Text:** This is a free text field for data. By default, it accepts any characters. You can set a character limit, hide text with asterisks, or fix the field size. Use the Validation option to [validate Text field data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=jgd1578456357365.html&_LANG=enus). To apply validation, place the field on your document, select it, and choose the format from the Validation section in the properties pane.

  Tip: You can verify data with apps. Third-party apps power our new Data Verification fields. They verify the data entered into them in real-time as signers are completing an envelope. Find them in the Custom Fields pane. For more information, see the [Data Verification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qki1698158012210&topicId=omw1740164520691.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

  ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/datavalidationdropdown2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)

title: Field Types
text:
en=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)

  **Default: Required; Validation - None**.
- **Number:** The number field offers an easy way to input numbers with features such as currency symbols, number formatting, minimum and maximum values, and negative value styling.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/numberfield.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)
- **Name:** This name field supports first name, last name, or full name formats. Place the field in your document and choose the format in the properties panel. For the first name, the first word is used. For the last name, the last word is used.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/nameoptions2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)The field auto-fills with the recipient's name as entered. If the recipient changes their name when adopting their signature, the field updates to show the new name. **Default: Full Name**.
- **Company:** This field auto-fills with the recipient's organization name based on their preferences. If the recipient has no Docusign account or hasn't specified an organization name, the field is blank and can be completed manually.
- **Title:** Like the Company field, the Title field automatically populates with the recipient's title based on their Contact Information. Otherwise, the recipient can type in their title in Manage Profile > My Profile > Contact Information.
- **Email:** This field automatically populates with the recipient's email address.
- **Checkbox:** This is a standard, square box for recipients to select one or more options. You can add a single box or a group of multiple boxes. A checkbox group can include a validation rule to enforce how many checkboxes in the group your signer must select. Checkboxes can be required or optional, but the field becomes required if you apply validation that requires the signer to select a certain number of boxes. For more information, see the [Checkbox Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=srj1578456517556.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

  Tip: If you have a scenario where your recipient must mark to indicate explicit agreement, use a single checkbox and select the **Required Field** option.
- **Radio:** Radio buttons help recipients to select only one option from a group. You can add, remove, or reposition individual buttons. Initially, a Radio field has two options. Use the "+" icon to add more.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/buttonvalue2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)

  Radio buttons have these properties:

  - **Group Label:** Identifies the option group (not shown to recipients).
  - **Radio Button Values:** Displayed to recipients. The values are unique and representative. Only the selected option displays in the completed PDF, displaying as an "x". **Default: "Radio1" "Radio2"**. Values for each option are shown to recipients, and the selected value is included when you [download form data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=fsi1578456281795.html&_LANG=enus) for the document.
  - **Group Tooltip:** Optional text is shown when recipients hover over or select an option.

title: Field Types
text:
.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=fsi1578456281795.html&_LANG=enus) for the document.
  - **Group Tooltip:** Optional text is shown when recipients hover over or select an option.

  Radio buttons can be required or optional. If set as required, recipients must select an option to complete signing. **Default: Required**.

  If you have a scenario where your recipient must mark to indicate explicit agreement, use a single [checkbox](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=srj1578456517556.html&_LANG=enus) with validation.

  Tip: Recipients navigate through fields based on your account's Auto-Navigation setting. For a smoother signing experience with radio buttons, use the option to navigate blank required fields. This setting is configured by your Docusign administrator. For more information, see the [Signing Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=lue1583277359885.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
- **Dropdown:** This field offers a dropdown list where recipients can select only one value. The Options section opens on the properties panel when you place the field.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/dropdown2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)

  Create dropdown options by choosing List or Series:

title: Field Types
text:
6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)

  Create dropdown options by choosing List or Series:

  - List: Add options using + Add Option and complete the values. Avoid using semicolons as they split entries.
  - Series: Enter a semicolon-separated list of values directly.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/dropdownseries.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQyMDMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.7b_Dq8UMBNTnwyVbCgyNya4XFyMayNi2TJSB0VavJbU&_LANG=enus)The Default option list helps you choose a default value and set additional properties such as formatting and requirement status. The dropdown field can be required or optional. If required, recipients must select an option to complete the document. **Default: Required**.
- **Attachment:** The Attachment field helps you request additional documentation, which recipients can upload or fax. If set as required, they must attach a document to complete signing. The attachment will be included in the final document and can be downloaded or printed. **Default: Required**.
- **Note:** The Note field is a free-text field that you can use to enter additional information for your recipients. You can add a note for an individual recipient, and the text that you enter displays only during the signing session. Notes are visible only to their assigned recipient. Notes do not remain on the completed documents. However, you can use the [download form data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=fsi1578456281795.html&_LANG=enus) option to recall and view the information.
- **Approve:** The Approve field provides an alternative to Signature or Initial fields for document approval and is always required. Selecting Approve signifies the recipient has signed. It doesn’t add information to the document, but records an "Approved" action in the Document history. To decline the document, use the Decline field or the **Decline to Sign** option. You can adjust the field's name using the Button Text property.
- **Decline:** The Decline field functions such as Decline to Sign. If selected, the remaining fields are skipped, and the recipient must provide a reason for declining. The action and reason are recorded in the Document history. Adjust the field's name using the Button Text property.
- **Formula:** This calculated field computes results based on recipient-entered values using [formulas](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=crs1578456361259.html&_LANG=enus) with addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. It supports formulas based on numbers or dates.
- **Envelope ID:** The Envelope ID field helps you manually place and format the ID on your document, which is useful for History, Certificates, and API developers. Alternatively, use the [Advanced Option](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=tfm1578456367923.html&_LANG=enus) to auto-stamp the ID at the top left of every page. Once added, the ID stamp is permanent and cannot be removed.

title: Add Private Messages to Individual Recipients
heading_path: Add Private Messages to Individual Recipients
text:
Learn how to add a private message to individual recipients.

You can add private messages to send to individual recipients. Only the selected recipient sees these messages, along with the general message. The private message displays in the email notification under Notes and is visible when the recipient views and signs the documents.

The private message field is limited to 1,000 characters and does not support HREF links, only plain text.

As the sender, you can see a private message that you added to an envelope on the [Details](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=otr1578456349846.html&_LANG=enus) view.

To add a private message for one recipient:

1. In the recipient's row, select Customize and Add private message.

   ![Add recipient card with Customize menu expanded and Add private message option highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/addprivatemessage.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxODMsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.FbUGqW3XROlxIfblB6mUvPX88SIs_MoWI9jTTXfnpco&_LANG=enus "Add recipient card with Customize menu expanded and Add private message option highlighted.")

   Note: The option to add a private message is only available when sending the envelope by email. It is not available when using the SMS (Text) option.
2. Enter your message.

   The private message field is limited to 1,000 characters.
3. Collapse the panel.

Your private message is added and sent to individual recipients.

title: Remove a Private Message
text:
Learn how to remove a private message added for individual recipients.

You can remove a private message from a draft envelope or when [correcting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=dxr1578456334187.html&_LANG=enus) an in-process envelope.

1. In the recipient's row, select the private message panel to expand it.
2. In the private message panel, select the trash can icon.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/ulp1643236876813/page/images/discardprivate-message.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQxODUsInNoZWFmIjoidWxwMTY0MzIzNjg3NjgxMyJ9.htqqFxWkyewBqPgDRhelxgQ02XZkeRV1x2rovx0aNn8&_LANG=enus)

The private message is removed.

title: Add a Document to an Envelope
heading_path: Add a Document to an Envelope
text:
Learn how to add documents to envelopes.

File names are used to generate the default envelope name and to identify documents that are downloaded using the separate PDF option.

To add a document to an envelope:

1. Start a new envelope from Start > Envelopes > Send an Envelope.
2. From the Prepare view, select Upload to add your files.
3. To add files from one of your [authorized cloud storage providers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=qqb1578456263434.html&_LANG=enus), select the provider. Next, select your files and select Add Selected.

   Note: Your account administrator controls the cloud storage providers that you can select from. For more information, see the [Document Sources](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=ebi1583277352404.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: eSignature Basic Terminology
text:
Read about the basic Docusign eSignature terminology.

Here are some common terms that you will regularly see and use as a Docusign eSignature administrator or user:

**Account**

An account is a container for a Docusign customer (organization or individual) that holds envelopes, user memberships, templates, and other data. It provides access to services based on the subscription plan and its features, and is uniquely identified by an Account ID (GUID). A customer can have multiple accounts if needed.

**Account Administrator**

An Account Administrator configures and manages the account, serving as the technical point of contact. They handle policies like passwords and document retention, as well as user memberships, permissions, and envelopes. See the [Introduction to Account Administration](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=vkq1583277322201.html&_LANG=enus) guide for more information.

**Developer Account**

Developer accounts, or sandbox accounts, enable you to test features and APIs for free and create an Integrator Key for going to Production. They operate in the Docusign Demo environment, which is similar to the Production environment, but documents are not legally binding and expire after 30 days. Sandbox accounts do not expire and include enterprise features for thorough testing. Sign up for a free [developer Sandbox account](https://go.docusign.com/o/sandbox/?postActivateUrl=https://developers.docusign.com).

**Document**

A document is a digital file that is delivered, reviewed, and signed by one or more recipients. Documents are encrypted in the system and can be uploaded from user devices, cloud storage, or other sources. Docusign accepts most file types and stores them in PDF format. Multiple documents can be included in one envelope. See the [Add a Document to an Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=wpl1578456263594.html&_LANG=enus) guide for more information.

**Envelope**

An envelope is a package used to send documents and manage transactions. It holds documents, recipients, fields, and includes sender information, metadata, and timestamps. Envelopes have statuses (sent, delivered, completed, voided), and Docusign generates a Certificate of Completion with the full audit history.

**Field**

Fields are placed on documents in an envelope and assigned to specific recipients during signing. They include text boxes, radio buttons, checkboxes, and signature fields. These fields indicate where signatures are required, display information (like dates and organization names), or enable recipients to enter data. See the [Add Fields to Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=jar1578456351432.html&_LANG=enus) guide for more information.

**Recipient**

Recipients of an envelope must complete fields placed in the document by the sender, including signature fields. Generally, they do not need a Docusign account to sign or participate, but certain recipient types or account settings may require login or additional authentication. See the [Add Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=jar1578456351432.html&_LANG=enus) guide for more information.

**Sender**

Senders are account members who create, send, and manage envelopes. They upload documents, add recipients, define routing orders, and place fields for recipients, among other tasks. See the [Sending Documents for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=lak1578456412477.html&_LANG=enus) guide for more information.

**Template**

title: eSignature Basic Terminology
text:
ks. See the [Sending Documents for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=lak1578456412477.html&_LANG=enus) guide for more information.

**Template**

Templates are a pre-set envelope containing documents, recipient roles, fields, and other business logic, serving as a reusable blueprint for Docusign workflows. Templates can range from simple one-signer, one-document setups to complex transactions with multiple recipients and documents. You can also apply multiple templates to the same envelope. See the [Working With Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=dqj1578456412286.html&_LANG=enus) guide for more information.

title: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
heading_path: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
text:
Read about how template creators can use merge fields to customize the envelopes
created from their templates. Inserting recipient name or email address, or envelope custom
fields into the message subject creates customized titles for each envelope sent from a
template.

When you use a template to send envelopes, if you use the message subject as is, the
envelopes will all have the same subject, making it hard to identify a particular one. This
can be especially problematic if you are using the bulk send feature to send the same
documents to many different people at once.

To customize the message subject for each envelope sent from a template, use merge fields to
automatically insert custom data. The template's subject becomes both the
notification message subject and the name of the envelope as shown in the sender's and
recipients' accounts.

title: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
heading_path: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
text:
Any template creator can add merge fields to their own templates or when editing any
templates that are shared with them. You must have the appropriate permissions to create
templates in order to customize the template message subject.

title: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
