heading_path: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
What data can go into a merge field
text:
You can customize a template message subject with one or more of any of the following
template data:

- Recipient name - any role recipient's full name
- Recipient email address - any role recipient's email address
- Envelope custom field (ECF) - any ECF defined on your account and visible to select on
  your template

title: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
heading_path: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
What happens if the message subject exceeds the character limit
text:
The message subject is limited to 100 characters. You can insert multiple merge fields into
the subject, up to the 100-character limit. If an envelope generated from a template has a
subject longer than the limit, anything beyond the limit is truncated.

title: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
heading_path: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
text:
If your template uses the option to  [send custom email messages
for each recipient](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=wxf1578456494201.html&_LANG=enus), you can specify different merge fields for each. However, the envelope name will take on the merge fields applied to the first recipient; notifications to each recipient will use the customized merge fields you set for each role.

title: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
heading_path: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
Adding merge fields to the message body
text:
The message subject field for a template offers the drop-down control to insert recipient
info and ECFs. It is also possible to add merge fields to the message body, but only
through manual entry. Simply type the merge field using the same format as when the field is
inserted in the subject. Examples: [[ECF:ECF field name]], [[Role\_UserName]], [[Role\_Email]]

title: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
heading_path: Use Merge Fields in the Template Message Subject to Customize Envelopes
text:
If your template has a PowerForm, any recipient merge fields defined on the template are
included in PowerForm responses. However, PowerForms do not support ECF merge fields.

title: Check and Set Matching Behavior on a Template
heading_path: Check and Set Matching Behavior on a Template
text:
Template creators and account administrators can set template matching behavior on
templates. Template matching compares uploaded documents with template documents. Matches can
speed up envelope preparation by importing the recipients, messages, and recipient fields from
the template.

Template matching compares uploaded documents with
template documents. Matches can speed up envelope preparation by importing the recipients,
messages, and recipient fields from the template. As a template creator or an account
administrator, you can select which templates to include in matching.

Note: The ability to review and set the template matching behavior on an
individual template is limited to certain account plans. Your plan may not support the task
described in this topic.

1. Navigate to the Templates page.
2. Locate the template that you want to check by searching, filtering, or simply scanning
   the Templates list.
3. The current matching state is shown in the Templates list.

   ![My Templates list with three templates highlighted and showing the matching setting for each one.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatelistmatching.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NjYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.AvR7ouuZZW5uuTzDMTK4_YjlnZegqAiyPrkV3huvjKc&_LANG=enus "My Templates list with three templates highlighted and showing the matching setting for each one.")
4. Set matching behavior from the Templates list:
   1. Select one or more templates by clicking the check box next to the template
      name.
   2. Click the more actions menu and select the matching behavior
      from the list.

   Include in Matching
   :   Template remains in the matching pool until you exclude or delete it.

   Exclude from Matching
   :   Template is removed from the matching pool until you include it again.
5. You can check matching from the template details view. The current
   matching behavior is listed below the template title. You can change the current setting
   from the MORE menu.

   ![Template Details view with MORE menu expanded and Include in Matching option selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatematchstate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NjYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.AvR7ouuZZW5uuTzDMTK4_YjlnZegqAiyPrkV3huvjKc&_LANG=enus "Template Details view with MORE menu expanded and Include in Matching option selected.")

title: Folders for Shared Templates
text:
Read about how shared templates are organized. Review descriptions of the different
categories to understand what each contains.

Provided you have the necessary user permissions to use templates, your Templates page includes a set of folders for shared templates:

![Templates list with the Shared with Me collection selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/sharedtemplates.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.Khmb1CGskBMLoM4JBsZF93Nkq8QPRontotujbEDtga0&_LANG=enus "Templates list with the Shared with Me collection selected.")

Shared templates are organized into the following categories:

Shared Folders
:   Contains any shared template folders that others created and shared with you. Included
    are templates shared with you directly and with groups to which you belong. When someone
    adds a template to a shared template folder, it is shared automatically with anyone with
    access. Shared Folders also contains any folders that you create to share with others on
    your account. See this article for more information: [Share Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=lrh1578456401532.html&_LANG=enus "Sharing a template allows other users and user groups on your account to access and use it to send documents.").

Shared with Me
:   A collection of all templates that are shared with you, either directly or through a
    shared folder.

All Templates
:   A collection of all templates you can access. This collection includes your own
    templates and those shared with you. If you are an account administrator, this collection also includes all
    templates on the account that you do not own.

title: Welcome to Web Forms
text:
Read the introduction about Docusign's Web Forms product.

Recipients complete forms for all facets of business. However, creating these forms can be
tedious and prone to human error. It may not be an overall positive recipient
experience. You want to create forms that provide a modern form-filling experience and
generate completed PDFs for signing. Welcome to Web Forms.

A web form is a digital form you build from an eSignature template. Recipients can complete the
web form, and the result is a completed agreement that collects a signature. Instead of
entering data in PDFs, recipients can provide their input in a user-friendly online
form.

Benefits of using Web Forms

Using a web form simplifies and enhances the data collection experience for your organization
in these ways:

- Build and edit interactive web forms with a no-code interface, minimizing information
  technology (IT) costs.
- Enable recipients to easily complete web forms on mobile devices.
- Enable basic form editing, such as adding or configuring fields.
- Add conditional logic to show or hide fields based on recipient input.
- Pre-populate a PDF agreement with data collected from the web form and sends it for
  signature.
- Add scalibility and more sophisticated use cases using Advanced Web Forms.

title: Delete a Template You Own
text:
Learn how to delete a template that you own.

You can delete any template you own. Deleted templates are moved to your Deleted bin for a short time, after which they are removed permanently from the Docusign system and cannot be restored.

1. Navigate to the Templates page.
2. Select the My Templates collection and locate the template you
   want to edit by searching, filtering, or simply scanning the Template list.
3. Click the template's more actions menu and select
   Delete.

   ![Template actions menu with Delete selected'](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/deletetemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NTEsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.h9o42__XYsc5QgsQQKQouNX64QgW4nclsyfB00AwZi4&_LANG=enus "Template actions menu with Delete selected'")
4. Confirm your action by clicking DELETE in the confirmation modal.
5. The template moves to your Deleted bin. It will remain there for up to 24 hours after
   which it is removed permanently from the Docusign system and cannot be recovered.

title: Download a Document From a Template
heading_path: Download a Document From a Template
text:
Learn how to download a document from a template.

You can download PDF files of the documents in a template.

1. Navigate to the TEMPLATES page.
2. Locate the template that you want to download from by searching, filtering, or simply
   scanning the Templates list.
3. Click the template title to select it and open the Template Details view.
4. In the documents panel on the right, click the document thumbnail image to initiate the
   download. You may need to first expand the document section to display the page
   thumbnails.

   ![The documents panel on the right where you click the document thumbnail image to initiate the download.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/selectdocumenttodownload.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MjAsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.jdTQrh6U7AhvXTKzFAqZo54j-KXXnZBfsc_AFep6MHw&_LANG=enus "The documents panel on the right where you click the document thumbnail image to initiate the download.")

   Your browser settings determine if the file opens in a browser window or downloads
   automatically.
5. If your document opens in a browser window, use the browser controls at the top of the
   window. You can download or print your document PDF.

title: Locked items on a template
text:
When you use a template, you might find that some aspects of the template are locked and cannot be edited. These areas include recipient information, the email message to all recipients and private messages to individual recipients, the brand, and individual fields. Fields or controls which are restricted in this way, will appear grayed out and you cannot change or use them.

The template owner (whom you can identify in the [Locate a Template ID](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=yww1578456609051.html&_LANG=enus "The unique Template ID is located in the URL string for the template and is visible from the Template details view.")
details) controls these settings, and can restrict modifications to various template settings,
recipient information, and individual fields in order to ensure the template is used as
designed.

title: Search for a Shared Template
text:
You can use the search box and filter by date to locate a shared template. These controls work on
the selected folder only.

![Screenshot of the Shared with Me view of Templates with the search controls highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/searchtemplates.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MzksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.-rhWIUGSUKvjtXBfXJeATaJbgLtJS0FNOPnDVL8YjJo&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Shared with Me view of Templates with the search controls highlighted.")

- **To search within a specific folder**, click on the folder to select it, and then enter your search or filter criteria. Folder search is strictly limited to the folder; it does not include results from any sub-folders present.
- **To search all templates you have access to**, click on the **All Templates** collection and enter your search or filter criteria.

title: Working With Templates
text:
Templates help streamline the sending process when you frequently send the same or similar documents, or send documents to the same group of people.

Looking for a way to save even more time with Docusign? Try creating a template. Templates are perfect for almost any Docusign workflow that you do over and over again. Using the documents and recipient information in your template makes the sending process a snap.

So what are some ways to use templates?

- **Same document, different recipients**: You have a disclosure form that you send to every potential client.
- **Same recipients, different document**: Every month, you send a report to your Board of Directors for their approval.

Note: Your access to templates is controlled by your user permissions, as defined by your Docusign administrator. If you cannot create or edit, or even use templates, check with your administrator.

title: Working With Templates
text: Here's a short video on templates to get you started:

title: Working With Templates
text:
This guide is part of a collection of user guides
for Docusign eSignature. The following table provides information about some of the other
user documentation available.

| Title | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [eSignature Basics for Senders](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ulp1643236876813&topicId=ezn1578456259592.html&_LANG=enus) | Introductory information for new users. Covers how to get started with Docusign eSignature and learn the basics. |
| [Account Preferences and Profile Settings for Docusign eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=kde1578456454912.html&_LANG=enus) | Information on how to manage your account preferences, including contacts list, notification preferences, managing delegated signers, and more. |
| [Docusign eSignature for Senders - Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=lxv1687564231272.html&_LANG=enus) | Information on adding recipients to your envelopes. Includes advanced recipient techniques, such as setting a signing order, assigning access permissions, and adding optional recipients. |
| [eSignature for Senders - Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=xln1578456261162.html&_LANG=enus) | Expanded information on preparing documents for signature. Includes details on the different types of recipient and pre-fill fields you can add. |
| [Document Templates for Docusign Agreement Preparation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map332c3ebc-8fca-4fac-9b9f-c7206adc3985&topicId=conc8f06624-6ef6-40a3-bbc8-c5c7fe18746b.html) | Information on creating reusable documents to use in multiple envelope templates. |
| [Document Generation for eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=als1679428547895&topicId=ldx1679428689631.html&_LANG=enus) | Information on using document generation to automatically generate customized documents. |
| [Docusign eSignature - Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=dqj1578456412286.html&_LANG=enus) | Information on creating, using, and managing templates. |
| [Docusign eSignature - PowerForms](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jbx1643062255110&topicId=eke1578456649038.html&_LANG=enus) | Information on creating, using, and managing PowerForms. PowerForms is a no-code solution for a signer to generate an agreement on demand. |

| [Docusign eSignature Reports](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=cnu1642812012214&topicId=wou1578456440763.html&_LANG=enus) | Information for senders and administrators on how to use reports to get insights into their Docusign account. |
| [Docusign eSignature Signing Process](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lou1573854977109.html&_LANG=enus) | Information for signers on how to sign documents with Docusign eSignature. |

title: Cancel Changes When Editing a Template
heading_path: Cancel Changes When Editing a Template
text:
Learn how to discard changes when editing a template.

When editing a template, you can discard the changes and leave the template in its original
form.

1. At any point while editing your template, click the X in the
   top
   menu bar.
2. Select DISCARD CHANGES to confirm your action.

   Your changes are discarded and your template is restored to its original state.

title: Deleting Templates for Administrators
text:
Learn how administrators can delete templates.

If you are an administrator, you have access to all templates created on your account and you can delete any of them.

1. Navigate to the Templates page.
2. Locate the template you want to edit by searching, filtering, or simply scanning the
   Template list.
3. Select one or more templates to delete by marking the checkboxes.
4. Click the more actions menu and select
   Delete.

   ![Screenshot of the All Templates view with the more actions menu, extended to show the Delete option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/deletetemplatesadmin.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NTUsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.J4z65BwGbEqQEfCG3km1fjjCg22kNvx5Aya_fsVa5FU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the All Templates view with the more actions menu, extended to show the Delete option.")
5. Confirm your action by clicking DELETE in the confirmation
   modal.

   The templates are moved to your Deleted bin. Deleted items remain in place for up to
   24 hours after which they are removed permanently from the Docusign system and cannot be
   recovered.

title: Create, Edit, and Delete Folders
text:
Learn how to create, edit, and delete template folders.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. To create a folder:
   1. In the left-hand navigation pane, select Show More to reveal additional elements.
   2. Select the + icon next to
      FOLDERS.

      ![Screenshot of the menu control to add a new template folder.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatefolderadd.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3OTYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.c5mfE_F9a2MhYrI1fYKfp7ehuOOSZOYxNQpgr_NJxTw&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the menu control to add a new template folder.")
   3. Enter a folder name and select Create.

   Your new folder is added to the list of folders.
3. To rename, move, or delete an existing folder, click the folder's 3-dot Action menu and make your selection.

   ![Screenshot of the Action menu for a folder.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/folder_action_menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3OTYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.c5mfE_F9a2MhYrI1fYKfp7ehuOOSZOYxNQpgr_NJxTw&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Action menu for a folder.")
4. To create a subfolder, click the folder's 3-dot Action menu and select
   New Subfolder.

   The new subfolder is nested under the selected folder.

title: Example Searches
text:
Review some examples of how you can use the template search to locate templates in your
account.

Here are a few examples of how you can use the template search to locate templates in your account.

title: Example Searches
heading_path: Search for one of your templates by template name
text:
Select the My Templates folder, enter "terms" in the search field, and
select the Template Name field filter.

The results include all of your templates with "terms" in the name. If the template is
located in one of your personal or shared folders, the folder name is listed in the
Folders column. If the Folders column is blank and you are not the
template owner, then the template is one that was shared with you directly and is in your
Shared with Me folder.

![Example search results by template name.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatesearchmybynameexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NzgsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.9GkjClf4GgG8UdpnVXFSg-QoBhGSq3Vs8KlCJgXu4K8&_LANG=enus "Example search results by template name.")

title: Example Searches
heading_path: Search for a template that someone shared with you
text:
Select the Shared with Me folder or any shared template folder. Select the
Owner Name and Email field and enter any portion of the owner's
name.

![Example template search by owner name and email.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatesearchbyownerexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NzgsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.9GkjClf4GgG8UdpnVXFSg-QoBhGSq3Vs8KlCJgXu4K8&_LANG=enus "Example template search by owner name and email.")

title: Example Searches
heading_path: Search for one of your templates by recipient email
text:
Find all templates that you own that have a recipient with a specific email address. Select your
My Templates folder and enter "records@dsxtr.com" in the search
field and select the Recipient Name and Email field to search:

![Example template search by recipient name and email.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/searchtemplatesbyemailexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NzgsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.9GkjClf4GgG8UdpnVXFSg-QoBhGSq3Vs8KlCJgXu4K8&_LANG=enus "Example template search by recipient name and email.")

title: Example Searches
heading_path: Search all templates by template name
text:
Select the All Templates folder and enter "conference" in the search
field.

The results list all of the templates you have access to that include "conference" in the
template name. If the template is in a personal or shared folder, the folder name is listed
in the Folders column. If the Folders column is blank and you are not
the template owner, then the template is one that was shared with you directly and is in
your Shared with Me folder.

![Example search of all templates by template name.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatesearchbynameexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NzgsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.9GkjClf4GgG8UdpnVXFSg-QoBhGSq3Vs8KlCJgXu4K8&_LANG=enus "Example search of all templates by template name.")

title: Example Searches
heading_path: Search all templates by template ID
text:
Locate a specific template by the template ID. Select the All
Templates folder, enter the complete template ID in the search field, and
select the Template ID field.

If the ID matches a template you have access to, it is listed in the results. If the
template is located in one of your personal or shared folders, the folder name is listed in
the Folders column. If the Folders column is blank and you are not
the template owner, then the template is one that was shared with you directly and is in
your Shared with Me folder.

![Example search by template ID.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templateidsearchexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NzgsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.9GkjClf4GgG8UdpnVXFSg-QoBhGSq3Vs8KlCJgXu4K8&_LANG=enus "Example search by template ID.")

title: Remove a Template From a Shared Template Folder
heading_path: Remove a Template From a Shared Template Folder
text:
Anyone with template *share* permission can remove a template from any of their shared template folders.

1. Navigate to the Templates page and select the All Templates
   view.
2. Locate the template you wish to remove from sharing by searching for some portion of
   the template name.

   ![Folder tags indicating which folder a template is in](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/searchalltemplates.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MDMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.uBQDfMjs9ow9CCntfSB14cFU7SbXkuAMulKr3DiXFqc&_LANG=enus "Folder tags indicating which folder a template is in")

   If you know which shared folder the template is in, you can just navigate there
   directly and skip to Step 4.
3. The FOLDERS column shows which folders the template is located in.
   Click the shared folder label to view the folder's contents. Example: From the above
   screenshot, click the "HR" label to open the HR shared template folder.

   ![Templates in a shared template folder](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/sharedtemplatefolder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MDMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.uBQDfMjs9ow9CCntfSB14cFU7SbXkuAMulKr3DiXFqc&_LANG=enus "Templates in a shared template folder")
4. Select the check box for the templates you want to remove from the shared folder, and
   then click MORE and select Remove from
   Folder.

   ![Remove option to remove a template from a shared folder](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/removetemplatefromshared-folder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MDMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.uBQDfMjs9ow9CCntfSB14cFU7SbXkuAMulKr3DiXFqc&_LANG=enus "Remove option to remove a template from a shared folder")

   The template is removed and no longer shared with the users who have access to the
   shared folder.

title: Related topics
text:
- [Video: Set Template Matching Behavior](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Set-Template-Matching-Behaviors-DocuSign-eSignature-Video?language=en_US). Watch a short video on how to set up and use template matching.
- [Set Template Matching Account Preferences](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=jux1643235969954&topicId=fxo1578456612662.html&bundleId=jux1643235969954&_LANG=enus). Information on how to enable template matching for your account.
- [Apply Templates to Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=aoz1578456267317.html&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&_LANG=enus). In addition to automatic template matching, you can manually select a template to apply to a document.

title: Locate Templates
text:
Read how to locate templates that you own or are shared with you.

The Templates page gives you access to all the templates that you own or are shared with you.
There are different ways to locate templates, including search, filters, and browsing through
template folders.
The
topics in this
section
explain how
to use these methods to locate a template.

title: Specify Sender Permission Settings for a Field
heading_path: Specify Sender Permission Settings for a Field
text:
1. From the template add fields view (either on a new template that you create or when
   editing an existing template), place the field onto the document.
2. In the properties panel on the right, expand the Sender Permissions
    section:

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatefieldlocks.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MTMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.Kblq0XLzKqsPLrXDHDmvG4kBOF0QvJ7NmDixAMwrIyo&_LANG=enus)
3. Select the permission settings that you want to enforce when senders use the template:

   - **Restrict changes** - sender cannot change any field properties or delete the
     field
   - **Mandatory** - sender cannot delete the field
4. Save and close the template.

title: Protect Your Shared Templates
text:
Read about setting passwords to protect shared templates.

When you share a template, you grant the selected users or groups the right to edit the
template, share it with others, and copy it, provided the users have the necessary permissions
(template create or share). To protect a shared template, you can set a password or lock parts
of the template.

- Set a password. You can protect your shared template from
  being modified by other users by  [setting a
  password](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=xwo1578456395432.html&_LANG=enus "Template passwords protect against editing. If the template is shared with other users, for them to edit the shared template, they must provide the password."). Then, if the template is shared with other users, they must provide the
  password to edit, share, or copy the shared template.

  Administrators on your account have full access to all templates created within the account. They can edit templates and modify
  sharing settings, regardless of the password setting.
- Lock template elements. You can also apply settings to
  [limit the types of changes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=gsz1578456308195.html&_LANG=enus "Use template and recipient modification settings to limit the types of changes that can be made when your templates are used to send a document. Use the Sender Permission settings to protect fields on a template from being changed or deleted.") that other users can
  make when using the template to send an envelope. You can control how others use the
  template by locking the recipient information, the email message to all recipients, private
  messages to individual recipients, and the brand.

title: Add a Template to an Envelope
heading_path: Add a Template to an Envelope
text:
Learn how to add templates to your envelopes. You can add one or more templates to an
envelope.

If you start an envelope, you can add one or more templates to the envelope. You can add
other documents, recipients, and fields to the envelope separately from the templates you
add.

1. From the Home or Agreements page, start a new
   envelope from Start > Envelopes > Send an Envelope.
2. From the prepare view, in the Add documents section, select UPLOAD >  Use a template.

   ![Add documents section showing the UPLOAD menu expanded and selection on Use a template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/addtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MjAsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.tlCm5cR8s-QhkPdQoPpj6BJlalVcmt9K-l4ifa9uQ-A&_LANG=enus "Add documents section showing the UPLOAD menu expanded and selection on Use a template.")
3. In the Select Templates list, browse or search through the
   template collections at left and select one or more templates to use.

   ![Select Templates window showing templates matching the search term "conference".](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/usetemplates.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MjAsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.tlCm5cR8s-QhkPdQoPpj6BJlalVcmt9K-l4ifa9uQ-A&_LANG=enus "Select Templates window showing templates matching the search term \"conference\".")
4. Click ADD SELECTED.

   The documents, recipients, messages, and recipient fields from the selected
   templates are added to your envelope.

title: Specify the Template Usage Option
text:
Learn how to specify the template usage option.

Use the step-by-step procedure to specify the
template usage option.

1. From the template prepare view (either on a new template that you create or when
   editing an existing template), select ADVANCED OPTIONS.

   ![The 'Add documents' page with an arrow pointed to the ADVANCED OPTIONS link.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/advancedoptionslink2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MDAsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.tyEu6KKnMwU6JcOEJSxumUo7SuTjN-hargAn8PK0EuI&_LANG=enus "The 'Add documents' page with an arrow pointed to the ADVANCED OPTIONS link.")
2. In the Template Usage section, select the option for the
   specific usage option you want to apply to the template:

   - Quick send with advanced edit option (default)
   - Quick send only
   - Advanced edit only
     ![The list of options for the specific template usage that you want to apply to the template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templateusageoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MDAsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.tyEu6KKnMwU6JcOEJSxumUo7SuTjN-hargAn8PK0EuI&_LANG=enus "The list of options for the specific template usage that you want to apply to the template.")
3. Click Save.
4. To finish editing your template, click Save and Close.

title: Specify Recipient Modification Settings
text:
1. From the template prepare view (either on a new template that you create or when
   editing an existing template), on a recipient row, select CUSTOMIZE >  Advanced settings.

   ![Template role recipient card with Customize menu expanded and Advanced settings option highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/advancedrecipientoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MDgsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.rk9dHBHkH5czfWnnTt8WQbfNKa0mp5C6LPUl8mgratQ&_LANG=enus "Template role recipient card with Customize menu expanded and Advanced settings option highlighted.")
2. In the Advanced Settings panel, select the check boxes for the
   modification settings you want to apply to the recipient:

   - Don't allow senders to edit recipient
   - Don't allow senders to delete recipient
3. If your envelope includes any manager recipients (a recipient of the type Allow to
   Edit, Specify Recipients, or Update Recipients), in the Advanced Settings panel, you can
   choose to lock their name or email address.
4. Click Done.

   The panel collapses and shows that settings are selected.
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templateadvancedsettingsselected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MDgsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.rk9dHBHkH5czfWnnTt8WQbfNKa0mp5C6LPUl8mgratQ&_LANG=enus)

title: When Can I Merge Recipients?
text:
In order for recipients to merge, some things must match exactly, and for others there must not be a conflict. The latter requirement applies to access code, authentication method, and private message recipient data. For these criteria, one duplicate recipient can have data specified (e.g., access code) and all other duplicates must not have any conflicting data specified.

Note: Merging recipients must occur before an envelope is sent; you cannot
merge recipients when correcting an envelope.

These are the criteria that must be met in order to merge duplicate recipients:

- **Signing order**: If a signing order is specified, the recipients' position in the order must be an exact match.
- **Role name**: If role names are used, recipient role names must be an exact match.
- **Recipient action**: Same recipient action (Needs to Sign, Receives a Copy,
  etc.).
- **Name and email**: If name and email address are specified, they must be an exact match.
- **Authentication**: Same authentication method, only one duplicate has an authentication method, or none (no conflict). Note that access code can be applied with an authentication method (phone, SMS, or knowledge based). So you can have one recipient with an access code and another with an authentication method and combine these into a single recipient. However, you cannot have two duplicate recipients with an access code or an authentication method unless the specifics are the same for both recipients.
- **Private message**: Same private message, only one duplicate has a private message, or none (no conflict).

title: Add a Password to a Template
heading_path: Add a Password to a Template
text:
Learn how to protect your shared templates from being modified or copied by adding a
password.

Protect your templates from being modified or copied
by adding a password. If the template is shared with other users, they must provide the
password to edit, copy, or share the template with others.

Note: Account administrators don't need to provide
passwords on protected templates. They can edit any template created within the account.

1. Start by creating a new template (see [Create
   Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=uab1578456394214.html&_LANG=enus "Creating a template is a lot like starting a document. You add files, recipients, messages, and signing fields much like when you create a document.")) or editing an existing template (see [Edit Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=xns1578456372645.html&_LANG=enus "You can edit any template that you previously defined and saved.")).
2. In the Add Documents and Recipients view, select Advanced
   Options.

   ![The 'Add documents' page with an arrow pointing to the ADVANCED OPTIONS link.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/advancedoptionslink2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1ODIsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.7_SFpuK7JmNofb0Lc9mMEVRokK9spS320p56FgyUJuU&_LANG=enus "The 'Add documents' page with an arrow pointing to the ADVANCED OPTIONS link.")
3. In the Advanced Options dialog, under the Template
   Password section, turn on password protection and enter a password.

   Template passwords are limited to 15 characters.
4. Click Save.
5. Finish creating or editing your template, and then save and close it.

   A key icon on the Template Details view indicates a password is
   active on the template.

   If your template is shared with other users, they must provide the password in order
   to edit it. Administrators are not required to enter passwords and can edit any template
   on the account.
   ![The Templates Detail page with an arrow pointing to the key icon. The key indicates a password is active on the template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatepasswordicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1ODIsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.7_SFpuK7JmNofb0Lc9mMEVRokK9spS320p56FgyUJuU&_LANG=enus "The Templates Detail page with an arrow pointing to the key icon. The key indicates a password is active on the template.")

title: Template Passwords
text:
Read about when to use template passwords.

If the templates that are shared with you have a password applied, you'll need the password
to edit the template. Passwords are set by the template creator. [Learn more...](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=xwo1578456395432.html&_LANG=enus "Template passwords protect against editing. If the template is shared with other users, for them to edit the shared template, they must provide the password.")

If you are an account administrator, you don't need a password to edit the templates created
by your account users.

title: Limit Changes to Templates and Template Fields
heading_path: Limit Changes to Templates and Template Fields
text:
Use template and recipient modification settings to limit the types of changes that can
be made when your templates are used to send a document. Use the Sender
Permission settings to protect fields on a template from being changed or
deleted.

For the templates you create, you can limit the changes that other users can make when using
a template to send an envelope. These restrictions are useful if you want to control how your
others use your template.

This topic describes the options and steps for restricting changes to your template that
include general template settings, recipient details and settings, and template fields.

Note: If you [share
a template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=lrh1578456401532.html&_LANG=enus "Sharing a template allows other users and user groups on your account to access and use it to send documents.") with other account users, they may be able to edit your template. This
includes changing modification or permission property settings. You can protect your shared
template from being modified by other users by [setting a
password](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=xwo1578456395432.html&_LANG=enus "Template passwords protect against editing. If the template is shared with other users, for them to edit the shared template, they must provide the password."). Then, if other users want to edit a shared template, they must provide the
password.

title: Limit Changes to Templates and Template Fields
heading_path: Limit Changes to Templates and Template Fields
Video: How to protect a template from changes
text:
Here's a short video to introduce the various ways you can limit the changes other users
can make when using your shared template.

title: Move a Template Into a Folder
heading_path: Move a Template Into a Folder
text:
Learn how to move a template from the Templates list into a folder.

- From the Templates list:
  - Select one or more templates, and click Move and select the folder to
    move to.
    ![Screenshot of the Templates list that shows two selected templates and the Move button.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/movetemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3OTksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.5PkzUv63cJGNb6WrgCxs1LCjg7OrHSqe6xuniXWVN6g&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Templates list that shows two selected templates and the Move button.")
  - To move one template, click the template's more actions menu and select
    Move. Select the folder to move the template to, and click
    MOVE.
    ![The Move to Folder window that displays when you select the Move button.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/movetemplateactions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3OTksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.5PkzUv63cJGNb6WrgCxs1LCjg7OrHSqe6xuniXWVN6g&_LANG=enus "The Move to Folder window that displays when you select the Move button.")
- From the Template details view:
  - Click MOVE and select a folder to move the template to.
  ![Screenshot of the template details view with the MOVE button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/movetemplatedetails.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3OTksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.5PkzUv63cJGNb6WrgCxs1LCjg7OrHSqe6xuniXWVN6g&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the template details view with the MOVE button highlighted.")

title: Share a Template With a User or Group
heading_path: Share a Template With a User or Group
text:
Read the procedure to share a template with a user or group.

1. Navigate to the Templates page.
2. Locate the template you want to share by searching, filtering, or simply scanning your
   My Templates list.
3. Mark the check box for the template to select it. You can select multiple templates to share at once.
4. Click MORE and select Share with Users.

   ![Share option in the More menu to share multiple templates](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/sharetemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2OTYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.q5Zh6vjc_Cb4CCJ82HxT7IGZuFiFnrGn6aYoWruT5sQ&_LANG=enus "Share option in the More menu to share multiple templates")

   The Sharing dialog box opens for you to select the users and groups to share your
   template with.
5. Make your user and group sharing selections - the Search function returns results for the selected tab:

   - USERS - lists all users on your account.
   - GROUPS - lists all groups defined for your account. Groups
     are listed by name only; group members are not displayed. The list includes standard
     account groups of Administrators and Everyone. By default, all templates are shared
     with the Administrators group and this doesn't change. Along with the standard groups,
     your Docusign administrator can define customized groups of account
     users and can provide further details. [Learn
     more...](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=cuv1583277363488.html&bundleId=pik1583277475390&_LANG=enus)

     Tip: To
     easily share your template with everyone in your account, on the
     USERS tab, mark the Select all
     checkbox.
   - SELECTED - all users and groups that are selected for
     sharing. Use this tab to verify your selections and make any necessary changes.
     ![Sharing dialog box to select users and groups to share a template with](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/sharingtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2OTYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.q5Zh6vjc_Cb4CCJ82HxT7IGZuFiFnrGn6aYoWruT5sQ&_LANG=enus "Sharing dialog box to select users and groups to share a template with")
6. Click SHARE.

   The Template Details view updates to indicate the template is now shared. You can
   always check to see whom it is shared with and make changes to the sharing selections.
   ![Template details showing where to check who a template is shared with](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/sharedtemplateindicators.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2OTYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.q5Zh6vjc_Cb4CCJ82HxT7IGZuFiFnrGn6aYoWruT5sQ&_LANG=enus "Template details showing where to check who a template is shared with")

   For the people you have shared the template with, it displays in the
   Shared with Me and All Templates
   collections on their Templates page.

title: Change or Remove a Template Password
heading_path: Change or Remove a Template Password
text:
Learn how to change or remove a template password.

Template passwords are encrypted. If you forget the password you added to a template, you can easily remove it or enter a new one.

Only the template creator or an account administrator can change or remove a password.

1. Start by editing your template (as described in the [Edit Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=xns1578456372645.html&_LANG=enus "You can edit any template that you previously defined and saved.") guide).
2. Select ADVANCED OPTIONS > Template Password >  Reset.

   ![Screenshot of the Template Password section of the advanced options for a template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/resettemplatepassword.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1ODQsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.gKMIAOcKdATeLi0M_rn1nOQoD8TNPkhV-lYYHMRqJII&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Template Password section of the advanced options for a template.")

   The empty password fields display.
3. Do one of the following:
   - To enter a new password, complete the password fields.
   - To remove the password, leave the fields blank.
4. Select Save.
5. Save and close the template.

title: Include or Exclude Templates From Template Matching
heading_path: Include or Exclude Templates From Template Matching
text:
The matching state determines if the template is evaluated for template matching when you [add files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=crk1578456412036.html&bundleId=ulp1643236876813&_LANG=enus) to your envelope. An individual template can be in one the following matching states:

- **Eligible for matching**

  When you create a template, it is automatically eligible for template matching. When you
  upload files to Docusign, eligible templates are included in the pool of templates
  evaluated for a match. The template remains eligible as long as it has been used within
  the past 40 days. This is the default matching state for all templates.

  After 40
  days of no activity on an eligible template, the matching state switches to Excluded. The
  template remains in your account, it is just not used for matching.

  You can set an
  eligible template to Included or Excluded.
- **Included in matching**

  If you set the matching behavior for a template to Included, then it is always
  checked for a match. The template remains in the matching pool and never times
  out.

  You can set an included template to Excluded.
- **Excluded from matching**

  If you set the matching behavior for a template to Excluded or an eligible template goes
  unused and switches automatically to Excluded, it is never checked for a match. The
  template remains in your account and you can still use it to create a document or apply it
  manually to an uploaded file.

  You can set an excluded template to Included.

Note: The ability to set the template matching behavior on an individual
template is limited to certain account plans.

title: Recipient Modification Settings
text:
Learn about recipient modification settings.

You can limit the changes that senders can make to individual recipients on your template using the recipient advanced options.

If your template includes any manager recipients, that is, recipients with the action Allow to Edit, Specify Recipients, or Update Recipients, you can limit the recipient changes they can make. For more information about these recipient types, see [Manager Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=jbn1578456307301.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Recipient Modification Settings
text:
For senders who use your template, the settings and their effects when the template is used or applied to a document are as follows:

![The Advanced Settings for a recipient highlighting the Recipient Modification options that don't allow senders to edit or delete the recipient.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templaterecipientmodificationsettings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MDMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.JSgdAfXZIBA4KbEQbbWOjwTAGD-Qd1Dv_bG8ook5hLo&_LANG=enus "The Advanced Settings for a recipient highlighting the Recipient Modification options that don't allow senders to edit or delete the recipient.")

- Don't allow senders to edit recipient

  Completed recipient information for the selected recipient cannot be modified. If there is any missing recipient information, such as when only the role is provided, and the name and email address are still needed to send the envelope, the sender can complete all remaining fields.
- Don't allow senders to delete recipient

  The selected recipient cannot be removed. This setting ensures that the recipient is included on all envelopes sent from the template.

title: Recipient Modification Settings
text:
For manager recipients, the settings and their effects when they are sent an envelope are as follows:

![The Advanced Settings page with Restrict Modification by manager recipients that lock names and emails.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templaterecipientmodificationmanagerrecipients.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MDMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.JSgdAfXZIBA4KbEQbbWOjwTAGD-Qd1Dv_bG8ook5hLo&_LANG=enus "The Advanced Settings page with Restrict Modification by manager recipients that lock names and emails.")

- Lock Name

  A manager recipient can't modify the recipient's name. If the name is not provided, either on the template or completed by the sender, then the manager recipient can enter it.
- Lock Email

  A manager recipient can't modify the recipient's email. If the email has not been provided, either on the template or completed by the sender, then the manager recipient can enter it.

title: Templates with PowerForms
text:
Read about how to edit a template with PowerForms.

If the template you want to edit has any PowerForms, then changes you make to the template will affect the PowerForms.

![The top part of My Templates page with a PowerForm column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatewithpowerforms.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NDYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.mTQ9IEuEUXP8IEcF9uk0gaXwaXttX0waROj3Yqg6lzQ&_LANG=enus "The top part of My Templates page with a PowerForm column.")

If you delete a template with PowerForms, all associated PowerForms are also
deleted. Any completed envelopes from the PowerForms remain in the PowerForm sender's
account.

title: Download and Upload Templates
text:
Use the download and upload functions to copy templates between users and between accounts, and even between Docusign environments.

The ability to download and upload templates is useful when you need to move copies of templates around. You can use the download and upload functions to copy templates between users and between accounts, and even between Docusign environments.

Note: You must have the appropriate template permission setting (*create* or *share*) in order to download or upload templates. This setting is controlled by your account administrator, as described in the  [Permissions Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus) guide.

title: Search for a Template
text:
Learn how to search for a template.

You can search for templates by:

- template name
- owner name and email
- recipient name and email
- template ID

You can apply a date range filter to narrow your search results to a particular time
period.

1. From the Templates page, select the collection you want to search
   in, such as All Templates or any other template folder in your account.
2. Click in the Search box to enter a search term.

   ![Templates page with the search box and filters highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/selecttemplates.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NzMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.wIy7tT-wmFTtYNxtMABsSMJpzv2Ux14x15RwKB_GrUo&_LANG=enus "Templates page with the search box and filters highlighted.")
3. (Optional) Adjust the Date range. By default, the range is set to "All time", but you
   can select shorter time periods to narrow your search. The Date range operates on the Last
   Change date.
4. Use the Advanced search to choose which template fields to
   search:

   ![Field selection for template search filters.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/searchtemplatesselectfieldoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NzMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.wIy7tT-wmFTtYNxtMABsSMJpzv2Ux14x15RwKB_GrUo&_LANG=enus "Field selection for template search filters.")
5. Click Apply.

   Your template list updates to show all matching results. The
   Folders column shows you where a template is located, such as "My
   Templates" or a folder name.

   Note: The search and filter criteria persist until you change or clear them. So,
   if you navigate to other folders and then return to Templates later, the filter criteria
   are still applied. Likewise, if you navigate to a different page in Docusign and then return to Templates later, the filter criteria are
   still applied.

title: Use Templates
text:
You can use your saved templates to start a new document.

Do you send the same or similar documents, or send documents to the same group of people, over and over? Templates help streamline the sending process. You can use your saved templates to start a new envelope. Everything defined in the saved template is applied and added to the new envelope. You can use the template as is, or you can add additional files, modify signing fields, add recipients, and add and modify messages.

Note: Your access to templates is controlled by your user permissions, as defined by
your Docusign administrator. If you cannot create or edit, or even use templates, check with
your administrator.

title: Related topics
text:
- [Download and Upload Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=jjd1578456627966.html&_LANG=enus "Use the download and upload functions to copy templates between users and between accounts, and even between Docusign environments."). Download an entire template in order to move it to a different account.
- [Download or Print Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=oeq1643226594604&topicId=ono1578456343275.html&_LANG=enus).
- [Edit Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=xns1578456372645.html&_LANG=enus "You can edit any template that you previously defined and saved."). Edit any template that you previously created and saved. If you have access to templates shared with you by other users, you may be able to edit these as well.

title: Share Templates
text:
Sharing a template allows other users and user groups on your account to access and use it to send documents.

Sharing a template allows other users and user groups on your account to access and use it to
send documents for signature. If you have template share permission on
your Docusign account, you can share any template you created. You can also modify sharing
settings for any template shared with you.

Important: Your template permissions are set by your
Docusign administrator. You must have template permission set to *share* in order to
share templates.

title: Share Templates
text:
There are two ways you can share your templates:

- Share a template directly with select users and groups.
- Share a template by adding it to a shared template folder.

title: How To Create a Template
text:
Learn how to create a template.

This procedure addresses everything that you can add or set for your template. Most of
these steps are optional, and you can choose exactly how you want to set up your template.
You can save a template at any level of completeness.

1. From the Templates page, select Start >  Envelope Templates >  Create a Template.

   The prepare view displays, where you can add documents, recipient roles, and
   messaging.
2. Enter a name and description for your template.

   These details are for internal use only and won’t be seen by signers.
3. Add documents.

   Upload the documents that will be part of your template.
4. Add recipient roles.

   Add roles as placeholders for recipients. Roles help users identify who should sign
   when they use the template. Roles are not required. However, you must enter a recipient
   for each individual who will act on your document to set up fields on your
   document.

   1. Enter a role name. Enter a unique role to represent each individual who must sign
      your document. Examples: Applicant 1, Applicant 2, Client, Sales Rep.
   2. Select the signing action for each recipient role. Example: Needs to Sign
   3. Set a signing order to control the routing order or route the envelope to the same
      role more than once.
   4. To add additional recipient roles, select ADD RECIPIENT and
      repeat these steps.

   Note: The maximum number of recipients for a single envelope is
   99.
5. Add named recipients.
   1. Enter the recipient's name and email.
   2. Select their signing action.
   3. (Optional) Enter a role for the recipient. Example: Sales Approval
   4. To add more named recipients, select ADD RECIPIENT and
      repeat these steps.
6. Set a routing order for your envelope or add an authentication method.

   Follow the procedure described in the [Add
   Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=jar1578456351432.html&bundleId=ulp1643236876813&_LANG=enus) guide.
7. Define a standard message for your template, or add private messages for individual
   recipients.

   Follow the procedure described in the [Add
   Messages](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=mpf1578456350875.html&bundleId=ulp1643236876813&_LANG=enus) guide.
8. Select an envelope type.

   This internal attribute classifies your agreement to aid in searching and reporting.
   Follow the procedure described in the [Specify an Envelope
   Type](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=scd1724821719754.html&_LANG=enus) guide.
9. Set advanced options by selecting ADVANCED OPTIONS from the top
   menu.

   Specify reminder and expiration schedules, or add a password to protect your template
   from being changed. See the [Set Advanced Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=tfm1578456367923.html&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&_LANG=enus)
   guide for more information.
10. Finalize your template.
    - To save without adding fields, select SAVE AND
      CLOSE.
    - To add signing fields, select NEXT and follow the steps in
      the [Add Fields to Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=odn1578456409392.html&bundleId=ulp1643236876813&_LANG=enus) guide.
    - To pre-fill document fields, follow the steps in the [Create a Template and Add Pre-fill Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=oqb1611172929107.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to create a template that contains pre-fill fields. Your senders can use these templates to pre-fill document fields before sending.") guide.
11. When done, select SAVE AND CLOSE.

    Your template is saved and ready to use.

title: Set Template Matching Behavior
text:
You can set matching behavior on the individual templates that you own or administer. The matching state determines if the template is evaluated for template matching when you add files to your document.

As a template creator or a Docusign administrator, provided you have [enabled template matching on your account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=fxo1578456612662.html&bundleId=jux1643235969954&_LANG=enus), you can set matching behavior on the individual templates that you own or administer. Template matching speeds up envelope preparation by importing the recipients, messages, and recipient fields from the template.

title: Set Custom IDR Zones
text:
Learn how to specify areas of a template for precise template matching.

Intelligent Document Recognition (IDR) is the process that reviews the text and information
in a document to determine if it matches a saved template. By default, template matching
examines the first and last 25 words in a document. To get more precise matching, you can
add custom IDR zones to your template documents.

Setting IDR zones is useful if you have multiple documents with similar opening and closing
texts. You can set IDR zones to focus on unique portions of the document.

You can select any region of text within a document for IDR. There is no limit to the
maximum or minimum volume of text that you can select to define a match. However, the less
text you select, the more false positive matches are likely. The more text that you select,
the longer the matching process will take.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Locate the template that you want to edit by searching, filtering, or simply scanning
   the Template list.
3. Click the template title to select it and open the template details view.
4. Click EDIT  to open the template for editing.
5. Click NEXT to continue to the Add Fields view for the template.
6. Click the gear icon at the top of the list of documents and
   select Add IDR Zones.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/editidrs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NjksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.P88xVgHe1iRbXE5QjzgUyqNwQWMRvSOwAvYbzY8PLK8&_LANG=enus)
7. In the IDR Tool panel, click and drag the Add IDR Zones tool to
   the text you want to mark as an IDR zone.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/idrselect.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NjksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.P88xVgHe1iRbXE5QjzgUyqNwQWMRvSOwAvYbzY8PLK8&_LANG=enus)
8. Click and drag on any of the sizing handles in the corners of the IDR zone to encompass
   the area you want to use for matching. Click and drag to zone to move the box around on
   your document.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/idrzone.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NjksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.P88xVgHe1iRbXE5QjzgUyqNwQWMRvSOwAvYbzY8PLK8&_LANG=enus)
9. Add additional zones as needed. When you're finished adding IDR zones, click DONE in the banner at the top of the page.
10. To edit existing zones, from the template Add Fields view, click the Edit
    IDR Zones  link at the top of the Documents list.

    ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/editidr.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NjksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.P88xVgHe1iRbXE5QjzgUyqNwQWMRvSOwAvYbzY8PLK8&_LANG=enus)
11. Edit the zones as follows:

    - To change the location of a zone, click and drag it to the new position.
    - To remove a zone, click to select it, and press **DELETE** on your keyboard.
    - To resize a zone, click to select it. Then click and drag on any one of the sizing
      handles at the corners of the zone.
12. When you are finished adding and editing IDR zones, click DONE > SAVE AND CLOSE.

Your IDR zone settings are applied and saved with the template.

title: Use a Shared Template
text:
1. Navigate to the Templates page and select the Shared
   with Me view.
2. Locate the shared template and click Use.
3. If the template is complete (contains at least one recipient, one document, and one field), you can fill in the recipient information for any role recipients, and either send as is, or select Advanced Edit to further customize your document.
4. If the template is not complete, follow the standard process to use a template and complete all required information and customize your document as needed.

title: Using Shared Templates
text:
Other template owners may choose to share templates with you. This topic explains how shared templates are presented in your account, and how to use and search for shared templates.

Besides the templates you create for yourself, other template owners may choose to share templates with you. Templates can be shared in two ways: directly or through a shared template folder. This guide explains how shared templates are presented in your account, and how to use them.

title: Deleting a template with an associated PowerForm
heading_path: Deleting a template with an associated PowerForm
text:
Learn how to delete a template that has a PowerForm associated with it.

When you delete a template with an associated PowerForm, the PowerForm is also deleted.

![A portion of the My Templates page that lists templates with Powerforms associated with them.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatewithpowerforms.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NjAsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.JPqyAIXmV0Txv4WPJy66raTH9fPzcCYg7WB9s4B2HLs&_LANG=enus "A portion of the My Templates page that lists templates with Powerforms associated with them.")

Any user who tries to access the form through the PowerForm URL will get an error
stating the form is no longer available.

Note:

Make sure to check if the Powerform URL is in Active distribution before you delete a
template associated with it. For example, if you have embedded the URL on your website or
included the URL link in an email campaign, you must remove it from these distribution
points before deleting the template.

title: How to Merge Duplicate Recipients
text:
Merging duplicate recipients depends on whether the duplicates are role-only recipients or named persons.

- Role duplicate recipients, where only the role is specified without any name or email information, will combine automatically when you apply or add the templates, provided all applicable conditions are met.

  Example: You upload two templates and both contain the role "Client" as the first recipient. Since the role names and the signing order match exactly, if there is no conflicting information for authentication and private message, these role recipients will combine into a single envelope recipient.
- Named duplicate recipients, where the name and email information is specified, if you meet the requirements, you have a choice of merging the duplicates or changing the signing order to make each recipient in the signing order unique. Duplicate named recipients are not merged automatically, even if they meet all of the criteria to merge.

  Example: You upload two templates and both contain the named recipient "Jane March" with the email address "jane.march@dsxtr.com" as the second recipient. Since the names, email address, and the signing order match exactly, if there is no conflicting information for authentication and private message, you will be prompted to merge the duplicate recipients. You can choose to merge them into a single recipient or change the signer information to retain both as individual recipients on the envelope.

1. After starting an envelope and adding your templates, your recipient list contains duplicate recipients as described in the previous sections. (For more details on this step, see [Use Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=kjb1578456404736.html&_LANG=enus "You can use your saved templates to start a new document.") and [Apply Templates to Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=aoz1578456267317.html&_LANG=enus).)

   ![Envelope prepare view with two duplicate recipients](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/duplicaterecipientslist.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NTUsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.S6VJaby4FQ-2yJ-prLe_qWZo0Tr6ECI1fVZQB5IShzA&_LANG=enus "Envelope prepare view with two duplicate recipients")
2. Click NEXT to continue to the Add Fields view.

   The Duplicate Recipients dialog displays for you to decide how to manage the duplicates. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/duplicaterecipientsmodal.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NTUsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.S6VJaby4FQ-2yJ-prLe_qWZo0Tr6ECI1fVZQB5IShzA&_LANG=enus)
3. Do one of the following:

   - MERGE RECIPIENTS. If all recipient details match, you can merge the duplicates into a single envelope recipient by clicking this option. You will continue to the Add Fields view, where you can review and add fields for the merged recipient.
   - EDIT RECIPIENTS. If you do not want to merge the duplicates, click this option and set a signing order such that the duplicates are no longer at the same position in the order. Or, if you know that recipient details are conflicting such that the recipients cannot be merged, click this option to edit the recipient information.
4. If you attempt to merge recipients but they cannot be merged, an error message displays. Review your duplicate recipients for any details that might be in conflict and correct as needed.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/mergerecipientserror.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NTUsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.S6VJaby4FQ-2yJ-prLe_qWZo0Tr6ECI1fVZQB5IShzA&_LANG=enus)

title: Related topics
text:
- [Working With Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=dqj1578456412286.html&_LANG=enus "Templates help streamline the sending process when you frequently send the same or similar documents, or send documents to the same group of people."). Information about templates including a short introductory video.
- [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus). Your user permission settings are controlled by your Docusign administrator.
- [Apply Templates to Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=aoz1578456267317.html&_LANG=enus). Apply template settings only to the documents you upload to send.
- [Using Shared Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=mpq1578456404101.html&_LANG=enus "Other template owners may choose to share templates with you. This topic explains how shared templates are presented in your account, and how to use and search for shared templates."). Use templates that others have shared with you.
- [Merging Duplicate Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=wux1578456611310.html&_LANG=enus). How to merge duplicate recipients when using multiple templates.
- [Locate Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=epp1578456404448.html&_LANG=enus "Read how to locate templates that you own or are shared with you."). How to locate templates using the search and filters options.
- [Limit Changes to Templates and Template Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=gsz1578456308195.html&_LANG=enus "Use template and recipient modification settings to limit the types of changes that can be made when your templates are used to send a document. Use the Sender Permission settings to protect fields on a template from being changed or deleted."). How to limit the changes that can be made when a template is used to send an envelope.

title: Use a Template to Create an Envelope
heading_path: Use a Template to Create an Envelope
text:
Learn how to quickly create an envelope using a prepared template.

You can use any saved template you can access to start a new envelope. Everything defined
in the saved template is applied and added to the new envelope. You can use the template as
is, add additional files, modify signing fields, add recipients, and add and modify
messages.

Watch a short video on creating and sending an envelope using a template. Then use the
step-by-step instructions to guide you through the process.

1. Navigate to the Templates page.
2. Select the template collection you want to select from:

   - **My Templates** - contains all of the templates you created or were transferred to your ownership.
   - **Shared with Me** - contains all the templates that are shared with you, either directly or through a shared folder. If you are an account admin, Shared with Me will include all templates on the account that you do not own.
   - **All Templates** - contains all templates to which you have access. This collection includes your own templates and those shared with you.
   - **Favorites** - contains all templates you mark as a favorite. You can have up to 50 favorite templates.
   - **Deleted** - contains all templates you have recently deleted.
3. Locate the template you want to use by searching, filtering, or simply scanning the list.
4. To use the template to start a new document, click USE.

   ![Screenshot of the Favorites template list with the Use button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/usetemplatelist.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MTUsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.mEd1gYuErb2TM3FZsJBo5KLYXsFGQ2ZHwq5ebX3ZIGg&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Favorites template list with the Use button highlighted.")
5. A new envelope opens with your template settings loaded. If your envelope is complete
   with at least one file, one recipient, and one field, then follow [Use a Complete Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=opk1578456289004.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use complete templates. Complete templates have everything required to generate an envelope. All you do is enter the recipient information.").

   Note: Depending on the usage options specified on the template, you may be
   directed to use the template as is, as described in [Use a Complete Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=opk1578456289004.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use complete templates. Complete templates have everything required to generate an envelope. All you do is enter the recipient information."). Or you may have to complete the remaining steps in
   this procedure.
6. For any recipient roles, enter the actual email and name values.
