   ![Add recipients section with a role recipient.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/rolerecipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MTUsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.mEd1gYuErb2TM3FZsJBo5KLYXsFGQ2ZHwq5ebX3ZIGg&_LANG=enus "Add recipients section with a role recipient.")
7. Enter your email message subject and text. If your template includes a message subject
   and text, that information is added to your envelope. You can use the template message as
   is, or modify it.

   TIP: If you send the same envelope over and over, enter a
   custom subject line. If you use the default subject as is, the envelopes will all be
   titled the same, making it hard to identify a particular one.
8. To review the recipient fields, click NEXT.
9. To send your envelope click SEND.

title: Download Individual Pages From a Template Document
heading_path: Download Individual Pages From a Template Document
text:
You can download full resolution images of any single page in any template document that you have access to. Pages are downloaded as PNG files. You can print or save the PNG file to PDF or other format, as needed.

1. From the TEMPLATES page, locate the template containing the document from which you want to download one or more full resolution pages.
2. Click the template name to go to the Details view.
3. In the document panel on the right, locate the page you want to download and click the
   download icon.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/downloadtemplatepage.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MjUsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.tTJuL6WzVGbwtEbF4vQI70SNCRPIUR84GQWYxKbrwgM&_LANG=enus)
4. The selected page opens in a new browser window. You can use the browser controls to
   print the page to a PDF, or you can right-click and save the image.

title: Complete Templates
text:
Learn how to use complete templates. Complete templates have everything required to
generate an envelope. All you do is enter the recipient information.

If your template is complete, you can save even more time. For a template to be complete,
it must contain at least one of each of the following items: document, recipient, and
recipient field. When you use a complete template, you have the option to simply enter the
recipient names and review the email message. If your account uses envelope custom fields,
you can enter the field values.

Note: EXAMPLE—Complete Templates

Your Facilities
staff uses Docusign to issue temporary badges to contractors working in
your facility. You receive several contractors every month, so you have a template
prepared with the temporary access and badge request files that contractors sign. The
template is complete, with the files, a recipient role for the contractor, and the fields
for the contractor to complete. When Facilities uses the template, they enter the
contractor's name and email, and send the envelope.

1. Use one of the following methods to start an envelope from a complete template. Your
   templates and the templates shared with you are available to search and select.

   - From the Agreements page, select Start > Envelopes > Use a Template. Select the complete template you want to use and click Add
     Selected.
   - **From the Templates page**: Locate the complete template that you want to use
     for your new document and select **USE**.
     ![Screenshot of the Favorites list of templates with the Use button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/usetemplatelist.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MTcsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.LYi4G5gOYwr_Pynk9etBgu0aJUFpjVCQ574CnEtrYbU&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Favorites list of templates with the Use button highlighted.")
2. Your new envelope is shown in a summary view for you to complete the required fields
   and review other information:
   1. Complete the recipient information for any role recipients.
   2. Review the email subject and text and adjust as desired.
   3. (Optional) Enter values for any envelope custom fields defined for your
      account.

      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/usetemplatedialog.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3MTcsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.LYi4G5gOYwr_Pynk9etBgu0aJUFpjVCQ574CnEtrYbU&_LANG=enus)
3. To send your envelope as is, click SEND.
4. (Optional) To make additional changes, such as upload additional documents, or add more
   recipients or recipient fields, click ADVANCED EDIT, and complete
   and send your envelope.

title: Download Templates
text:
Learn about downloading templates in your account or those shared with you.

You can download any template available in your account. This includes both the templates
that you create and those created by others and shared with you.

You can download templates one at a time or in bulk. Downloading a single template from the
template details view exports a copy of the template in JSON format. If you select one or
more templates from the Templates page to download, they are delivered in JSON format in a
.ZIP file.

To download one template

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Locate the template that you want to download by searching, filtering, or simply
   scanning the Templates list.
3. Click the template actions menu and select Download.

   A .ZIP file containing a copy of the template in JSON format is saved to your Downloads
   folder. The file name is "DocuSignTemplates\_[date\_and\_time].zip".

To download multiple templates at once

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select the All Templates view and locate the templates
   you want to download by searching, filtering, or simply scanning the list.
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/selecttemplates.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3ODIsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.UCuKgsmM88ZmuAZAYit1KDlSq0ussCW1rBeEN9KK9iE&_LANG=enus)
3. Select the checkboxes next to the templates that you want to download.
4. Click the more action menu and select
   Download.

   A .ZIP file containing copies of the selected
   templates in JSON format is saved to your Downloads folder. The file name is
   "DocuSignTemplates\_[date\_and\_time].zip".

title: Create a Template and Add Pre-fill Fields
heading_path: Create a Template and Add Pre-fill Fields
text:
Learn how to create a template that contains pre-fill fields. Your senders can use these
templates to pre-fill document fields before sending.

When you create a template for documents you send frequently, you can add
pre-fill fields to save more time. While a manager or administrator usually creates
templates, any user who has permission to create templates can perform this step.

For pre-fill text and checkbox fields, you can add data validation. Data validation
allows you to enforce a validation standard on the field, helping to ensure you get
the right data from the sender. Learn more about [data validation for text fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=jgd1578456357365.html&_LANG=enus) and [checkboxes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=srj1578456517556.html&_LANG=enus).

When you use the template and send an envelope, as soon as a signer opens the document to view
or sign, or when a carbon copy recipient receives the envelope, data entered in a
pre-fill field becomes a permanent part of the document. The pre-fill data is
visible to all recipients with access to the document that it is placed on. From
that point on, the pre-filled data can no longer be changed, corrected, or
removed.

Note: If you add pre-fill fields to a template the template usage
option for quick send only is not supported. Template usage
is an advanced option setting. Read more about template usage in the [Template Usage Options](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=zak1578456299489.html&_LANG=enus "Read about the different template advanced options settings.") topic.

You should already know how to [start a new
template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=uab1578456394214.html&_LANG=enus "Creating a template is a lot like starting a document. You add files, recipients, messages, and signing fields much like when you create a document.").

1. When you enter the prepare view for your template, select the pencil icon on
   the left to access the Pre-fill Tools.

   ![The Pre-fill Tools tab.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/prefilltoolstemplate072021.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1NjMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.vlOly3QQiS7w4OVtK_LKwYLKmGxd2nwn0ffln2ALIRA&_LANG=enus "The Pre-fill Tools tab.")
2. Drag the pre-fill fields that you want to use onto the document. See the [pre-fill field types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=hlt1627421322398.html&_LANG=enus) for details.
3. (Optional) You can require senders to complete a pre-fill field before sending.
   Select the field, then select Mandatory in the Field
   Properties panel.

   ![Selected pre-fill field and property panel highlighting the Mandatory Sender Permissions option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/prefilltoolstemplatemandatory072021.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1NjMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.vlOly3QQiS7w4OVtK_LKwYLKmGxd2nwn0ffln2ALIRA&_LANG=enus "Selected pre-fill field and property panel highlighting the Mandatory Sender Permissions option.")
4. (Optional) For pre-fill text and checkbox fields, you can add data validation.
   Select the field, then expand the Validation property and
   select the validation to apply.

title: Create a Template and Add Pre-fill Fields
heading_path: Create a Template and Add Pre-fill Fields
text:
Permissions option.")
4. (Optional) For pre-fill text and checkbox fields, you can add data validation.
   Select the field, then expand the Validation property and
   select the validation to apply.

   ![Validation property menu expanded to show list of options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/validationoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1NjMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.vlOly3QQiS7w4OVtK_LKwYLKmGxd2nwn0ffln2ALIRA&_LANG=enus "Validation property menu expanded to show list of options.")
5. (Optional) If you want to set initial values for any pre-fill fields before
   saving the template (such as the name of your department or division), do that
   now. Select the field, then enter the value for it in the Add
   Text property.

   Note: To lock the values so senders can't change them,
   select Restrict Changes in the Sender
   Permissions property.
6. Finish preparing your template with fields for signers.
7. Select SAVE AND CLOSE.

The template is saved, and all administrators on the account can
access and use it. If you want others in your organization to use the template, you must
share it. Those users must have permissions to use templates. For details, see [Permission Profiles](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pik1583277475390&topicId=pof1583277362435.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Can I Merge Different Roles?
text:
If you apply templates with roles that are not exactly the same, you cannot merge the envelope recipients, even if you fill in the same name and email address. For example, if Template 1 has a role "Signer A" and Template 2 has a role "Signer 1", you cannot merge these two on the envelope.

To get these to merge, you would have to [edit the
template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=xns1578456372645.html&_LANG=enus "You can edit any template that you previously defined and saved.") such that both roles are exactly the same. Then you can create an envelope,
add the templates, and assuming [all other criteria are
met,](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=gdz1578456609353.html&_LANG=enus) the roles will merge.

title: Template Basics
text:
This topic describes the basics of using templates.

Templates help streamline the sending process when you frequently send the same or similar documents, or send documents to the same group of people. Templates allow you to create a standard document with any of the [supported file types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=xln1578456261162.html&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&_LANG=enus), with set recipients and recipient roles, and signing fields. A template can include multiple files and can be sent to one or more recipients, using a signing order or not, as desired. Templates can also contain the signing instructions for the document.

Templates are flexible. You can use a template as is, without making any changes, or you can use a template as a starting point for your document. You can make changes to all aspects of a template, customizing your document however you like.

title: Template Basics
text:
Your technology start-up regularly meets with potential partners. For each of these
meetings, you require a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) to protect your nascent business
ideas and intellectual property. You create a template and upload your NDA file. You define
a recipient role of "Technology Partner" and set signing fields for the role's signature,
title, company name, and date signed. You define a message for your Document with the
subject "NDA - Please Sign" and some appropriate text in the message field, asking for the
signed document prior to your upcoming meeting.

When you schedule a meeting that requires an NDA, you create a document using your
template, entering the actual contact information for the Technology Partner role that you
defined.

title: Template Basics
heading_path: What's the difference between templates and documents?
text:
Creating a template is as easy as setting up a document. The key difference is that a
document is a one-time transaction and a template is a blueprint for repeatable
transactions. With templates, you can add named persons, just as you do when creating a
regular document. But you can also define placeholder roles, that is,
not an actual person but rather a role that would regularly participate in a transaction
using the template. Typically, with templates you assign roles rather than named persons, as
it's likely your recipients and signers will change every time you use the template.

The NDA template example illustrates a situation where you would set a placeholder role for your template recipient, rather than a named person.

title: Shared Template Folders
text:
Shared folders allow you to simplify template sharing. Learn how to share a template
folder.

Shared template folders allow you to simplify template sharing. You set up the sharing options on the folder, selecting which users and groups have access to the folder. A shared template folder appears in the Shared Folders list for all users granted access. When you add templates to the folder, the templates are immediately accessible to the users through their access to the folder.

1. Navigate to the Templates page.
2. Click the New Folder icon next to Shared Folders.

   ![New folder control for shared folders](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/addsharedtemplatefolder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2OTgsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.0tfjVZsTkVhZpWg-Oq9Wm723KuYWohmp86xtmBBENiA&_LANG=enus "New folder control for shared folders")
3. Enter a name for your shared template folder and click CREATE.

   When you share the folder, the name you enter identifies the folder to the users with
   whom you share it.
4. Click the menu icon next to your new folder and select
   Share.
5. In the Sharing dialog, make your user and group sharing selections - the Search
   function returns results for the selected tab:

   - USERS - lists all users on your account.
   - GROUPS - lists all groups defined for your account. Groups
     are listed by name only; group members are not displayed. The list includes standard
     account groups of Administrators and Everyone. By default, all templates are shared
     with the Administrators group and this cannot be changed. In addition to the standard
     groups, your Docusign administrator can define customized groups of
     account users and can provide further details. [Learn
     more...](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=cuv1583277363488.html&bundleId=pik1583277475390&_LANG=enus)

     Tip: To
     easily share your template with everyone in your account, on the
     USERS tab, mark the Select all
     checkbox.
   - SELECTED - all users and groups that are selected for sharing. Use this tab
     to verify your selections and make any necessary changes.
6. Click SHARE.

   Your folder is now shared with the users and groups you selected, and these users can
   see the folder listed under their Shared Folders section. Any templates you add to the new
   folder are shared automatically and accessible to everyone with access to the folder.

   Important: Anyone with
   access to the shared folder and who has template share permissions can also add and
   remove templates from the shared folder.

title: Delete Templates
text:
Learn how to delete templates.

You can delete any template you own. When you delete a template, it remains in your Deleted
bin for up to 24 hours, and then it's removed permanently from the system. Once removed, a
template cannot be restored.

If you are an administrator on an account, then you can delete any template owned by any user
on the account.

title: Related topics
text:
- [Video: Share Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Share-Templates?language=en_US). Watch a short video on how to share
  templates.
- [Using Shared Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=mpq1578456404101.html&_LANG=enus "Other template owners may choose to share templates with you. This topic explains how shared templates are presented in your account, and how to use and search for shared templates."). A guide on how to use templates that are
  shared with you.

title: Create a Template with Merge Fields for Bulk Send
heading_path: Create a Template with Merge Fields for Bulk Send
text:
Learn how to add merge fields to your template message subject to personalize bulk send
envelopes. By including recipient data and envelope custom fields, you can ensure that each
message subject is unique and relevant to the recipient.

If you have a template you want to use for a bulk send, you can customize the message subject
and title for each envelope generated. You can add merge fields to include recipient data
and envelope custom fields (ECF) in the message subject.

By customizing the message subject with this data, you avoid creating multiple envelopes
with identical titles. The customized titles help you distinguish one transaction from one
another.

When the template is used for a bulk send, the bulk send list contains the custom data for
each envelope. The list contains the recipient data for each role specified on the template.
If you include ECF merge codes, your bulk list must include a column with the ECF name and
provide the text string value. For ECFs, bulk send supports text string entries only. List
value ECFs are not supported. For more information, see [Prepare your template for the bulk send](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=wtn1643071711000&topicId=sed1578456384133.html&_LANG=enus).

Note: For information on bulk send, see [Bulk Send for Multiple Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=wtn1643071711000&topicId=kjz1578456386508.html&_LANG=enus).

1. Create a new template and add your documents as usual.
2. In the
   Add
   Recipients section, add the recipient roles.
3. In the Add Message  section, click the merge field
   icon at the end of the message subject field. Select one or more options to
   insert, up to the character limit for the field.

   The dropdown list contains options to insert the Name and
   Email Address for each recipient role on the template. Also
   listed are any envelope custom fields defined on your account and
   visible to select on your template.

   ![Adding a merge code to a template message subject.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/mergecodes.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1NzIsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.-Jy0in57Td0bi7fGQdTrm7xWmzJGzpMNyQZW916Aj4w&_LANG=enus "Adding a merge code to a template message subject.")
4. Add fields for your role recipients as usual, and save and close the template.

When you use the template for a bulk send, the uploaded bulk
list provides the data for the merge
fields. For ECFs,
include a column with the ECF name and enter the value for each envelope in the bulk
list. Each envelope message subject is customized, making it easier to locate
and identify individual envelopes in the bulk send.

title: Template History
text:
Learn how to locate the history for a template. The template history shows the template
details including the template ID, owner, list of documents, and more.

The template history shows the template details including the template ID, owner, list of
documents, and more. The activity history lists the date and user for when the template was
created and each time it was edited.

1. Browse to the Templates page.
2. Locate the template that you want to edit by searching, filtering, or simply scanning
   the Templates list.
3. Click the template title to select it and open the template details view.
4. Click the MORE menu and select History.

   ![A portion of a template that shows the More menu with the History option selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatehistorymenu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MzksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.Vs3obdgPiieJw86MRxqJ2N1LA9iJ68haSUAEbxSKW08&_LANG=enus "A portion of a template that shows the More menu with the History option selected.")
5. The Template History displays in a window.

   ![The Template History window.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatehistorywindow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MzksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.Vs3obdgPiieJw86MRxqJ2N1LA9iJ68haSUAEbxSKW08&_LANG=enus "The Template History window.")

title: Common Use Cases
text:
Review some common use cases for uploading and downloading templates.

Templates are downloaded in JSON format when downloaded one at a time from the Template
details view. If you select templates from the Templates page to download, they are delivered
in JSON format in a .ZIP file. You can edit the template using a standard text editor or a
specialized editor. To send the downloaded file to another Docusign user, use your email or
other shared file service. Once the person receives your file, they can upload it to their
account.

Some common use cases for downloading and uploading templates:

- Give a copy of a template to someone in a different Docusign account
- Troubleshoot a template issue with Docusign Customer Support
- Move a copy of a template developed in your Docusign demo account to your production account
- Receive a template built for you by Docusign Professional Services

title: Download Template Documents
text:
You can download entire documents or individual pages from any template that you have access
to. When you download template documents, the files are in PDF format, regardless of the
original document format when they were added to the template. The downloaded file name
defaults to the document name used on the template.

See the following guides to learn how to download documents:

- [Download a Document From a Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=clw1578456603100.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to download a document from a template.")
- [Download Individual Pages From a Template Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=omh1578456603868.html&_LANG=enus)

Note: Your access to templates is controlled by your user permissions, as defined by
your Docusign administrator. If you cannot create or edit, or even use templates, check with
your administrator.

title: Create Templates
text:
Creating a template is a lot like starting a document. You add files, recipients,
messages, and signing fields much like when you create a document.

Creating a template is a lot like starting an envelope. You add files, recipients, messages,
and signing fields, and you can add any of the standard [supported
file types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=xln1578456261162.html&bundleId=gbo1643332197980&_LANG=enus) to your template.

The key difference between creating envelopes and templates is the option to add
placeholder roles to the recipients list. The placeholder role
represents a recipient in the envelopes created when you use the template. When you later
start an envelope using your template, you enter the actual person's information for that
role. Typically, you use placeholder roles for the recipients rather than named persons,
enabling easy reuse for sending to different people.

You can create a template from scratch; or copy an existing template and make changes to it.
You can also use the [Save as Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=fjw1578456298928.html&bundleId=oeq1643226594604&_LANG=enus) feature to make a
template out of any of your envelopes. You can save a template with as little or as much
information as you like. You can save it with just a title or just a document, or any other
level of completeness.

Note: Your access to templates is controlled by your
user permissions, as defined by your Docusign administrator. If you cannot
create or edit, or even use templates, check with your administrator.

title: Create Templates
heading_path: Create complete templates for maximum productivity
text:
If your template is complete, you can save even more time. A complete template contains at
least one of each of the following: file, recipient, and recipient field. Sending an
envelope using a complete template is fast and easy. You simply enter your recipient
information and add the email message. If your account uses envelope custom fields, you can
also enter the field values.

title: Create Templates
text:
When you share a template, you grant other users and user groups on your account access to
use it to send documents for signature. Provided you have template share permission on your
Docusign account, you can share any template you create. See [Share Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=lrh1578456401532.html&_LANG=enus "Sharing a template allows other users and user groups on your account to access and use it to send documents.") for more information.

title: Create Templates
heading_path: Limit the changes senders can make when they use your template
text:
There are several ways that you can restrict changes when a sender uses a template to send
an envelope. These restrictions provide control over your template and how senders use it.
For more information about how to limit usage and changes, see [Limit Changes to Templates and Template Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=gsz1578456308195.html&_LANG=enus "Use template and recipient modification settings to limit the types of changes that can be made when your templates are used to send a document. Use the Sender Permission settings to protect fields on a template from being changed or deleted.").

Note: To learn more about Docusign
eSignature templates and best practices, see Docusign University's [Building Advanced Templates: Become a NIGO Ninja Series](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/page/all-courses?q=Building%20Advanced%20Templates).

title: Edit a Template
text:
Learn how to edit a template.

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Locate the template that you want to edit by searching, filtering, or simply scanning
   the Template list.
3. Click the template actions menu and select Edit.

   If you do not see the Edit option, check with your Docusign administrator regarding
   your user permissions for templates.
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/edittemplatelist.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.NRVMuyqR60hJpMUTOMmLp8x67w7w_JXaBT25Rna5E3E&_LANG=enus)
4. If the template is password protected and you are not the owner, enter the password in the Enter Password dialog and click SUBMIT.

   The selected template opens in the Prepare Template view.
5. Edit the template. You can change any aspect of the template, including the template name, and the files, recipients, and recipient fields.

title: Template Modification Settings
text:
Read about the template modification settings.

Through the template advanced options settings, you can limit changes to template recipients, messages, and branding. These modification settings, and their effects when the template is used or applied to a document, are as follows:

- Don't allow sender to edit, add, or remove recipients

  Completed recipient information cannot be edited, and recipients cannot be deleted or
  added to the envelope. However, the sender can complete fields of any missing recipient
  information, such as when only a role is provided and the name and email address are
  needed to send the envelope.
- Don't allow senders to edit the subject, email, or private messages

  Protects the email message sent to all recipients, and any private messages
  entered for individual recipients. Anything you enter into these areas on the template is
  carried over to all documents created from the template. If you leave any of these areas
  blank, they will remain so in any template documents.
- Don't allow senders to edit the brand

  Protects the brand setting that you select for your template. Any envelopes sent from the
  template will use the specified brand for email notifications and the signing experience.

title: Add Merge Fields to a Template Message Subject
heading_path: Add Merge Fields to a Template Message Subject
text:
Learn how to use merge fields to personalize template message subjects with recipient
data and custom envelope fields.

You can add merge
fields to include recipient data and envelope custom fields (ECF) in the message subject. By
customizing the message subject with this data, you avoid creating multiple envelopes with
identical titles. The customized titles help you distinguish one transaction from one another.

1. Create a new template and add your files as usual.
2. Add one or more recipients, completing the Role field. You can
   also enter a name and email address for the recipient but it is not required.

   ![Template prepare view showing the Add Recipients section with two role recipients defined.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/mergerecipient.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1NzAsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.zPEJuCcmriEpSdxX6XA2QlgjpOMKrhOOtMoXnUbHzMI&_LANG=enus "Template prepare view showing the Add Recipients section with two role recipients defined.")
3. For the
   Subject, select the merge field icon
   at the end of the
   field,
   and select one or more options to insert.

   The dropdown list contains options to insert the name and email address for each
   recipient on the template. Also listed are any ECFs defined on your account and visible to
   select on your template.

   ![Example of selecting an ECF to insert into the message subject.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/mergecodes.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1NzAsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.zPEJuCcmriEpSdxX6XA2QlgjpOMKrhOOtMoXnUbHzMI&_LANG=enus "Example of selecting an ECF to insert into the message subject.")

   Note: The
   Subject field is limited to 100 characters. To insert a merge field, there
   must be sufficient characters remaining. You can edit the subject as needed to
   insert a merge field.
4. Add fields for your
   recipients
   as usual, and save and close the template.

When you use the template to start a new envelope, the message
subject and envelope title include the recipient information you specified. To populate ECF
merge fields, specify the values in the Add envelope custom fields
section of your envelope. If you don't set ECF values, the merge codes are ignored.

title: Merging Duplicate Recipients
text:
There are times when you might want to use multiple templates in one envelope. Those templates could contain the same role recipients and even the same named recipients. For example, let's say you have two templates you send to tenants that both use the role "Tenant" as the first recipient. If you use both templates, you could merge these two duplicate recipients into one so your tenant receives the documents once to sign.

Duplicate recipients occur when the same recipient is listed more than once at the same position in the routing order. To combine duplicate recipients, the recipients must meet certain conditions.

title: Copy a Template
text:
Learn how to copy a saved template.

Use this procedure to copy a saved
template.

1. Navigate to the Templates page.

   The Template list displays, showing your saved templates.
2. Locate the template you want to copy by searching, filtering, or simply scanning the
   Template list.
3. Click the template more actions menu and select
   Copy.

   ![A portion of the My Templates page with the Copyoption selected from the more actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/copyatemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1NjYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.bGNLZyjdUC-fCI4DIM9MXyrmEole-R0d8vBIPcEcf7o&_LANG=enus "A portion of the My Templates page with the Copyoption selected from the more actions menu.")

   The new template copy opens for you to change any aspect, including the template name,
   documents, recipients, and recipient fields.
4. When you are finished making changes, click SAVE AND CLOSE.

title: Sharing Templates by Adding Them to a Shared Template Folder
heading_path: Sharing Templates by Adding Them to a Shared Template Folder
text:
You can select one or more templates and then select one or more shared folders to add them
to.

1. Navigate to the Templates page.
2. Locate the template you wish to share by searching, filtering, or simply scanning the
   Template list, and mark the check box to select it. You can select multiple templates at
   once.
3. Click SHARE TO FOLDERS.
4. In the Share to Folders dialog, search or scroll to locate the
   shared folder you want to add your selected templates to. To select multiple folders, hold
   down the Ctrl key and click the folders. Each folder name you click remains
   highlighted.
5. Click SHARE.

The templates are added to the selected shared folders
and available to all users with access to the folders.

title: Identify Who Owns a Shared Template
heading_path: Identify Who Owns a Shared Template
text:
1. Navigate to the Templates page.
2. Select the All Templates view.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/alltemplateslist.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NDEsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.pKw6UepqqrxDZZdCV9ttzD4EHmQPbvYoFo3X1SI7XYw&_LANG=enus)
3. Locate the shared template by searching or simply scanning the templates listed.
4. The owner's name is listed in the OWNER column.

title: Sender Permission Field Properties
text:
Read about how to protect template fields from changes or deletion, and how sender
permission settings affect field editing.

You can protect the fields you add to your template from being changed or deleted. When the template is used, depending on which permission properties you set, the sender cannot change or delete protected fields. The sender permission property settings are as follows:

- Restrict changes

  This setting guarantees the field will be present on all envelopes created with the
  template, exactly as it was set up on the template. For all field types, the sender cannot
  delete the field or change the recipient, or modify the placement, size, data label,
  conditional logic, or any other field property setting.

  For text, checkbox, and
  radio button fields, the sender can enter text and make selections. For note fields, the
  sender can enter the note text. However, since the note field size is fixed, only the text
  that fits into the field is included and visible to the recipient.
- Mandatory

  This setting guarantees the field will be present on all envelopes created with the
  template. The sender cannot delete a mandatory field. They can, however, make other
  changes to the field, such as moving, resizing, or setting any other field
  properties.

title: Edit Templates
text:
You can edit any template that you previously defined and saved.

You can edit any template that you previously created and saved. If you have access to templates shared with you by other users, you may be able to edit these as well.

Note: Your access to templates is controlled by your user permissions, as defined by
your Docusign administrator. If you cannot create or edit, or even use templates, check with
your administrator.

title: Upload Templates
text:
Follow the procedure to upload temples to your account.

When you upload a template, it creates a new template in your account and you are the owner of the template.

You can upload any individual template downloaded from Docusign
eSignature. You can upload individual templates in either JSON or XML format to any Docusign account. You can also upload .ZIP files containing multiple
templates. When you upload a .ZIP file, it creates separate templates for each file in the
.ZIP file.

Note: There is a 25-MB limit for uploading
templates in any format (JSON, XML, or as a .ZIP file).

1. Go to the Templates page.
2. Select Start > Envelope Templates > Upload Template.

   ![Upload template menu selection.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/uploadtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3ODUsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.VHEkOMdVSwdqoW21kAwnpsuQ7qctz9ghAM8-MtigkYU&_LANG=enus "Upload template menu selection.")
3. Browse to the location of the template file you want to upload and open it.

   You can upload an individual JSON or XML template file, or a collection of zipped
   template files. If you upload a zipped file, the system automatically unzips and creates
   individual templates.
4. The new template displays at the top of your Templates list.

   If an uploaded template has the same name as an existing template, the name of the
   uploaded template has a number in parentheses added to the template name.

title: Template Passwords
text:
Template passwords protect against editing. If the template is shared with other users,
for them to edit the shared template, they must provide the password.

Template passwords protect against editing, sharing, copying, or downloading a template. You
can set a password on any template you create.

If a [template is shared with other users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=lrh1578456401532.html&_LANG=enus "Sharing a template allows other users and user groups on your account to access and use it to send documents.") who have
the permissions to edit (template create or share), they must first provide the password. In
this way, you can share templates and not worry about them being edited, shared, or copied. If
you want to permit another user to edit your template, you can provide them with the password.

Note: Account administrators don't need to provide
passwords on protected templates. They can edit any template created within the account.

When you download a template, the password is removed from the downloaded copy. You can provide the downloaded template to others without having to provide password details.

title: Template Matching Considerations
text:
Learn about template matching when you upload documents to an envelope or manually
apply templates to a document.

Template matching can occur automatically when you upload documents to an envelope and when
you manually apply templates to a document. For each case, there are a couple of rules to
consider when working with template matching.

- Automatic template matching: Requires the uploaded document page
  count be greater than or equal to the template page count. When you upload a
  document, templates with more pages than the document will not be included in the list of
  matching templates.

  For example, your template contains a 5-page document. You upload a
  3-page document that matches the template. However, because the document has fewer pages
  than the template, it does not match to the template. You can still *manually* apply
  the template, but *automatic* template matching will not work.
- **Manually applying a template: Matches pages of the document one to one**. When you
  apply a template manually to an uploaded document, each template is matched to the
  corresponding page in the document.

  For example, you have a 10-page document and you
  apply a 15-page template, along with the recipient and message details. The first 10 pages
  of the template are applied to the first 10 pages of the document. Any fields on the
  subsequent 5 pages in the template are ignored.
- For shared templates, only those shared directly with a user or group are used
  in template matching. The templates shared through a shared folder are
  excluded from the matching process when:
  - The template user isn't an administrator
  - The template user isn't the owner of the template
  - The template is not shared with the Everyone Group
  - The template is not shared with the group of which the user is a member

title: Template Collections
text:
Read the description of the types of template collections that display on your template
page.

The Templates page includes a set of collections to organize the templates you can access
automatically. These collections are as follows:

![Screenshot of the My Templates page that includes the side panel. It shows the template collections in the side panel.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatecollections.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ2NjksInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.NmXF4R4H-9B5mDmncFL0EgmwUz8BgNzKQS75bndaszs&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the My Templates page that includes the side panel. It shows the template collections in the side panel.")

- My Templates - contains the templates that you created or
  that were transferred to your ownership.
- Shared with Me - contains all the templates that are
  shared with you, either directly or through a shared folder. If you are an administrator,
  Shared with Me includes all templates on the account that you do
  not own.
- All Templates - contains all templates to which you have
  access. This collection includes your own templates and those shared with you.
- Favorites - contains all templates that you mark as a
  favorite. You can have up to 50 favorite templates.
- Deleted - contains all templates that you have recently
  deleted.

title: Specify Template Modification Settings
text:
Follow the procedure to specify template modification settings.

1. From the template prepare view (either on a new template that you create or when
   editing an existing template), select ADVANCED OPTIONS.

   ![The Add Documents page with a document uploaded and an arrow pointed to the ADVANCED OPTIONS button.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/advancedoptionslink2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.1OI1xaq-EUJ40Dhq9v3TbDKsJDmqxn5Yo5bYaHg36K4&_LANG=enus "The Add Documents page with a document uploaded and an arrow pointed to the ADVANCED OPTIONS button.")
2. In the Template Modification section, select the check boxes for the specific restrictions you want to apply to the template:

   - Don't allow sender to edit, add, or remove recipients
   - Don't allow senders to edit the subject, email, or private messages
   - Don't allow senders to edit the brand
     ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templatemodificationsettings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1OTMsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.1OI1xaq-EUJ40Dhq9v3TbDKsJDmqxn5Yo5bYaHg36K4&_LANG=enus)
3. Click Save to save your changes to the advanced options.
4. To finish editing your template, click Save and Close.

title: Locate a Template ID
text:
The unique Template ID is located in the URL string for the template and is visible from the Template details view.

The Template ID is a unique identifier for a template. This identifier is useful for API
developers using the Docusign API and also for troubleshooting issues with Docusign Customer
Support. You can find and copy the Template ID from the Template Details view.

1. Navigate to the Templates page.
2. Locate the template for which you want to get the Template ID by searching, filtering,
   or simply scanning the Templates list.
3. Click the template title to select it and open the template details view.
4. Select the Template ID link and click
   Copy.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templateid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ3NDUsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.M_VpIdjufyT_LTmerJez0a4w_6wGQH1kQzBl75zlg-U&_LANG=enus)

   The Template ID is copied to your clipboard.

title: Template Folders
text:
Template folders provide a way to organize your Templates list.

Template folders provide a way to organize your Templates list. You create folders from the Templates page. Once you create one or more folders, you can file your Templates into folders.

title: Template Usage Options
text:
Read about the different template advanced options settings.

Through the template advanced options settings, you can control how a template is used. The
selected template usage option applies only when the template is used from the
Templates page. It does not apply if the template is applied to
a document or added to an in-process envelope.

![Screenshot of the Template Usage options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/xry1643227563338/page/images/templateusageadvancedoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1OTYsInNoZWFmIjoieHJ5MTY0MzIyNzU2MzMzOCJ9.HBpzgBxuvzY6E2-2tCs1XtrmHPrMwQhjawH83BbE-MY&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Template Usage options.")

Details about the three template usage options and tips on why to use each type are
presented in the following sections.

title: Template Usage Options
heading_path: Quick send with advanced edit option
text:
When the template is used, the sender sees the quick send view. In this view, the
sender simply completes the recipient and envelope information. The template used
must contain at least one document, one recipient, and one recipient field.
Optionally, the sender can select the Advanced Edit option
and use the full envelope edit mode. If the template is incomplete, the sender goes
directly to the full envelope edit mode. This is the default setting.

Why use this option: Offers full control to the sender over how they want to
complete the envelope generated from the template.

title: Template Usage Options
text:
When the template is used, the sender simply completes the recipient and envelope
information and sends. The sender cannot make any changes to the recipient list,
documents, or recipient fields. This setting requires that the template have at
least one document, one recipient, and one recipient field.

Note: The quick send only option is not supported for templates
containing any pre-fill fields.

Why use this option: Restricts senders to using the template as is, without
any options to change recipient, document, or field details.

title: Template Usage Options
text:
When the template is used, the sender completes the recipient and envelope
information using the full envelope edit mode. This sender can make any allowed
changes, including prefilling recipient fields.

Why use this option: Senders must go through all envelope and document
information. Use this option when senders must complete field entries before sending
or make other changes to recipients or documents.

title: Related topics
text:
Review the links to related topics about templates.

- [Template Folders](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=zak1578456296231.html&_LANG=enus "Template folders provide a way to organize your Templates list."). Organize your templates by creating folders.
- [Share Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=lrh1578456401532.html&_LANG=enus "Sharing a template allows other users and user groups on your account to access and use it to send documents."). How to share templates with other account users.
- [Use Shared Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xry1643227563338&topicId=mpq1578456404101.html&_LANG=enus "Other template owners may choose to share templates with you. This topic explains how shared templates are presented in your account, and how to use and search for shared templates."). How to use templates shared with you.

title: Sign Through an SMS Text Notification
heading_path: Sign Through an SMS Text Notification
text:
Learn about how to sign a document sent through a Docusign SMS text
notification.

Senders can choose to send envelopes through real-time SMS text notifications directly to your
mobile device.

With
SMS notifications
,
you
can
quickly access and complete agreements from any location.

For example, you are currently traveling, and your rental
organization
sends your new lease agreement to review and sign. You receive notification through
an SMS text message (as well as an email notification). You can immediately review
and sign the lease from your mobile device.

Depending on your sender's configuration of this feature, you may receive SMS
notifications through:

- An email notification along with the SMS notification
- SMS notification only

Note: The document mark-up feature is not available on a mobile
device.

To sign a document
through
an SMS notification:

1. When you receive an SMS text message from Docusign, select
   the link in the message.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/sms-initial-msg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjUsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.u2iU5eFLFMUcV7R-l0V1TnXI7NIZv9qhNpifzqi7y2k&_LANG=enus)
   ![A screenshot of an SMS text message asking you to sign a document.]()
2. Select Yes at the following screen to consent to receive
   future SMS messages from Docusign.

   If you decline to receive future SMS text messages, you only see the
   messages from this sender
   through
   an email notification.

   The following message displays for an SMS-only notification.

   ![A screenshot of an SMS-only decline to sign message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/sms-decline.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjUsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.u2iU5eFLFMUcV7R-l0V1TnXI7NIZv9qhNpifzqi7y2k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of an SMS-only decline to sign message.")
3. Optionally share your current location at the prompt.

   ![A screenshot of an SMS-only review documents prompt.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signmobileshareloc.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjUsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.u2iU5eFLFMUcV7R-l0V1TnXI7NIZv9qhNpifzqi7y2k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of an SMS-only review documents prompt.")
4. If this is the first time using SMS to review a document, you are prompted to
   select the Mobile-Friendly experience.

   ![A screenshot of a selecting the SMS Mobile-Friendly experience.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signmobileselmf.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjUsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.u2iU5eFLFMUcV7R-l0V1TnXI7NIZv9qhNpifzqi7y2k&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a selecting the SMS Mobile-Friendly experience.")

   Selecting the mobile experience changes the formatting to enhance viewing on
   a small screen.
5. Select Start to display the document.
6. When the document you must sign displays, begin the signing process as
   described in the [Docusign eSignature Signing Process](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lou1573854977109.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Docusign eSignature signing process and eSignature signing terminology.") article.
7. Complete the signing actions that you usually perform from an email message
   request.

   You can:

title: Sign Through an SMS Text Notification
heading_path: Sign Through an SMS Text Notification
text:
arn about the Docusign eSignature signing process and eSignature signing terminology.") article.
7. Complete the signing actions that you usually perform from an email message
   request.

   You can:

   - Access the signing session with or without an access code as described in the [Docusign eSignature Signing Process](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lou1573854977109.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Docusign eSignature signing process and eSignature signing terminology.") article.
   - Finish later: save your changes and complete signing at another
     time.
   - Change signing responsibility as described in the [Change Signing Responsibility](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=uin1573855010064.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to assign a document to another person if you're not the right person to sign the document.")
     article.
   - Decline to sign as described in the [Decline To Sign an Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=fqo1573855017663.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how and when to decline to sign an envelope you review.") article.

Once signed, you receive a text message for the completed document that you can
download to your device and save for reference.

Note the following about SMS text delivery:

- SMS-only recipients who have existing Docusign accounts cannot have their
  completed documents associated with their accounts. If you choose to download
  the completed documents, the documents are only saved to the download
  location.
- If you opt out of Docusign SMS text notifications, any subsequent requests
  through Docusign SMS delivery are blocked.
  Manually
  opt in to Docusign
  SMS
  to reinstate SMS notifications. For detailed information about
  opting in, see the [Docusign SMS Service](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/sms-service) article.
- Senders can also require identity verification before you sign. See the [Verify Your Identity](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=mbg1573854981921.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about using the different authentication methods to verify your identity for Docusign eSignature.") article to learn about identity verification
  methods for signing a document.

title: Download or Print a Supplement From an Email Notification
heading_path: Download or Print a Supplement From an Email Notification
text:
Learn how to download or print a document from the eSignature supplement
view.

You can download a copy or print from the supplement
view. The easiest way to access a document is from the email notification.

1. Open any of the email notifications that you received and select
   REVIEW DOCUMENT to begin the signing process.
2. Select Agree to use electronic records and signatures  > Continue

   If there's no electronic records and signature (ERSD) option, the document
   opens when you select Review Document..
3. From the opened document, find the supplement you want to download and select
   VIEW.

   ![A screenshot of selecting the option to View the supplement](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewviewfromemail.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjMsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.wMkq8irhFCjXHDgWovPPaIyxORftVKrQQgjn-oayuzs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of selecting the option to View the supplement")
4. After viewing the supplement, save a copy of it by selecting the
   Download icon ![Download document icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewdownloadicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjMsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.wMkq8irhFCjXHDgWovPPaIyxORftVKrQQgjn-oayuzs&_LANG=enus "Download document icon.").

   The PDF of the supplement opens in a new browser window. In the new window,
   select the Download icon on the top right of the PDF
   and save to your computer.
5. After viewing the supplement, you can print it by selecting the
   Print icon ![Print document icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewprinticon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjMsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.wMkq8irhFCjXHDgWovPPaIyxORftVKrQQgjn-oayuzs&_LANG=enus "Print document icon.")

   The PDF of the supplement opens in a new browser window. In the new window,
   select the Print and follow printing instructions for
   your printer.

title: Complete the Signing Session
text:
Learn how to complete signing and optionally save or print a copy of your signed
documents.

After you add information to all the required fields and any signatures or initials, you
are ready to complete the signing session.

For short documents, such as a job offer letter, you can easily find and sign all the
required fields. However, for long or complex documents, such as a merger proposal, you may
fail to find all required fields. During a Docusign signing session, the
system makes sure you find and complete all required fields before completing the
process.

1. Review the information that you added to all the information fields and make any
   changes if necessary.

   Note the following:

   - You cannot complete the signing session if you enter information in an optional
     field, but the data is invalid. For example, if you added your zip code, but omitted a
     number. When you attempt to complete signing, the system takes you to the zip code
     field and prompts you to revise your input.
   - Make sure the provided information is correct, as you cannot change it after
     completing the signing session.
2. After reviewing the document, and completing all required fields, the following prompt
   displays.

   ![A screenshot of the Ready to Finish prompt.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewreadytofinish.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzksInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.8rO1HPAFpZze0h9FcywPyZLGlkBOu4JUjDZgEd-wjjE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Ready to Finish prompt.")

   Note the following:
   - If you select Finish without completing all required signing
     fields, the system displays the next required field that you must complete.

     ![A screenshot of a prompt to complete the next signing field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewpromptifnotdone.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzksInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.8rO1HPAFpZze0h9FcywPyZLGlkBOu4JUjDZgEd-wjjE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a prompt to complete the next signing field.")
   - If you attempt to exit the browser, change the URL, or refresh the signing session
     page, the system prompts you to confirm that you want to abandon your signing
     session.

     ![A screenshot of the Leave site confirmation message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewleaveconfirm.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzksInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.8rO1HPAFpZze0h9FcywPyZLGlkBOu4JUjDZgEd-wjjE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Leave site confirmation message.")
3. Complete the required fields and select Finish.

   Your signing information is saved and a confirmation displays.

   - If you already have a Docusign account, a copy is saved to your account.
   - If you don't have a Docusign account, a copy is sent to your email account when
     completed by all signers.

     You can optionally sign up for a free Docusign account as
     described in the [Electronic signing made simple](https://www.docusign.com/esignature/sign-documents-free) article. Sign up and save the document in
     your account.
4. If you don't complete the signing session, it eventually times out, and the following
   notice displays:

title: Complete the Signing Session
text:
signature/sign-documents-free) article. Sign up and save the document in
     your account.
4. If you don't complete the signing session, it eventually times out, and the following
   notice displays:

   ![A screenshot of signing session timeout notice.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signtimeoutnotice.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzksInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.8rO1HPAFpZze0h9FcywPyZLGlkBOu4JUjDZgEd-wjjE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of signing session timeout notice.")
5. Optionally download or print the document by selecting the appropriate icon.

   ![Screenshot of signing download and print icons.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signingprintdownload.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzksInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.8rO1HPAFpZze0h9FcywPyZLGlkBOu4JUjDZgEd-wjjE&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of signing download and print icons.")

   1. Select the Download icon to save the document as separate PDFs or combined PDFs and
      choose the download location.

      - Separate PDF: Downloads the documents in a .ZIP file and individual PDFs.
      - Combined PDF: Downloads a single PDF that includes all documents.
   2. Select the Print icon and select a printer.

   You have successfully completed electronically signing your documents.

title: Use Freeform Signing
text:
Learn how to use Docusign Freeform signing using the menu on the left
side of the signing page.

Use Freeform Signing to add information to the document using the menu on the left side of
the page. The menu has a list of fields that you can use to add signatures, initials, and
other information to the document. Drag the signature and information tags to the
document.

![Screenshot of the Freeform signing menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/newsigningfreeform.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQzOTQsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.egnE9tMGooVdBzlq7lKtnZZIDymjgFlJ-bgqVDzLMyQ&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Freeform signing menu.")

Note the following:

- When adding a signature, initial or other information to a document, drag the
  appropriate field (such as Signature, Initial, Date Signed) from the palette. Place the
  field onto the location in the document where you want the field to display.
- When you place a Signature or Initial field, you are asked to adopt your signature and
  initials. See the [Adopt Your Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=dga1573854990297.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to choose a style for your signature and initial when you first sign a document with Docusign.") article for more information.
- You can modify prefilled information as needed. The Name, First Name, Last Name, Email
  Address, organization and Title fields use pre-filled information based on what the sender
  enters for you
- The Date Signed field uses the current date.
- When you need to add text in the document, drag a Text field onto the document. After
  placing the Text field, you can type information directly into it. You might must resize
  the Text field to have all the information fit.
- The Checkbox field places a checked box on the document.

Note: After placing your first field,
FINISH becomes active. However, continue to place fields to
complete the documents before selecting FINISH.

To manage fields:

- Reposition a field by dragging it to a new location.
- Re-size a field by selecting it and then dragging the corner of the field to change the
  size.
- Remove a field from the document by selecting it and then selecting the delete icon on
  the field.

After you have placed all the fields on the document, select FINISH
to complete signing.

For more information, see the [Complete the Signing Session](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=abm1573854992359.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete signing and optionally save or print a copy of your signed documents.") article.

title: Sign Documents With the Signer-Held Mexican SAT Qualified Certificate
heading_path: Sign Documents With the Signer-Held Mexican SAT Qualified Certificate
text:
Learn how to sign documents using the certificate and private key that the SAT (Tax
Administration Service) office provides to Mexican residents.

title: Sign Documents With the Signer-Held Mexican SAT Qualified Certificate
heading_path: Sign Documents With the Signer-Held Mexican SAT Qualified Certificate
text:
The SAT (Tax Administration Service) provides Mexican taxpayers
with a certificate (.cer file) and a private key (.Key file) they can use to sign
documents. Before you sign with Mexican SAT qualified certificates, you must ensure you
have both items available on the device where you sign documents. For more information
about how to get SAT certificates, see the [Obtain your e.firma certificate](https://www.sat.gob.mx/tramites/16703/obten-tu-certificado-de-e.firma) page.

Someone sent you a Docusign email with documents to review and sign. The sender of the
envelope requires that you sign with a qualified certificate recognized under
Mexican law. As part of the signing process, you must use your qualified SAT
certificate and private key.

The following steps show you how to sign documents with the Signer-Held Mexican
SAT qualified certificate. Alternatively, watch this video to see the steps in
action.

1. Select REVIEW DOCUMENT.

   The Review and sign documents window opens with
   documents to sign.
2. Select CONTINUE.

   You can access your documents.
3. Select START.

   The signature process begins and takes you to the first
   Sign tag.
4. Select the Sign tag.
5. Select CONTINUE.

   The We need to attach your SAT certificate to your
   signature screen displays. It includes the Add your
   .cer file and Add your .key file sections
   for uploading your certificate and private keys.
6. Upload your certificate (.cer file).

   The details of your certificate are displayed in the Add your .cer
   file section.

   Note: If your SAT certificate
   has another format, select the certificate from a different provider in the
   page footer to access the relevant signing workflow.
7. Upload your private key (`.key` file).

   The details of your private key display in the Add your .key
   file section.
8. Enter the password of your certificate.
9. Select Next.

The signing process completes when the You’ve finished
signing! message displays. To access the final document, open the
completed notification email you receive when all recipients have finished signing.
Alternatively, you can access your document from your Docusign
account. If you want to contact the sender of the envelope, reply to the notification
email you received to review and sign the document.

title: Save or Print Document Supplements
text:
Learn how to save or print a supplement to a document you're signing.

Save a copy of a supplement

A document you print or download can include supplements. Because supplements can be
lengthy, sometimes supplements may not be included when you download the signed
document.

For instructions on how to download or print a document, see the [How do I download or print a document I
receive?](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=sto1596228661240.ditamap&topicId=kug1599162827686.html&_LANG=enus) article.

If supplements are not included in the document download, you can download a copy or
print from the supplement view. The easiest way to access a document is from the email
notification.

To download or print a supplement from the email notification:

1. Open an email notification that you received about the document.
2. Select Review Document.
3. Select Agree to use electronic records and signatures  > Continue

   If there's no electronic records and signature (ERSD) option, the document opens
   when you select Review Document..
4. From the opened document, find the supplement to download and select the supplement
   thumbnail to open the document.

   ![Screenshot of the thumbnails of supplemental documents.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signsuppliment.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.ZtbNhKaVyM-Lp8jD19_koHKLPOTbVMyEMN970dhGGRI&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the thumbnails of supplemental documents.")
5. After viewing the supplement, save a copy of it by selecting the
   Download icon ![Document download icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewdownloadicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.ZtbNhKaVyM-Lp8jD19_koHKLPOTbVMyEMN970dhGGRI&_LANG=enus "Document download icon.") .

   The PDF of the supplement opens in a new browser window. In the new window, select
   the Download icon on the top right of the PDF and save to
   your computer.
6. After viewing the supplement, you can print it by selecting the
   Print icon ![Document print icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewprinticon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.ZtbNhKaVyM-Lp8jD19_koHKLPOTbVMyEMN970dhGGRI&_LANG=enus "Document print icon.")

   The PDF of the supplement opens in a new browser window. In the new window, select
   the Print and follow printing instructions for your
   printer.

title: Signer-Held Digital Certificates Example
text:
Learn how to sign documents using signer-held digital certificates.

Someone sent you an email with documents to review and sign. You’re using a signer-held certificate to sign the documents in the envelope.

The following steps show you how to sign agreements using signer-held certificates.

1. Select REVIEW DOCUMENT.

   Signers can also access envelopes by selecting the Agreements > Action Required menu. They can open an envelope by selecting SIGN.

   The Please Review & Act on These Documents screen opens.
2. Select START.
3. Select Sign.
4. Select CONTINUE.

   The There’s one more thing to do screen opens.
5. Select Next.

   eSignature checks your setup for the Docusign browser extension and the Docusign PKI application. If you installed both resources on your computer, the Select a certificate screen opens.

   If you’re using your signer-held certificate with eSignature for the first time, the You need our PKI browser extension and PKI software screen opens. Install the Docusign browser extension and the Docusign PKI application. For more information about the installation process, visit the page that corresponds to your browser:
6. Select your signature certificate.
7. Select Next.

   The We’ll use this certificate screen opens with the name of the certificate that you selected.
8. Select Next.

   The We're completing your digital signature screen opens. Docusign shows the following message when eSignature has applied your digital signature to the document: Your digital signature is complete.

   Related information

   - [EU/EEA Trusted List Browser](https://eidas.ec.europa.eu/efda/tl-browser/#/screen/home)
   - [Accepted Trust Service Providers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=xww1594245808169&topicId=def1594657272301.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Sign a Document with Data Verification Address Auto-Suggestion Fields
heading_path: Sign a Document with Data Verification Address Auto-Suggestion Fields
text:
Learn about using address auto-suggestions to enter address fields easily and accurately
in Docusign envelopes and documents.

Your sender may use address auto-suggestion fields to easily and accurately
obtain addresses in Docusign envelopes and documents. With these
auto-suggestion address fields you can:

- Speed up the process of entering addresses
- Minimize errors

1. Begin signing the document as described in the [Sign a Document With Data
   Verification Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=vyg1698168078020.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to sign a document that has fields with data that must be verified before successfully signing the document.") article.
2. In the Address Line 1  field, enter the first field of
   the address block.

   ![A screenshot of the typing in Address Line 1 to begin the Auto-suggestion address process.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/dvsmartyautosugline1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.wUmBd2SFiyC5ntaSriIGUC1B7rfYG9vSB-zC1pAN-Ns&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the typing in Address Line 1 to begin the Auto-suggestion address process.")

   The Auto-Complete feature displays address options for the information that
   you entered.

   ![A screenshot of completed auto-complete address fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/dvsmartyautosugoptions.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.wUmBd2SFiyC5ntaSriIGUC1B7rfYG9vSB-zC1pAN-Ns&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of completed auto-complete address fields.")
3. Select the correct information from the suggestions.

   The Auto-Complete feature completes the remaining address fields.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/dvsmartyautosugcomplete.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzQsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.wUmBd2SFiyC5ntaSriIGUC1B7rfYG9vSB-zC1pAN-Ns&_LANG=enus)
   ![A screenshot of completed auto-complete address fields.]()

   Tip: Optionally select Undo
   autofill and enter your own address
   information.

   When you've completed your entry and the verification process completes, you
   received a verification confirmation message.

   `Verification succeeded`

   4.

   5. If you receive an Address Verification failed message, select Check
   Address to display a list of suggested addresses.
4. If you receive an `Address Not Found` message, select
   Continue
   to
   return to the address fields and try again.
5. If you receive an `Address verification failed. We found a better match
   to ensure deliverability` message, select Check Address
   to
   display a list of suggested addresses.
6. Select Confirm to accept the address.
7. Complete any other signing fields and select
   Finish.

title: Upload Signed Documents
text:
Learn how to upload documents you must print, sign, and return.

To upload your documents as the return method, follow these instructions. If you've
already downloaded the documents but quit or timed out before returning your
documents, download the documents again and return using these
instructions.

1. In the Print &Sign Download Document dialog box, select
   DOWNLOAD to save the document to your computer.
