Complete any other signing fields and finish signing as described in the [Complete the Signing Session](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=abm1573854992359.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete signing and optionally save or print a copy of your signed documents.") article.

title: Sign With the A-Trust Trust Service Provider (TSP) to Issue Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) in the European Union
heading_path: Sign With the A-Trust Trust Service Provider (TSP) to Issue Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) in the European Union
text:
Learn how to sign documents with the A-Trust Trust Service Provider (TSP). Use this tool
to issue Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) in the European Union.

title: Sign With the A-Trust Trust Service Provider (TSP) to Issue Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) in the European Union
heading_path: Sign With the A-Trust Trust Service Provider (TSP) to Issue Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) in the European Union
text:
To sign with A-Trust, you must have:

- A valid A-Trust account you can use to generate qualified signatures
- Installed the A-Trust mobile signature application

You can sign documents with the A-Trust Trust Service Provider (TSP) to issue
Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES) in the European Union.

You received an email prompting you to review and sign some documents. The envelope sender
requires you to use A-Trust to sign your documents. Signing with A-Trust creates a
Qualified Electronic Signature (QES).

Note: If you receive multiple envelopes containing documents to sign with
A-Trust, open and complete the envelopes one by one. Finish verifying your identity
and signing before you open the next envelope.

1. Select REVIEW DOCUMENT.

   The document to review and sign displays.
2. Select CONTINUE.

   You can now access the envelope.
3. Apply your signature to all the required SIGN
   tags.
4. Select CONTINUE.

   The Finish Signing screen opens.
5. Select NEXT.

   The A-Trust authentication screen opens.
6. Enter your A-Trust credentials.
7. Select Identify.
8. Follow the instructions on the A-Trust pages.

When A-Trust processes your signature, you receive a notification
that the signing is complete. You can log on to your Docusign account to save a copy of
the document.

title: Add Attachments to a Document to Provide Supporting Information
heading_path: Add Attachments to a Document to Provide Supporting Information
text:
Learn how to return requested documents to the sender as an attachment.

If the sender enables the Add Attachment feature for their documents, they can request that
you add additional supporting information to the document. These are the options for adding
attachments:

Add multiple attachments per field
:   If enabled, you can add multiple attachments to a Signer
    Attachment field.

Append attachments to the document with the attachment field
:   If enabled, when you upload an attachment, the document is inserted at the end of the
    envelope. Additionally, with this option, the attachment becomes a permanent part of the
    envelope. You cannot remove the attachment after uploading it. Also, if the envelope is
    ever copied, any signer attachments are included in the new envelope.

These attachments are uploaded to the Docusign system and shared with the sender and any
other signers or viewers on the document.

Attached items are converted to a PDF and must be under
50
MB in size. For more information on supported attachments and file sizes, see the [Supported File Formats](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980&topicId=xln1578456261162.html&_LANG=enus)
article.

Note: You cannot upload zipped or compressed files.

1. Select the Attachment icon.

   ![Screenshot of Attachment icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/newsigningattachmentfield1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQzOTYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.gQGfTN20u1DmALC_om_ON2ft4VMhCeqmPRabu7goTs0&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of Attachment icon.")
2. Select UPLOAD A FILE and select a file to attach.

   ![Screenshot of Upload a File.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signingattachment2.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQzOTYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.gQGfTN20u1DmALC_om_ON2ft4VMhCeqmPRabu7goTs0&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of Upload a File.")

   Note: You can attach a
   variety of file formats. For a full list, see the [Supported File Formats](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gbo1643332197980.ditamap&topicId=xln1578456261162.html&_LANG=enus) article.
3. Repeat for any additional files you want to attach.
4. When finished adding attachments, click DONE.

After you have uploaded the documents and completed all other tags, you can finish signing
as described in the [Complete the Signing Session](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=abm1573854992359.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to complete signing and optionally save or print a copy of your signed documents.") article.

title: Sign as a Delegated Signer
text:
Learn how to sign on behalf of another person to ensure that document signing can
continue uninterrupted when signers are unavailable to sign.

Your sender may enable the optional delegated signing feature. This feature ensures that
envelope completion continues uninterrupted when signers are out of the office, or
otherwise unavailable to sign.

As a delegate signing an agreement on another signer's behalf, you sign under your
own name and signature.

1. When you receive a notification to sign, select
   REVIEW DOCUMENT.
2. Agree to the disclosure statement that details you are signing on behalf of the
   delegator.

   Once you accept the disclosure statement, the signing session displays a
   notice that you are signing on behalf of the delegator as shown in this
   example.

   ```
   |  |  |
   | --- | --- |
   |  |  |
   |  | You are signing on behalf of Tamara Morgan |
   ```

   This helps distinguish between signing as a recipient and as a delegate.
3. After completing the signing, you receive any further status notifications,
   such as if the envelope is voided or when it is completed.

   If you do not sign, you do not receive any further notifications.

   When an envelope has a signer with an active delegate, delegate information is displayed in
   the envelope details. Anyone with access to the envelope can see the
   delegated information.

   ![A screenshot of Delegated Signer indicator on a document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signdelsigdetails.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MjksInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.pzMRqFZMZgRRkI5OZ33p-enGOcVLRWyc4fHhxp8A_uo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of Delegated Signer indicator on a document.")

title: Verify a Signature Request
text:
Learn how Docusign proactively combats fraudulent activity, by
flagging possibly risky envelopes and requesting that the signer verify the signing
request.

To proactively combat fraudulent activity, Docusign
scores the risk associated with envelopes. This risk is based on
the sender account and envelope characteristics.

If
an envelope’s risk score is too high, you may
receive
a request to verify that the sender and contents are legitimate by confirming:

- You recognize the sender
- You were expecting the envelope

1. Begin signing as described in the [Docusign eSignature Signing Process](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lou1573854977109.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Docusign eSignature signing process and eSignature signing terminology.")
   article.

   If it is determined that this envelope is possibly fraudulent, a message similar to
   this
   example displays:

   ![A screenshot of a sample signature verification request.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewmedrisksignotice.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQzNzAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.teTFADf30OwUj6fHDfqQkHlB6qoNDGu02-v01b3hIMo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample signature verification request.")
2. Select one of these actions:
   - Report Abuse if you don't recognize the sender or
     content. See the [Report Abuse or Fraudulent Activity](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=uec1690221505200.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Docusign Report Abuse feature that you can use to notify Docusign about instances of fraudulent activity.") article for more
     information.
   - Continue if you're expecting the document or are
     familiar with the request's content.

title: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Microsoft Edge
heading_path: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Microsoft Edge
text:
Follow these instructions to install the Docusign browser extension
and the PKI application for Microsoft Edge.

When you attempt to sign documents with your signer-held certificate, eSignature prompts you
to install the Docusign browser extension and the PKI application.
Follow these instructions to install the Docusign browser
extension and the PKI application for Microsoft Edge.

1. Select Install to open the Edge Add-ons store Docusign
    PKI page.
2. Select Get to display the Add Docusign
   PKI overlay.
3. Select Add extension.

   The following message displays: Docusign PKI has been added to Microsoft
   Edge.
4. Return to the You need our PKI browser extension and PKI
   software screen and select Next to display
   the Docusign PKI software installation screen.
5. Select Download to download the Docusign
   .exe file to your computer.
6. Run the Docusign.exe file and follow the
   Docusign PKI Setup wizard instructions.
7. Return to the Docusign PKI software installation screen
   and select Next to display the Select a
   certificate screen.

title: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Microsoft Edge
heading_path: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Microsoft Edge
text: Complete the signature process as described in the [Sign Documents With Signer-Held Digital Certificates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lpb1607560293943.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about signing documents using signer-held digital certificates.") article.

title: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Mozilla Firefox
heading_path: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Mozilla Firefox
text:
Follow these instructions to install the Docusign browser extension
and the PKI application for Mozilla Firefox.

When you attempt to sign documents with your signer-held certificate, eSignature prompts you
to install the Docusign browser extension and the PKI application.
Follow these instructions to install the Docusign browser
extension and the PKI application for Mozilla Firefox.

1. Select Install to install the add-on on your browser and
   open the Add Docusign PKI overlay.
2. Select Add.

   The following message
   displays:

   ```
   |  |  |
   | --- | --- |
   |  | Docusign PKI was added. |
   ```
3. Select OK.
4. Return to the You need our PKI browser extension and PKI
   software screen and select Next to open
   the Docusign PKI software installation screen.
5. Select Next to open the License
   Agreement screen.
6. Select the I accept the terms of the License Agreement
   checkbox and select Next to display the Choose
   Components screen.
7. Select Install to install and follow the steps to
   install the `Docusign PKI`  application on your computer and
   complete the installation.
8. Return to the Docusign PKI software installation screen
   and select Next to display the Select a
   certificate screen.

title: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Mozilla Firefox
heading_path: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Mozilla Firefox
text: Complete the signature process as described in the [Sign Documents With Signer-Held Digital Certificates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lpb1607560293943.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about signing documents using signer-held digital certificates.") article.

title: Additional Signing Actions
text:
Read about all signing options when signing Docusign agreements.

Docusign eSignature provides many other signing options:

- In the Finish menu, select the dropdown arrow to display the
  Other Actions options.

  ![A screenshot of the Signing Other Actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/sign-new-other-actions-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NDQsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.rXKd9ikd0gUlDsVLqcxQkyin7PgxPQ2WM438nthd-Mw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Signing Other Actions menu.")

  You can perform these signing actions on a document you're signing as
  described in these topics:

  - Finish later: Save your changes and complete signing at another time
  - [Change Signing Responsibility](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=uin1573855010064.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to assign a document to another person if you're not the right person to sign the document.")
  - [Decline To Sign an Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=fqo1573855017663.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how and when to decline to sign an envelope you review.")
  - [Print and Sign Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=wdr1573855014901.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use the Docusign Print & Sign option to return the document to the sender by upload or fax.")
- [Sign Through an SMS Text Notification](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=aas1646334384936.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about how to sign a document sent through a Docusign SMS text notification.")
- [Sign Part 11 Module Enabled Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=yzu1573855023673.html&_LANG=enus "Read about special requirements, including U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations, for signing Part 11 Module-enabled documents.")
- [Digital Signatures for Enhanced Agreement Security](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=hxl1594246065723.html&_LANG=enus "Read about how Docusign Digital Signatures meet industry and country standards for security.")
- [Sign Documents With Signer-Held Digital Certificates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lpb1607560293943.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about signing documents using signer-held digital certificates.")

title: Sign With the DigiCert QuoVadis Trust Service Provider to Issue Qualified Electronic Signatures in the Netherlands and the European Union
heading_path: Sign With the DigiCert QuoVadis Trust Service Provider to Issue Qualified Electronic Signatures in the Netherlands and the European Union
text:
Learn how to sign documents using Docusign and DigiCert QuoVadis
qualified electronic signature (QES) certificates in the Netherlands and the European
Union.

title: Sign With the DigiCert QuoVadis Trust Service Provider to Issue Qualified Electronic Signatures in the Netherlands and the European Union
heading_path: Sign With the DigiCert QuoVadis Trust Service Provider to Issue Qualified Electronic Signatures in the Netherlands and the European Union
text:
Before you begin, ensure that you completed the following prerequisites:

- You installed the Go>Sign Mobile™ application on your mobile
  phone.
- You registered with the DigiCert QuoVadis team, enrolled your DigiCert QuoVadis certificate,
  and registered your mobile phone. For more information, contact the DigiCert
  QuoVadis support team as described in the [digicert and
  QuoVadis](https://www.quovadissupport.nl/) page.

Use the DigiCert QuoVadis Trust Service Provider (TSP) to issue Qualified Electronic
Signatures (QES) in the Netherlands and the European Union.

You received a Docusign email prompting you to review and sign the
documents. If you have a DigiCert QuoVadis qualified electronic signature (QES)
certificate, a sender might require you to use it to sign documents and comply with
the Dutch government root of trust. For detailed information, see the [PKIoverheid](https://www.quovadisglobal.com/nl-en/#:~:text=DigiCert+QuoVadis PKIoverheid Certificates,for your needs.) page.

1. Select REVIEW DOCUMENT in the email informing you that
   you have a document to sign.

   The document to review displays in your Web browser.
2. Select CONTINUE.
3. Select the SIGN tags.
4. Select CONTINUE.

   The Finish Signing dialog box opens.
5. Select NEXT.

   ![Screenshot of Docusign DigiCert Finish Signing dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/DocuSign-DigiCert-Finish-Signing-dialog.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1MDUsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.QvWd0Rcy4MFLHjO8nZNz7V3TlWPv2_2KqrNkYeRnHgw&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of Docusign DigiCert Finish Signing dialog box.")

   The DigiCert QuoVadis authentication page opens.
6. Enter your email and password.

   ![Screenshot of the DigiCert log on page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/DigiCert-login-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1MDUsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.QvWd0Rcy4MFLHjO8nZNz7V3TlWPv2_2KqrNkYeRnHgw&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the DigiCert log on page.")
7. Select Login.

   If you logged on successfully, you receive a notification to the mobile phone you
   registered during the DigiCert QuoVadis registration process.
8. Select the DigiCert QuoVadis notification on your mobile phone.

   The Go>Sign Mobile application opens on your
   mobile phone.
9. Authorize DigiCert QuoVadis to sign your document as shown in the following
   examples.

   ![Screenshot of the Go>Sign Mobile application signing flow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/GoSign-Mobile-application-signing-flow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1MDUsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.QvWd0Rcy4MFLHjO8nZNz7V3TlWPv2_2KqrNkYeRnHgw&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Go>Sign Mobile application signing flow.")

The Go>Sign Mobile application signs your
document using your DigiCert QuoVadis certificate. When the application finishes
processing your signature, you get a notification that signing is complete. You can also
log on to Docusign to save a copy of the document to your
account.

title: Other Signing Actions
text:
Learn about the available options for signers from the Other Actions menu.

The Other Actions menu contains additional signing
actions available to you when signing documents in Docusign.
For example, if you run out of time to complete a document, you can elect to
finish later. Or you may want to decline to sign or assign the document to
someone else if:

- The document was sent to you by mistake
- The document is incorrect
- You're not the right signer

Note: Depending on the
settings the sender has enabled for your documents, some options are not
available.

Access the Other Action menu by selecting the arrow on the
Finish menu.

![A screenshot of a sample Other Actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/sign-new-other-actions-menu.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NDcsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.xoDgAPnyTTT0dp92qJ3wEkb0doYvYMTsxGv5DTKDbMo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample Other Actions menu.")

- Finish Later: Exit the signing process, saving any
  information you entered. Return to finish signing the document by using the
  link in the original email notification.

  ![A screenshot of a finish signing later message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signfinishlatermsg.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NDcsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.xoDgAPnyTTT0dp92qJ3wEkb0doYvYMTsxGv5DTKDbMo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a finish signing later message.")
- Assign to Someone Else: Reassign the signing
  responsibility to another person.
  - Provide the new signer’s name, email address, SMS information
    (telephone number), and a reason for the change.
  - The sender receives a change notification including the new
    signer's info and the reason for the change.
  - The new signer that you've identified is prompted to sign the
    document.

  Note: Add SMS
  information for the new signer if the SMS and phone authentication is
  required for your document, Otherwise, the new signer does not receive
  the document.
- Print & Sign: Print and sign the document on
  paper.
- Decline to Sign: Decline to sign the document.
  Optionally provide a message for the sender of the document indicating why
  you have declined to sign.

  When there are other signers, those who have
  already completed signing receive an email stating that you have
  declined to sign. Other signers who have not completed signing cannot
  access the documents.
- Void the Envelope You and other recipients receive
  an email notification that includes the reason for voiding the envelope.
  Recipients who have finished signing or whose turn it is to sign can still
  access the documents. A VOID  watermark displays on
  all pages and no further signing actions by anyone is possible. Any future
  recipients in the signing order will never receive any notification or be
  granted access to view or sign.

My
Document Menu

You can report abuse and
find support information by selecting the three-dot
menu.
to display the My Document
menu.

![A screenshot of accessing additional actions through the three-dot menu when you finish signing a document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/sign-new-3dot-menu-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NDcsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.xoDgAPnyTTT0dp92qJ3wEkb0doYvYMTsxGv5DTKDbMo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of accessing additional actions through the three-dot menu when you finish signing a document.")

title: Other Signing Actions
text:
NoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.xoDgAPnyTTT0dp92qJ3wEkb0doYvYMTsxGv5DTKDbMo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of accessing additional actions through the three-dot menu when you finish signing a document.")

- Report Abuse: Use the Report abuse wizard to report
  fraud or abuse to Docusign. You can indicate the report
  reason, and optionally be contacted by someone in Docusign to follow up.
- Help and Support: Opens the Docusign Support Center in a new browser window.
- About Docusign Opens the Docusign
  corporate site that has more information about Docusign
  and available features.
- View History: displays the sending and signing
  history for the documents.
- View Certificate (PDF): Opens a new browser window
  with a PDF version of the certificate of completion. This PDF includes
  information about the events, and the disclosure. The PDF can be saved or
  printed.
- View Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure:
  Displays the sender’s Electronic Record and Signature Disclosure.
- Session Information: Displays session-related
  details, such as the Envelope ID, Recipient ID, and the Signing
  Version.

title: Data Verification Issues for Signing
text:
Learn about issues that can occur when signing an agreement with Docusign Data Verification fields.

Here are some issues that can occur when signing agreements with Data Verification fields.

| Issue | Context | Solution |
| --- | --- | --- |
| You entered information in a Data Verification field, but you're not prompted to verify the information. | You may have quickly moved through multiple fields and the system is catching up. | Refresh the page.  Select Finish Later from the Finish menu [v] and try again. |
| You're confident that the information in the field is correct, but verification attempts fail. | The databases for the verification providers may have limitations. Or there may be formatting errors in your input. | Check the format of the data that you entered to ensure it meets field input requirements. For example:   - The phone number must start with a country code. - The email address must include proper email syntax, with an   email prefix and an email domain. |
| You're confident that the information in the field is incorrect, but verification is passed. | There may be verification provider's database limitations. | To learn more about the reason for each verification result, contact the sender. During Beta, sending accounts must contact [Docusign Support](https://support.docusign.com/s/contactSupport) for more information than is available in the envelope history. |
| A `Verification Failed` message displays with no explanation | Verification results may not be compatible with the signing process. | Ask the sender to void and resend the envelope. |
| You cannot finish signing the agreement because a Data Verification field failed validation. | The sender blocks signers from finishing the envelope unless they pass verification. | Ask the sender to void or resend the envelope with verification turned off. |

title: Sign With IDnow - EU Qualified to Issue GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures﻿
heading_path: Sign With IDnow - EU Qualified to Issue GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures﻿
text:
Learn how to sign documents with qualified electronic signatures (QES) using the IDnow
application.

title: Sign With IDnow - EU Qualified to Issue GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures﻿
heading_path: Sign With IDnow - EU Qualified to Issue GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures﻿
text:
If you want to verify your identity using IDnow VideoIdent, you must
comply with the following requirements:

- You have a valid ID as listed on the [Documents
  Supported by Docusign](https://go.idnow.de/docusign/documents) page
- You have a webcam available on your computer, tablet, or smartphone
- You have a mobile phone to receive the IDnow activation code by SMS

If you want to verify your identity using your German ID, you must comply with the
following requirements:

- You have a mobile phone with Near Field Communication (NFC) enabled
- Your German ID has the eID functionality enabled. You know the PIN associated
  with your electronic ID

Note:

IDnow Trust Service AB is now the Trust Service Provider (TSP) for video
identification and eID solutions. Previously, Docusign France
was the signer certificate. Today, IDnow Trust Service AB handles certificate
issuance.

A sender sent you a Docusign envelope containing documents to review and
sign with the IDnow Trust Service Provider (TSP). The sender requests that you
generate an eIDAS-compliant Qualified Electronic Signature (QES). They may also
request that it comply with GwG.

1. Select REVIEW DOCUMENT in the Docusign email.

   Docusign opens the envelope.
2. Select CONTINUE to access the envelope.
3. Select the START tag to begin the signature
   process.
4. Select all the required SIGN tags.
5. Select CONTINUE.

   If you have not created an IDnow account yet, a dialog box opens
   requesting your authorization for IDnow to read and apply digital
   signatures.
6. Select ACCEPT.

   Docusign redirects you to the
   Identification step of the IDnow identification
   service.
7. Select a device to verify your identity: Smartphone App
   or Computer and Webcam.

   If you selected Smartphone App, IDnow displays a
   QR code for you to scan using your smartphone. If already installed, the IDnow
   application opens on your mobile phone. Otherwise, IDnow lets you download and
   install it. As the application executes, IDnow lets you choose between the
   following identification methods: Video call or
   eID.

   If you selected Computer and
   Webcam, IDnow redirects you to its web application. IDnow
   will use Video call to verify your identity.

   Note: select Smartphone App
   if you want to identify using your German ID.
8. Select an identification method: Video call or
   eID.

When you select Video Call, an agent will
guide you through the verification process. If you choose the eID
flow, the IDnow application prompts you to scan your eID and submit the associated PIN.
Next, you must place your mobile phone over your German ID to enable NFC-chip
scanning.

Once the IDnow application or the agent has verified your identity,
IDnow generates a qualified electronic signature. Docusign
receives the signature and appends it to the document. The “Your digital signature
is complete” screen opens.

title: Sign With IDnow - EU Qualified to Issue GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures﻿
heading_path: Sign With IDnow - EU Qualified to Issue GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures﻿
text:
If the sender requests that your signature complies with GwG, IDnow creates a KYC
(Know Your Customer) file. You can retrieve this file using the Docusign eSignature
API or the IDnow API within 90 days. For more information, read the [IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=ukt1742396525044.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about GwG, its main requirements, and how IDnow integrates with Docusign to provide compliant, qualified electronic and digital signatures.") help
topic.

title: There was an unexpected fault (I get an error while using My SAT certificate )
heading_path: There was an unexpected fault (I get an error while using My SAT certificate )
text:
Situations that cause the There was an unexpected fault error
message and how to solve them.

The error message There was an unexpected fault displays after I submit
my SAT certificate and private key for signing documents.

title: There was an unexpected fault (I get an error while using My SAT certificate )
heading_path: There was an unexpected fault (I get an error while using My SAT certificate )
text:
- The certificate is expired.
- The certificate is revoked.
- The network encountered an issue.
- The uploaded file is not supported.

title: There was an unexpected fault (I get an error while using My SAT certificate )
heading_path: There was an unexpected fault (I get an error while using My SAT certificate )
text:
1. Ensure that your internet connection is working properly.
2. Ensure you submitted a .cer file for your SAT certificate and
   the`.key` file for the private key.
3. Open your certificate and check the following details:

   - The end date is still valid.
   - The certificate is not revoked.

   If the details of your certificate contain inaccurate or expired
   information, contact the department that provided you with your
   certificate to create a new one.

title: Supported Languages for Signing
text:
Discover which languages Docusign supports for signing documents in
localized languages.

Docusign offers users the ability to sign documents in localized
languages. Languages are detected through browser settings, and document senders can
also configure language settings accordingly. See the Extensive language support
for signing section in the [Global Business](https://www.docusign.com/products/electronic-signature/learn/global) article to view the available
languages for signing

title: Sign a Document With Supplements
text:
Learn about working with supplemental information that you review before
signing.

title: Sign a Document With Supplements
text:
While signing a document, the document may include supplements.

Supplements contain additional information and are included with your document for
your acknowledgment.

![A screenshot of a document with a supplement.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signsuppliment.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MTMsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.pa-0QZ4ManwbyCRW09wDu_sFF-rffOOb5vZuBxMjhdM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a document with a supplement.")

Supplements can be (but are not restricted to):

- Terms and conditions
- Disclosures
- Financial statements

title: System Requirements for Signing
text:
Review these minimum signing system requirements. These include signing online, signing with mobile devices, signing with assistive technologies, and Docusign Notary signing.

Modern computers work well with Docusign eSignature and must adher to these requirements

title: System Requirements for Signing
text:
- Windows 10 and newer
- macOS 10.13 (High Sierra) and newer
- Android 10 and newer
- iOS 14 and newer

title: System Requirements for Signing
text:
Docusign supports the latest stable release (except where noted) of these browsers:

- Chrome
- Chrome for Android
- Firefox
- Microsoft Edge
- Safari
- Safari for iOS

Note: Prerelease (such as Beta) versions of operating systems and browsers are not supported.

title: System Requirements for Signing
text:
- Recommended: 1400x900 or higher
- Minimum: 1366x768

title: System Requirements for Signing
text:
- Enable per-session cookies.
- Enable JavaScript
- Users access the internet through a Proxy Server must enable HTTP 1.1 settings through a proxy connection.
- Users access to the server through the firewall setting `https://docucdn-a.akamaihd.net`. Docusign uses Akamai as a content delivery service to enhance our application's performance.

title: System Requirements for Signing
text:
- Acrobat Reader or similar software might be required to view PDF files.
- The minimum required PDF version that your PDF reader must be able to open is PDF 1.5.

  For detailed information about PDF versions, see this [PDF versions](https://www.prepressure.com/pdf/basics/version) article.

Note: When uploading PDF/A files, we recommend using PDF version 1.5 to ensure that the files maintain their original appearance. For information about PDF/A files, see the [PDF/A file format](https://www.prepressure.com/pdf/basics/pdfa) page.

title: System Requirements for Signing
text:
- The latest Apple iOS version
- The latest Android version

title: System Requirements for Signing
text:
Docusign Notary remote signing has additional requirements as well as the general signing requirements.

Note: See the  [Docusign Notary Signing Guide](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=zle1621013049545&topicId=lap1604000866404.html&_LANG=enus)  for detailed information about signing documents with a remote Docusignonline notary.

- Devices: A laptop, desktop, or mobile device with a working camera and microphone

  Note: For the best results, Notaries and signers should use a laptop or desktop device. Signers can optionally use mobile devices.
- Browsers
  - Windows
    - Google Chrome current version
    - Firefox current version
    - Microsoft Edge current version for signers
  - macOS
    - Safari (macOS only) 12 +
    - Google Chrome current version
    - Firefox current version
    - Microsoft Edge current version
  - Mobile device: signers only; notary users are not supported
    - Apple iOS 11 +
    - Android 5.0 +
- Internet connection
  - User internet access broadband service must support a live, real-time audio and video connection. For the best results, we recommend that you hard-wire your device to the internet.
  - We recommend the following minimum internet speeds. Test your internet speed at [Speedtest.net](https://www.speedtest.net/).
    - Upload: 5 Mbps
    - Download: 5 Mbps

Validate that your system meets all requirements at the [Pre-call Test](https://tokbox.com/developer/tools/precall/) page.

title: System Requirements for Signing
text:
With Docusign, you can use different assistive technologies to ensure that all users are able to sign our documents. You can sign documents in Docusign using:

- Screen readers that permit visually impaired users to follow the necessary guidelines to sign an envelope
- Keyboard-only use to make sure that any action can only be executed using this device

For specific information, see [Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#new-features-in-wcag-2-2).

title: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Google Chrome
heading_path: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Google Chrome
text:
Follow these instructions to install the Docusign browser extension and the PKI
application for Google Chrome.

When you attempt to sign documents with your signer-held certificate, eSignature prompts you
to install the Docusign browser extension and the PKI application.
Follow these instructions described in the [Sign Documents With Signer-Held Digital Certificates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lpb1607560293943.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about signing documents using signer-held digital certificates.") article to install the Docusign browser extension and the PKI application for Google
Chrome.

1. Select Install.
2. Select Add to Chrome to open the Add Docusign
   PKI overlay.
3. Select Add extension.

   The following message displays: `Docusign PKI has been added to
   Chrome`.
4. Return to the You need our PKI browser extension and PKI
   software screen and select Next to display
   the Docusign PKI software installation screen.
5. Select Download to download the
   DocusignPKI.exe file on your computer.
6. Run the DocusignPKI.exe file and follow the
   Docusign PKI Setup wizard steps to install the
   Docusign PKI application on your computer.
7. Return to the Docusign PKI software installation screen
   and select Next.

   The Select a certificate screen opens.

title: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Google Chrome
heading_path: Install the Docusign Browser Extension and PKI Application on Google Chrome
text: Complete the signature process as described in the [Sign Documents With Signer-Held Digital Certificates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lpb1607560293943.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about signing documents using signer-held digital certificates.") article.

title: Why Can't I Change My Signature?
heading_path: Why Can't I Change My Signature?
text:
Read about situations where you can't change your signature.

There is certain scenario where you cannot change your signature. Follow these steps to
determine why you can't change your signature:

- Make sure you have an active Docusign account. If you do not have an
  account at the time of signing, you cannot change your selected signature. See the [Sign Documents for Free](https://www.docusign.com/esignature/sign-documents-free) page to learn about getting a Docusign account.
- Check with your account administrator to ensure that your recipient name is not locked.
  Some senders lock recipient names.
- When you self-sign documents using the Sign a Document feature,
  you cannot select a saved signature. This occurs when you self-sign documents and do not
  send envelopes to other signers.

  To change your signature for a document you are
  self-signing, you must send yourself an envelope and add yourself as the only
  recipient. Using this method, you can optionally select another saved signature.
- Make sure you are not trying to change your signature when:
  - Using Docusign Mobile App for Android or Mobile App for iOS.
  - Using in-person signing as described in the [In person signing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gav1643676262430&topicId=hak1578456316043.html&_LANG=enus) article/
  - There are digital or universal signatures. See the [Digital Signatures/Universal Signatures](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Electronic-and-Digital-Signatures) article (for
    situations described in the [Sign Part 11 Module Enabled Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=yzu1573855023673.html&_LANG=enus "Read about special requirements, including U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) regulations, for signing Part 11 Module-enabled documents.") article.)
  - The document you're signing originated from an account on another server (cross
    site).
  - The document was signed and returned.

title: Search for Text in a Document
heading_path: Search for Text in a Document
text: Learn how to use the Search feature to quickly locate specific keywords or phrases in a document during a signing session.

title: Search for Text in a Document
heading_path: Search for Text in a Document
text:
You must use a desktop device set to English to search envelopes with 10 or fewer documents and 100 or fewer pages. This feature is disabled when Universal Signatures are enabled for the envelope. Image-only documents are not searchable.

You can use the Search feature to find exact text matches within a document. This allows you to quickly navigate to relevant sections without scrolling through the entire file.

1. During a signing session, open the Search tool using one of the following methods:
   1. Select the Search icon (magnifying glass) in the right-hand vertical toolbar.
   2. On Windows, select Ctrl+F**.**
   3. On Mac, select Cmd+F.
2. Enter a search term containing two or more characters.

   All exact matches are highlighted in the document.![The screenshot shows the Search tool and highlighted keywords in a document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/Searchexample.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQzNTQsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9._cH1IlD9h9BxbJhRgPz18V2y3x8QE4mUTNFw5WJp2C0&_LANG=enus "The screenshot shows the Search tool and highlighted keywords in a document.")
3. Use the Up and Down arrows in the search panel to move between matches.

You have located the specific text within the document.

title: Report Abuse or Fraudulent Activity
text:
Learn about the Docusign
Report Abuse feature that you can use to notify Docusign about instances of fraudulent activity.

The Report Abuse feature reports fraudulent emails or abuse
directly to Docusign.

1. Choose Other Actions to access the Report Abuse
   feature.
   - From the Review and complete page within the
     document you're signing, select the vertical ellipsis and select
     Report Abuse

     ![A screenshot of the Docusign Other Action menu Report Abuse menu option.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewselrptabuse.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NjYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.EjRA7UTebVcVK3HcOD1LA-wAml8WMTYNK53Hf2QdbgQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Other Action menu Report Abuse menu option.")
   - From the Initial page, select Other Actions > Report Abuse > Continue.

     Note: This option is only available if
     your sender implements this feature in this screen.

     ![A screenshot of the Docusign Other Options menu for an envelope you received.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signinitscreenselect.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NjYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.EjRA7UTebVcVK3HcOD1LA-wAml8WMTYNK53Hf2QdbgQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign Other Options menu for an envelope you received.")
2. Select one of the following Report Abuse options:

   ![A screenshot of the Report Abuse options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewreportabusereason.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NjYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.EjRA7UTebVcVK3HcOD1LA-wAml8WMTYNK53Hf2QdbgQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Report Abuse options.")
   - I know the sender and want them to stop contacting
     me.
   - I don't know the sender and think this is
     spam.
   - I believe this is fraudulent or contains illegal
     content.
3. Add details in the Comment section.
4. Optionally, select a follow-up action and select SEND ABUSE
   REPORT.

   The following example shows a completed fraud report.

   ![A screenshot of a completed Docusign envelope fraud report.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signlbrptabcompletedb.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NjYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.EjRA7UTebVcVK3HcOD1LA-wAml8WMTYNK53Hf2QdbgQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a completed Docusign envelope fraud report.")
5. If you select the I believe this is fraudulent or contains illegal
   content option:
   1. Select Start to access the New
      Case page.

      ![A screenshot of the Docusign New Case page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/newcasereport.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NjYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.EjRA7UTebVcVK3HcOD1LA-wAml8WMTYNK53Hf2QdbgQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Docusign New Case page.")
   2. Select Accept and complete the relevant New Case
      Incident report fields.
6. Select Submit.

You receive a confirmation message that your report was sent to Docusign.![A screenshot of the report abuse confirmation message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/reportconfirmation.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NjYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.EjRA7UTebVcVK3HcOD1LA-wAml8WMTYNK53Hf2QdbgQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the report abuse confirmation message.")

title: Change Signing Responsibility
text:
Learn how to assign a document to another person if you're not the right person to sign
the document.

If you’re not the right person to sign a document, you can assign someone else. For
example, you transfer to a different position and no longer have signing responsibility for
certain types of documents.

The new signer can be anyone, provided that you know their email address.

The sender's security and account settings affect the email reassignment:

- Depending on the settings the sender has enabled for your documents, this option might
  not be available
- If the sender's account settings require the signer to log in to access envelopes, the
  new assignee's email address must exactly match their user name.
- Depending on the sender's security settings, you may receive an error message if the
  email
  address
  is
  incorrect, or not associated with a Docusign user.

You cannot change signing responsibility if:

- The change signing responsibility setting is disabled by the sender
- You are not a valid recipient type for reassigning the envelope
- The new recipient is not assigned to any Docusign account. However, an
  account is required by a custom sending setting
- The new recipient already exists in the same routing order

1. After opening the documents, in the top menu, select OTHER ACTIONS >  Assign to Someone Else.

   ![A screenshot of the Assign to Someone Else in the Other Actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewassignsomeoneelse.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NjEsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.yFq3RHe7Po1eL4CR4FVH6vq808bmjyKWWVLflFgTpJg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Assign to Someone Else in the Other Actions menu.")
2. Enter the new signer's email address, name, and a reason for changing the signing
   responsibility.
3. When you're finished, select ASSIGN TO SOMEONE ELSE.

All the fields assigned to you are now assigned to the new
signer. Any fields you completed before you changed the signing responsibility are reset. The
sender and the new signer receive a notification about the change. You become a Carbon Copy
(CC) recipient of the envelope documents. The notification to the new signer looks like
this:![A screenshot of the email notification to the new signer.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/nottonewsigner.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NjEsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.yFq3RHe7Po1eL4CR4FVH6vq808bmjyKWWVLflFgTpJg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the email notification to the new signer.")

title: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
heading_path: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
text:
Learn about GwG, its main requirements, and how IDnow integrates with Docusign to
provide compliant, qualified electronic and digital signatures.

Docusign partners with IDnow to provide GwG and eIDAS-compliant
Qualified Electronic Signatures (QES). Signers can use the IDnow VideoIdent or eID
service to verify their identity. If the verification is successful, the IDnow Trust
Service Provider generates a qualified signature and sends it to Docusign. When customers request a GwG-compliant signature, they
initiate the creation of a KYC (Know Your Customer) file. IDnow creates the evidence
file for the KYC process.

title: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
heading_path: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
Docusign and IDnow for GwG compliant signatures
text:
BaFin stands for Bundesanstalt für Finanzdienstleistungsaufsicht, the Federal
Financial Supervisory Authority in Germany. BaFin enforces the German Anti-Money
Laundering Act. GwG stands for Geldwäschegesetz and outlines strict rules for
financial institutions. These rules address customer due diligence, risk assessment,
and the detection of money laundering and terrorist financing. Noncompliance with
these regulations can trigger significant fines.

Docusign offers GwG-compliant remote identification through IDnow. This
solution supports a Qualified Electronic Signature (QES) that meets strict security
standards. IDnow gathers the required Know Your Customer (KYC) data and ensures
authenticity through video identification (VideoIdent) or electronic ID (eID)
methods.

Clients who use Docusign and IDnow benefit from a consistent signing
experience. Docusign creates a secure workflow, and IDnow verifies
the identity. If the verification is successful, IDnow generates a qualified
signature and sends it to Docusign, which appends it to the
documents. Next, IDnow generates an evidence file for the KYC process. This approach
satisfies GwG's requirements for strong customer due diligence. It also helps
mitigate the risk of identity fraud and enables you to meet Anti-Money Laundering
(AML) and KYC obligations.

title: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
heading_path: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
Enabling the IDnow Trust Service Provider
text:
To add IDnow GwG compliant and QES signatures to your agreements, you must add the IDnow EU
Qualified signature type to your account. For more information, contact your account
representative at Docusign.

title: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
heading_path: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
text:
When customers request GwG-compliant signatures, IDnow creates a KYC (Know Your
Customer) evidence file. By default, customers who added the IDnow TSP to their Docusign account after February 27th, 2026, can ask their
integrators or API administrators to retrieve this evidence file using the Docusign eSignature API. If they added the IDnow TSP before that
date, integrators must use the IDnow API to retrieve the KYC file. The KYC file is
available for 90 days.

Note: Contact your Docusign representative for more information on how to use the Docusign or the IDnow API to retrieve the KYC file. Contact them also if
you want to change the way you retrieve the KYC evidence file and use either the Docusign eSignature API or the IDnow API.

Important: It is the customer's responsibility to
maintain a record of the KYC data in accordance with the German Anti-Money
Laundering Act (GwG).

title: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
heading_path: IDnow GwG Compliant and Qualified Electronic Signatures
Transition to Swedish TSP and removal of SMS OTP requirement
text:
IDnow Trust Service AB is now the Trust Service Provider (TSP) for video identification and
eID solutions. Previously, Docusign France was the signer
certificate. Today, IDnow Trust Service AB handles certificate issuance. This change in TSP also resulted in an updated signing experience where the SMS OTP (one-time password) is no longer required at the end of the process. This optimization is part of the new workflow and is compliant with QES regulations.

title: Sign With the UAE PASS
text:
Learn how to sign documents with the UAE PASS Trust Service Provider (TSP). Use this
tool to issue signatures that are compliant with the UAE electronic transactions and trust
services law.

title: Sign With the UAE PASS
text:
To sign with UAE PASS, make sure you comply with the following
requirements:

- Verify your identity through the UAE PASS mobile application
- Use a device whose operating system is Microsoft Windows, iOS 16.x or newer, or Android 8.0
  or newer.

  Note: Enable background application refresh on
  your mobile phone to update the UAE PASS application even when you’re not
  using it.
- Use the Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, or Mozilla Firefox browser
- Use distinct devices for authentication and signing

You received an email prompting you to review and sign documents. The envelope sender
requires you to use UAE PASS to sign your documents.

1. Select REVIEW DOCUMENT in the Docusign email.

   Docusign redirects you to the document to review.
2. Select CONTINUE.

   You can now access the envelope.
3. Apply your signature to all the required SIGN
   tags.
4. Select CONTINUE.

   Docusign opens the Finish Signing dialog
   box.
5. Select NEXT.
6. Select Allow Access.

   Docusign opens the UAE PASS authentication dialog box.
7. Enter your UAE PASS credential.
8. Select Login.
9. Follow the instructions in the UAE PASS mobile application.

When UAE PASS finishes processing your signature, you get notified
that the signing is complete. You can log in to Docusign to save a
copy of the document to your Docusign account.

title: Get Started on the Docusign Signing Welcome Page
heading_path: Get Started on the Docusign Signing Welcome Page
text:
Learn about the Docusign signing welcome screen and signing your
documents

The welcome page includes a message from the sender and a prompt to review and act on the
documents you received.

![A screenshot of the Review and Act on These Documents prompt.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/sign-review-other-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQzNDQsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.WrAs043YNIV4pTbcCGYMkP_SGxBxuJm1UA95sl9W_Zc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Review and Act on These Documents prompt.")

1. If available, select the Electronic Record and Signature disclosure checkbox. Selecting
   this checkbox indicate you agree to the disclosure. The system starts the signing
   process.
2. Select CONTINUE to open and review the document.

   Note: We recommend that you review the document by scrolling. Or, you can
   also use the page thumbnails in the right-side navigation before signing.
3. Optionally, you can use the OTHER ACTIONS menu to select a
   different option for the documents.

   See the [Other Signing Actions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=qdy1573854987499.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the available options for signers from the Other Actions menu.") article for information about these
   options.
4. After you have added the required information and signing the documents, the actions move
   to the bottom of the browser. FINISH becomes active.

   Select
   FINISH to complete signing. The success message
   displays.

   ![A screenshot of the successfully signed agreement message.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signsuccess.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQzNDQsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.WrAs043YNIV4pTbcCGYMkP_SGxBxuJm1UA95sl9W_Zc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the successfully signed agreement message.")

title: Sign a Document With Data Verification Fields
heading_path: Sign a Document With Data Verification Fields
text:
Learn how to sign a document that has fields with data that must be verified before
successfully signing the document.

With Data Verification Fields in a Docusign document, the verified
data is authenticated against data sources in real-time. For example, when completing an
employment application, you must verify your email address and phone number.

When you complete the signing field entries, the information you enter is authenticated
by a third-party application. Upon verification, you can complete the signing.

Note: Your organization may create custom Data Verification Fields
specific to your company. See the [Data Verification Field Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qki1698158012210&topicId=omw1740164520691.html&_LANG=enus) article for details.

1. Open the document as described in the [Get Started on the Docusign Welcome Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=uwx1573854978359.html&_LANG=enus) article.
2. Fields that require verification are indicated by a tool tip when you hover over
   them, as shown in the following example:

   ![An example of a tool tip for a data field that must be verified when signing a document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/dvreqfieldtip.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzIsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.wwCjF1Qm01teVdziY3ZQPR8Lz4D5CD4APJatVhakWKM&_LANG=enus "An example of a tool tip for a data field that must be verified when signing a document.")
3. Enter the information and go to the next field.

   Note: If you're entering a phone number, you are
   required to include both the country code and phone number.

   While validating, the system displays the following message:

   `Verifying data`
4. If you correctly entered the information, you received a verification confirmation
   message.

   `Verification succeeded`
5. If you incorrectly entered the information, you receive a failed verification
   message:

   `Verification failed`

   Review and reenter your information.
6. Continue entering information in any subsequent fields.

   ![A screenshot of a sample document with Data Verification Fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signing-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzIsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.wwCjF1Qm01teVdziY3ZQPR8Lz4D5CD4APJatVhakWKM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample document with Data Verification Fields.")
7. Select Save to complete the signing process.
8. To review the audit trail details about the agreements you signed, select the
   vertical three-dot menu and select View History to view the
   envelope history or View Certificate to view the Certificate
   of Completion.

   ![A screenshot of the Other Actions menu audit trail options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewviewcert.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0MzIsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.wwCjF1Qm01teVdziY3ZQPR8Lz4D5CD4APJatVhakWKM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Other Actions menu audit trail options.")

   See these articles for detailed audit information:

   - [Envelope history](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qki1698158012210&topicId=uro1698158848949.html): Includes a list of sender and
     recipient activities to date
   - [Certificate of Completion](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qki1698158012210&topicId=qjs1701290377826.html): Includes the identifying
     information about the envelope and complete details of the envelope events

title: Sign a Document With Data Verification Fields
heading_path: Sign a Document With Data Verification Fields
text:
Learn how to sign a document that has fields with data that must be verified before
successfully signing the document.

With Data Verification Fields in a Docusign document, the verified data is
authenticated against data sources in real-time. For example, when completing an
employment application, you must verify your email address and phone number.

When you complete the signing field entries, the information you enter is
authenticated by a third-party application. Upon verification, you can complete the
signing.

Note: Your organization may create custom Data Verification Fields specific
to your company. See the [Data Verification
Field Types](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qki1698158012210&topicId=omw1740164520691.html&_LANG=enus) article for details.

1. Open the document as described in the [Get Started on the Docusign Signing Welcome Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=uwx1573854978359.html&_LANG=enus) article.
2. Fields that require verification are indicated by a tool tip when you hover
   over them, as shown in the following example:

   ![An example of a tool tip for a data field that must be verified when signing a document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/dvreqfieldtip.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDcyNzYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.UMwrSDPPuyxf0ftwomJeENNvBcFYPGCb3HwSN9jQQcc&_LANG=enus "An example of a tool tip for a data field that must be verified when signing a document.")
3. Enter the information and go to the next field.

   Note: If you're entering a phone number, you
   are required to include both the country code and phone number.

   While validating, the system displays the following message:

   `Verifying data`
4. If you correctly entered the information, you received a verification
   confirmation message.

   `Verification succeeded`
5. If you incorrectly entered the information, you receive a failed verification
   message:

   `Verification failed`

   Review and reenter your information.
6. Continue entering information in any subsequent fields.

   ![A screenshot of a sample document with Data Verification Fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signing-example.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDcyNzYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.UMwrSDPPuyxf0ftwomJeENNvBcFYPGCb3HwSN9jQQcc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a sample document with Data Verification Fields.")
7. Select Finish to complete the signing process.
8. To review the audit trail details about the agreements you signed, select the
   vertical three-dot menu and select View History to view
   the envelope history or View Certificate to view the
   Certificate of Completion.

   ![A screenshot of the Other Actions menu audit trail options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signnewviewcert.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MDcyNzYsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.UMwrSDPPuyxf0ftwomJeENNvBcFYPGCb3HwSN9jQQcc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Other Actions menu audit trail options.")

   See these articles for detailed audit information:

   - [Envelope history](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qki1698158012210&topicId=uro1698158848949.html): Includes a list of sender
     and recipient activities to date
   - [Certificate of Completion](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qki1698158012210&topicId=qjs1701290377826.html): Includes the
     identifying information about the envelope and complete details of the
     envelope events

title: Print and Sign Documents
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign Print & Sign option to return the document to the
sender by upload or fax.

Complete the entire print and sign task in a single session. If you quit in the middle, you
must start at the beginning the next time you attempt to print and sign.

Print and sign your documents

1. From the document you are signing, open the menu OTHER ACTIONS.
2. Select PRINT & SIGN.

   Note:

   PRINT & SIGN is only available if the sender turns
   on this feature. If the  PRINT & SIGN option is not
   available in the Other Actions menu, select FINISH
   LATER. Contact the sender and ask to resend the envelope with the
   Print & Sign option enabled.

   If you see Print & Sign: Download Document, the
   document's sender doesn't support return by FAX.

If the sender supports documents to be returned by either upload or fax, this Print
& Sign dialog box displays. You can choose how you return the signed documents.

![A screenshot of the Print & Sign dialog box.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/newsigningprintupload.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NTAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.CQkuC2OmbaiU7k22HcK2nYyyGXi8gfPkZsVTNImFaok&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Print & Sign dialog box.")

Choose between the Upload or Fax return options:

- Prepare your documents for upload.
- Ensure all required fields are completed.
- Scan the signed documents.
- Upload the scanned documents to the designated platform.

You can sign the documents electronically anytime by selecting
CANCEL. Selecting CANCEL brings you back to
the electronic signing.

See these articles to return signed document

- [Upload Signed Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=ctc1573855014359.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upload documents you must print, sign, and return.")
- [Fax Your Documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=ivg1573855015484.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to select Fax the Docusign signing Other Actions menu.")

title: Sign With Singpass
text:
Learn how to sign a document with Docusign and a Singaporean digital ID from
Singpass.

If you’re eligible for a digital ID from [Singpass](https://www.singpass.gov.sg/),
a sender might require you to use one to sign a document. You must switch between Docusign in your browser and the Singpass mobile app. You should
have:

- An iOS mobile device (version 11.0 or higher)
- The Singpass app installed and set up on your mobile device

1. In the notification that you have a document to sign, select REVIEW
   DOCUMENT.
2. Sign the document by following the steps in the [Docusign eSignature: How To Sign a Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-sign-a-DocuSign-document-Basic-Signing) video,
   then select CONTINUE.
3. You’re prompted to finish signing with your digital ID by logging in to the
   Singpass mobile app. Select CONTINUE.

   ![Screenshot of the Docusign prompt to finish signing with Singpass.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/singpass1finishsigning.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1MDAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.dmu9C2l4GeX8iaFe10svDeKcV9URwz1aQcgSxRmspgM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Docusign prompt to finish signing with Singpass.")
4. A reference code and a QR code display. Open the Singpass mobile app on your
   phone, where you enter these codes.

   ![Screenshot of the Singpass mobile app reference code and QR code.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/singpass2qrcode.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1MDAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.dmu9C2l4GeX8iaFe10svDeKcV9URwz1aQcgSxRmspgM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Singpass mobile app reference code and QR code.")

   Note: The QR code is only
   valid for a short time (less than two minutes). If you don’t scan the QR
   code with Singpass right away, you might receive an error. You must then
   request a new QR code. If this happens, close the window on your computer
   that contains the QR code. Upon return to the document in the Docusign app, select Continue to
   start the process of connecting to Singpass again. A new QR code for you to
   scan displays.
5. In the Singpass mobile app under SAFEENTRY CHECK-IN,
   select Scan QR code.

   ![Screenshot of selecting the QR code scan location in the Singpass app.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/singpass3singpassapp1.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1MDAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.dmu9C2l4GeX8iaFe10svDeKcV9URwz1aQcgSxRmspgM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of selecting the QR code scan location in the Singpass app.")
6. Scan the QR code that displays on your computer screen with the app.
7. A reference code displays in the Singpass mobile app. To check that you’re
   signing the right document, make sure that this reference code matches the one
   from the Docusign app, and select
   Sign.

   ![Screenshot of selecting Sign after confirming that the Signpass reference codes match.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/singpass4appsignscreen.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1MDAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.dmu9C2l4GeX8iaFe10svDeKcV9URwz1aQcgSxRmspgM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of selecting Sign after confirming that the Signpass reference codes match.")
8. Authenticate with Singpass by using your fingerprint or facial recognition, or
   by entering your Singpass passcode.

title: Sign With Singpass
text:
t of selecting Sign after confirming that the Signpass reference codes match.")
8. Authenticate with Singpass by using your fingerprint or facial recognition, or
   by entering your Singpass passcode.

   ![Screenshot of the waiting screen in the Singpass app.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/singpass5singpassappfinalscreen.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1MDAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.dmu9C2l4GeX8iaFe10svDeKcV9URwz1aQcgSxRmspgM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the waiting screen in the Singpass app.")

   ![Screenshot of the Docusign confirmation page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/singpass6done.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ1MDAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.dmu9C2l4GeX8iaFe10svDeKcV9URwz1aQcgSxRmspgM&_LANG=enus "Screenshot of the Docusign confirmation page.")

   Important: The Docusign and Singpass apps are temporarily in waiting mode
   for up to a minute while you complete steps in the opposite app. Then the
   system processes and applies your signature. Don’t close either app until
   you see a notification that signing is complete.

The Docusign app displays a
Finalizing signature message. When the system finishes
processing your signature, you receive a notification that signing is complete in the
Docusign and Singpass apps. You can also log on to Docusign to save a copy of the document to your Docusign account.

Additional
resources

If you need help with signing with Singpass:

- Check the FAQs [Singpass Frequently Asked Questions](https://www.singpass.gov.sg/main/html/faq.html) page
- Contact Docusign Support as described in the [How do I contact Docusign Customer
  Support?](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-contact-DocuSign-Customer-Support) page
- Contact Singpass Support as described in the [Contact Us](https://www.singpass.gov.sg/home/ui/contact-us) page

title: Why Do I Keep Getting Email Reminders to Sign with Docusign ?
heading_path: Why Do I Keep Getting Email Reminders to Sign with Docusign?
text:
Choose one of these options to discontinue repeat email reminders to sign a
document.

Docusign sends email notifications on behalf of senders. Each sender sets how often to send
email reminders.

Select one of these options to stop receiving email reminders to sign:

- Decline to sign, as described int the [Decline To Sign an Envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=fqo1573855017663.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how and when to decline to sign an envelope you review.") article.
- Sign the document as described in the [Docusign eSignature Signing Process](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=lou1573854977109.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the Docusign eSignature signing process and eSignature signing terminology.") article.
- Contact the sender by replying to the email notification and ask them to void or cancel the
  document

title: Select Your Default eSignature Account
text:
If you have multiple Docusign eSignature accounts associated with the same email
address, learn how to designate a default account to receive envelopes and the associated
documents.

If you have multiple Docusign eSignature accounts associated with the same email address, you
can designate a default account to receive envelopes and the associated documents.

You can now change envelope routing to a designated account that you can easily monitor.

1. Log on to your preferred eSignature account.
2. If you have multiple accounts, make sure you are logged on to the one you want
   for your default. To switch to a different account, select your profile image
   and select Switch Account.
3. In the account you want as your default, select your profile image and select
   My Preferences.
4. In General Settings, select Your Default
   eSignature Account.
5. To ensure consistent envelope routing select Set as
   default twice.

Any envelopes you receive now go to default account.

Note: If you are a recipient in an envelope you sent, you receive
that envelope in the sending account. Disregard the default account.

title: Sign Part 11 Module Enabled Documents
text:
Read about special requirements, including U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA)
regulations, for signing Part 11 Module-enabled documents.

As a signer, if you receive an email that has the
notation Part 11 Module, there are some special requirements when
signing these documents. The signing process must follow regulations from the U.S. Food and
Drug Administration (FDA).

![A screenshot of the Part 11 Module enabled document notation.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signpart11.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NzAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.1zeO2xOxVSUEpJhrMiaZesZUUboeqSREEuO0cU8BClU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Part 11 Module enabled document notation.")

You might authenticate when:

- You open the document and finish signing
- The sender requires you to authenticate to open the document
- The sender requires you to authenticate every signature or initial field

Note: If the sender chose the Rapid Part 11 Signing method, you
must authenticate when you open the documents and finish signing.

1. Review the disclosure and select AGREE AND CONTINUE to use
   Electronic Records and Signatures.
2. You’re prompted to authenticate. To learn more about how the sender might require you
   to authenticate, see the [Verify your identity](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=yca1573855023892&topicId=mbg1573854981921.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about using the different authentication methods to verify your identity for Docusign eSignature.") article.
3. Select
   CONTINUE to begin the signing process.

   Note: Review the document by scrolling or by using the page
   thumbnails in the right-side navigation before signing.
4. Enter any required information and select a signature field.
5. Select a Signing Reason from the list, and select
   SIGN to continue. You cannot continue until the signing reason is
   selected.

   ![A screenshot of the Reason for Signing dialog box with SIGN or CANCEL options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/part11signingreason.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NzAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.1zeO2xOxVSUEpJhrMiaZesZUUboeqSREEuO0cU8BClU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Reason for Signing dialog box with SIGN or CANCEL options.")
6. If you are prompted to authenticate your signature or initials, select
   CONTINUE.

   ![A screenshot of the authentication pop-up window with the CONTINUE or CANCEL options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/yca1573855023892/page/images/signpart11authprompt.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODE4MjQ0NzAsInNoZWFmIjoieWNhMTU3Mzg1NTAyMzg5MiJ9.1zeO2xOxVSUEpJhrMiaZesZUUboeqSREEuO0cU8BClU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the authentication pop-up window with the CONTINUE or CANCEL options.")
7. Enter your Docusign password on a secure Logon page.

   Note: If you're asked to authenticate by logging on to a Docusign account:
   - Create an account if you do not have a Docusign account.
   - The password authentication is only required per one document.
   - You have a limited number of chances to enter your password correctly. If you
     don’t enter the correct password, you are locked out and can’t finish signing.

   Whenever you select a signature or initial field, the process of selecting a reason and
   authenticating might be repeated.
