8. When you add all information and signatures to the documents, review the documents and
   complete the signing by selecting FINISH at the bottom of the
   browser window.

title: Accessibility at Docusign
text:
Learn how Docusign provides high-quality products that are accessible
to all users.

Docusign is committed to providing high-quality products that
are accessible to all users.

As a company, Docusign strives to meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA in the design and
development of all our products. Docusign applications are designed and developed with
accessibility in mind. For example, these applications:

- Ensure that document text and tags can be read by assistive technology tools like screen
  readers.
- Provide easy navigation that allows users to skip past content with proper headings
- Have a common screen reader keyboard shortcut for form figures and tables to improve
  navigation for a reader

For detailed information about additional accessibility features and Docusign’s conformance to government and international requirements,
see the [Docusign Accessibility Hub](https://www.docusign.com/accessibility?postSignIn=true).

title: Sign With itsme
text:
Learn how to sign a document with Docusign and a Belgian digital ID
from itsme.

If you’re eligible for a digital ID from itsme, as described in the [itsme is](https://www.itsme.be/) article, a sender
can require you to sign with one. Signing with this ID creates an extra-secure
Qualified Electronic Signature (QES). You must switch between your browser and the
itsme mobile app.

Note: If you receive multiple envelopes containing
documents to sign with Docusign and itsme, open and complete the
envelopes one by one. Finish verifying your identity and signing before you open the
next envelope.

1. In the notification that you have a document to sign, select REVIEW
   DOCUMENT.
2. Sign the document by following the steps in the [How do I sign a Docusign Document?](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-sign-a-DocuSign-document-Basic-Signing)
   article, and select CONTINUE.
3. You’re prompted to use itsme to finish signing. Select
   NEXT.
4. In your browser, enter your mobile phone number and select
   send to send an action, or signing
   request, to the itsme mobile app.
5. Open the itsme mobile app on your mobile device.

   Note: After you open the
   itsme mobile app, you have three minutes to complete all the steps that
   itsme requires to confirm the request.
6. Open and review the action that displays in the itsme mobile app and select
   Confirm to confirm that you want to sign the
   document.
7. A set of icons displays in your browser. Make sure the icons that display in
   the itsme mobile app are identical to the set in your browser.
8. If the icons match, in the itsme mobile app, select the icon that’s orange in
   your browser.
9. To confirm the request, enter your 5-digit itsme code.

Your browser returns to the document and a message confirm that
you’re finished signing.

title: Access your Docusign Account with a Passkey
heading_path: Access your Docusign Account with a Passkey
text:
Learn how to access your Docusign agreements and account using a passkey.

To securely log on to your Docusign account, you can access your Docusign agreements and
account using a passkey. Passkeys are a safer alternative to passwords, eliminating
the possibility of forgotten or incorrect passwords. With passkeys, you can use any
authentication method on your device, such as fingerprint, face recognition, or a
device PIN. You can also sync passkeys across devices and platforms, including
iCloud, Google Password Manager, or by scanning a QR code.

1. Log on to your account through a mobile device.
2. Select the passkey method.
   - Face recognition using your mobile device's camera, and then scanning a QR code from
     your computer.
   - Finger print using your mobile device
   - Entering a PIN on your mobile device
3. To synch the passcode to all devices, log on to your Docusign account through
   your computer.
4. Select User Phone or Tablet.
5. Enter your passkey.
   - Face recognition: Scan a QR code from your mobile device.
   - Finger print using your mobile device: Use your fingerprint on your
     mobile device.
   - Enter the PIN you created on your mobile device.
6. Once your passkey is accepted, log on to your Docusign Account.

You can now access your Docusign account from any device
with the passkey.
